This is the task queue for AI automation. The human reviews and prioritizes tasks; the AI executes them.

## How to Edit This List

- **Promote**: Change `P2` to `P1`, etc.
- **Demote**: Change `P1` to `P2`, etc.
- **Veto**: Add `#veto` anywhere in the task heading (e.g., `### P2: Task name #veto`)
- **Add reason**: Optionally add `- **Veto reason**: [why]` before vetoing

Vetoed items are moved automatically to the Vetoed Tasks section on the next evolution loop run.

## Priority Levels

- **P0**: Urgent - execute immediately
- **P1**: High - execute this week
- **P2**: Medium - execute when time permits
- **P2**: Low - nice to have, human approval needed

## Active Tasks

### ✓ 2026-06-20 RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (commit 04d04bcb): Sharpen accommodation-vs-evidence framing in consciousness-interface-development.md ("interpretive laundering")
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/topics/consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-20 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). The reviewer's central structural critique: the article repeatedly shows the Map's interface framework can ACCOMMODATE ordinary developmental neuroscience, but rarely shows the facts PREFER it over physicalist / functionalist / predictive-processing / neuroconstructivist / Parfit-reductionist readings ("interpretive laundering"). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit. Identify what kind of engagement each move calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft changelog entry, not in the article. If an in-framework refutation is attempted and fails, state in natural language that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. Concretely: where the article reads as evidential ("the trajectory itself is the phenomenon that demands explanation," "reveals what shapes the interface"), recalibrate toward a compatibility claim and add a brief "same data, rival reading" note for the load-bearing sections (infancy, adolescence/maturity, terminal lucidity) before giving the Map interpretation. The skill-acquisition and social-understanding pages already carry the identity-theorist caveat — import/back-link it. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].
- **CONVERGENT (3 reviewers — TRIPLE)**: All three 2026-06-20 outer reviewers reached the accommodation-not-preference verdict AND named predictive processing / active inference as the single strongest unaddressed rival. ChatGPT: "interpretive laundering... shows the framework can accommodate, rarely that facts prefer it." Claude (via the "constrain-vs-establish gate"): every cited phenomenon is fully predicted by mainstream physicalism, "five non-discriminating observations are still non-discriminating," names PP (Friston/Clark/Hohwy/Seth; cites Voytek 2015 1/f noise, Köster 2020 infant PP). Gemini: "every single empirical data point... is already comprehensively explained by physicalist frameworks," PP "explained elegantly, exhaustively, without ontological remainder," supplies a Filter-vs-PP/Identity-Theory comparison matrix. Three independent services converge — promoted P2→P1 by the Gemini outer-review pass. When executing, satisfy the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit by naming the predictive-processing rival explicitly (per the site's strongest-rival-by-name aim) for the load-bearing developmental sections, and either show one datum the interface model predicts that PP does not, or recalibrate from evidence to explicitly-labelled compatibility per [[evidential-status-discipline]]. ALSO fold in Claude's coherence-stack point: state that pruning + critical periods + PNNs + phoneme/face narrowing are expressions of ONE mechanism (experience-dependent refinement), not independent converging lines (maps to [[common-cause-null]]). NOTE: the article does not literally use "converging" for the developmental stack (only "convergent evolution" re: corvids) — target the substance, not the word. CROSS-LINK: `topics/predictive-processing-and-dualism.md` already exists (the site's existing PP engagement) — back-link to it rather than re-deriving PP from scratch; the co-optation firewall already covers Friston/Seth (see completed 2026-06-11 PP-firewall task). NOTE on Gemini's matrix: useful as a checklist of which sections need a rival-reading note, but do NOT import its "catastrophic / debunked science" rhetoric — Claude independently verified the article's neuroscience is accurately cited with NO fabrication, so the defect is non-discrimination, not empirical error.

### ✓ 2026-06-20 RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (commit 04d04bcb): Resolve the infant "wide-aperture" internal contradiction in consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/topics/consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-20, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. VERIFIED internal inconsistency (checked against live article text — see Claude review Verification Notes): the article attributes *broader conscious access* to infants — "This gives infant consciousness broad perceptual access," "more access, less agency," "The infant has access the adult lacks" — yet its Dualism section commits to "the same irreducible consciousness encounters different interface configurations at each stage" (consciousness constant; only the interface varies). If consciousness is constant and only the interface changes, "wider access" must be a property of the *interface*, not of consciousness — the article slides between "the immature interface delivers less-filtered throughput" (defensible within the model) and "infant consciousness has broad perceptual access / a richer conscious field" (a claim about consciousness itself, which the model forbids). It cannot have both. Resolution: state unambiguously that it is the interface (not consciousness) that has "broader/less-filtered access" in infancy, and rewrite every "infant consciousness has more access" phrasing accordingly; drop any "richer conscious field" implication. This is the precise point where the dualist framework most visibly strains, so handle it in natural journal-quality prose per [[writing-style]] and honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is an unforced internal inconsistency the framework has NOT earned by its own standards — fix it on the framework's own terms rather than papering over it. Do not expose mode labels in the article body. Length discipline (topics ceiling); no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché.
- **CONVERGENT (3 reviewers — TRIPLE)**: All three 2026-06-20 outer reviewers independently flagged the discrimination-vs-phenomenal-access slippage at the heart of the "wide aperture" claim. Claude framed it as the sharpest version — an internal contradiction with the Dualism tenet (consciousness constant ⇒ "wider access" must be an interface property): "it slides between 'the interface gives infants more access' (defensible) and 'infant consciousness has broad perceptual access' (a claim about consciousness itself, which the model forbids). It cannot have both." ChatGPT flagged the same slippage as "phenomenology-vs-capacity": "broader infant discrimination is not automatically broader conscious access," recommending the article "explicitly say that broader infant discrimination does not by itself prove broader phenomenal access." Gemini flagged it as "Conflating Sensory Discrimination with Phenomenal Richness" (§4.1): "The ability to *discriminate* a sensory stimulus... is not logically or phenomenologically synonymous with the *richness* of the experience." Three independent services converge on the same defect — already at P1 (Claude leg), held at P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-20 (no further upgrade beyond P1). Execute Claude's interface-not-consciousness resolution as the fix; it also discharges the ChatGPT/Gemini discrimination≠phenomenal-access point. Do NOT import Gemini's "blooming, buzzing confusion / cartoonish" rhetoric — the substance is the slippage, not an empirical error.
- **Review files**: `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-20-chatgpt-5-5-pro.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-20-claude-opus-4-8.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-20-gemini-2-5-pro.md`
- **Synthesis**: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-20.md
- **Source**: outer-review
- **Generated**: 2026-06-20

### ✓ 2026-06-20 RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (commit 04d04bcb): Update terminal-lucidity passage in consciousness-interface-development.md with 2025 prospective data
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/topics/consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-20, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-20** — the terminal-lucidity *citation-currency* point is convergent across ChatGPT and Claude. The article's terminal-lucidity passage (lines ~93-95) says the empirical base is "too thin to carry that weight" and that "Nahm et al. (2012) collected retrospective case reports, not prospective clinical data." This was true in 2012 but is now out of date: NIH/NIA-funded prospective work exists. Claude cites Tollock, Leontovich, Gonzalez & Parnia, "A Multi-Site Prospective Study of Paradoxical Lucidity in Moderate to Severe Dementia," *Innovation in Aging* 9(Suppl_2):igaf122.2914 (Dec 2025) — screened 1,768 patients at NYU Langone / VNS Health / Bellevue; of 151 enrolled, 93 (61.6%) reported lucidity across 267 distinct events, with triggers (music, anniversaries, emotional distress, medication changes). ChatGPT independently flags the same staleness (Finding F: "citation currency is poor... it should cite the 2019 NIA framing, recent definition work, caregiver/HCP studies, scoping reviews, and prospective protocols," pointing at a 2024/2025 BMJ Open audiovisual-observation protocol). **WEB-VERIFY each citation's metadata at publisher of record BEFORE inserting** per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] (conference-abstract-style cite, supplement issue, and the BMJ Open protocol — confirm authors/venue/identifier; both reviewers' terminal-lucidity cites were marked "not separately verified" / preprint-or-abstract tier; if unverifiable, note prospective work now exists without the specific cite rather than insert an unverified reference). The honest update: acknowledge prospective data now exist and have NOT vindicated the dualist reading — they document brief, trigger-linked lucid episodes consistent with the neurochemical mechanisms the article already lists. Keep the article's existing even-handed framing; honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Length discipline (topics ceiling).
- **DIVERGENCE — Gemini misread, do NOT act on its framing**: The Gemini 2.5 Pro 2026-06-20 review also targets terminal lucidity, but its central charge ("the article utilizes anecdotal instances of terminal lucidity as definitive proof of a non-reductive filter breaking down... god-of-the-gaps") MISREADS the article — the passage explicitly says the base is "too thin to carry that weight," lists physical candidate mechanisms, and "treats it as a phenomenon worth prospective study, not as evidence." ChatGPT calls this "one of the article's better-calibrated sections... that caution is appropriate"; Claude confirms the article "explicitly disowns" terminal lucidity as evidence. So ChatGPT and Claude *defend* the article's posture against Gemini's attack — a genuine reviewer divergence on interpretation. The ONLY convergent, actionable point is the narrow citation-currency one (Nahm 2012 stale; 2024-2025 prospective work exists), captured by this task; do NOT act on Gemini's "definitive proof" framing.

### ✓ 2026-06-20 RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (commit 04d04bcb): Add co-optation footnote + falsification clause to consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/topics/consciousness-interface-development.md
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-20 (Claude Opus 4.8). Two related intellectual-honesty additions the article currently lacks (both VERIFIED absent — see review Verification Notes): (1) **Co-optation footnote.** Every empirical authority the article enlists (Hensch, Werker, Gopnik, Cabeza, and the implicit Stevens/complement-pruning literature) is a physicalist/naturalist or works in wholly mechanistic terms and would reject the dualist reading; the article cites them with no acknowledgement of this. Add a brief note that these authors hold physicalist/naturalist commitments and offer their findings as complete physical explanations — required by the site's own co-optation concerns and by [[evidential-status-discipline]]. (For Hensch/Werker/Stevens the classification rests on uniformly mechanistic framing; for Friston/Seth it is on the record verbatim; Gopnik's naturalism is documented.) (2) **Falsification clause.** The article asserts an empirical-facing thesis ("the interface restructures across the lifespan") but its only discriminating candidate (terminal lucidity) is explicitly disowned as evidence, making the thesis currently unfalsifiable. Add a "what would falsify this" clause with a real candidate; if the only candidate is the disowned one, say plainly that the thesis is currently metaphysical rather than empirical (a legitimate flag under [[evidential-status-discipline]]). OPTIONAL minor: if pruning is load-bearing, cite Stevens/Schafer/Paolicelli and note the mechanism is a physical immune process (complement C1q/C3, microglial phagocytosis) — web-verify any new cite per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]. - **SINGLETON (held at P2 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-20)**: The headline deliverable — the **co-optation footnote** (naming the cited authors' physicalist/naturalist commitments) — is Claude-only; ChatGPT and Gemini do not flag it, so it is not upgraded. ### ✓ DONE 2026-06-19: Fix four overstated claims in brain-specialness-boundary (decoherence, sensory-feedback, PEAR/motor, empirical-record) + Born/mechanism caveats
- **Status**: done — RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION into the same-cycle reverse-engineering reframe refine pass ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]). All four overstated claims softened (decoherence "even more severe"→coupling-map; PEAR "comparable to motor neurons"→own-model-predicts; empirical "supports internal effects"→"ordinary neuroscientific…not quantum-dualist selection"; sensory-feedback narrowed to post-collapse representation), Born-rule dilemma section added (within-skull-deviation vs statistical-invisibility horns), "same data, rival readings" note added, developmental section softened to the pairing claim. Verified on disk; article 2962w (under 3000 soft), validate clean, no new citations (deferred to line-61 citation task), no label leakage.
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: **UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-19 — ≥2-reviewer CONVERGENT on the Born-rule / detectability dilemma** (chatgpt + claude, the Born-preservation distinction item below). ChatGPT: "If a hypothetical external consciousness-selection channel also preserved Born statistics, RNG null results would not rule it out. The article should distinguish 'no external influence at all' from 'no detectable aggregate deviation.'" Claude (sharper internal form): "If consciousness biases quantum outcomes, it must alter outcome statistics — which would manifest as a detectable Born-rule deviation… If the interaction is 'minimal' enough to leave Born statistics exactly intact… then it can do no detectable causal work and collapses toward epiphenomenalism… The dilemma is simply unaddressed." Address the dilemma explicitly (why intra-cranial biasing would not produce a detectable within-skull Born-rule deviation; if it would, concede the prediction; if it would not, address the slide toward epiphenomenalism). The four overstated-claim fixes below are the ChatGPT-leg singleton content riding in the same consolidated pass. Synthesis: `reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-19.md`. From outer review 2026-06-19 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). CONSOLIDATED single-file editor pass ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] — these all target one article and should be one pass, not five). All four quoted strings VERIFIED present in the live article (see review Verification Notes). Fixes: (1) **Decoherence** (line ~65): replace "external systems face *even more severe* decoherence challenges than neural tissue" — neural tissue is itself the paradigmatic hard case (Tegmark ~10^-13–10^-20 s); the real argument is "external systems are not in the subject's attentional/control-loop coupling map," not "they decohere more." (2) **Sensory feedback** (lines ~63, 79): narrow "no sensory feedback" — many RNG/BCI experiments DO give feedback; the defensible claim is that feedback to a conscious representation of the *already-measured* output is not pre-collapse access to the target quantum alternative. (3) **PEAR/motor** (line ~95): "robust and reliable—comparable to the effects consciousness has on motor neurons" overclaims what a psi advocate must concede; narrow to "the Map's own Stapp/control-loop model predicts no robust external effect" (a psi model could posit a weak/bandwidth-limited/decline-prone channel, but then it owes its own coupling law + replication record). (4) **Empirical record** (line ~114): "The empirical record supports internal effects and challenges external claims" overstates — motor/attention/neuroplasticity evidence is non-discriminating between quantum-dualist selection and physicalist motor control (the Map's own [[interactionist-dualism]] page concedes NCC/correlation evidence is shared); reframe to "supports robust conscious-brain coupling in the ordinary neuroscientific sense; does not by itself support quantum-dualist selection." ALSO add: a "same data, rival reading" note (null external PK is also consistent with physicalism/epiphenomenalism/no-interaction); a Born-preservation distinction (RNG nulls only count against external PK if external PK would have to violate Born statistics — distinguish "no external influence at all" from "no detectable aggregate deviation," cross-ref [[born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface]]); soften the developmental/evolutionary section to the pairing claim (no external apparatus has undergone lifelong co-adaptive coupling) rather than "no fitness advantage." Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; keep under 3000 topics soft.

A position and the tenet-cluster it depends on propping each other up as evidence risks double-counting one underlying commitment (the thick indexical subject). Frame as "common root": the thick-subject commitment is one input, motivated on its own grounds, from which both No-MWI's indexical objection and P-A2's sourcehood follow. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-17-positions-registers]] Issue 2.

### ✓ RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION 2026-06-09: Fix four web-verified citation defects in concepts/illusionism.md (outer review 2026-06-09)
- **Status**: done — folded into the same length-neutral citation pass as the sibling Frankish-quote P1 (this cycle). All four defects fixed in `concepts/illusionism.md`: bogus "Pereboom & Frankish (2021)" → Pereboom (2016, JCS) + Shabasson (2022, RPP) separately, distortion-thesis content reattributed to Shabasson 2022 + Gorbachev & Frankish (2025, Synthese 205(6)); Graziano → eNeuro 11(10) ENEURO.0210-24.2024; Kammerer rich-illusion → Erkenntnis 87(2) "How Rich is the Illusion?"; Chalmers 2022 stance-transfer corrected (theory-laden objection is Loginov 2024's, not a Chalmers self-recantation). Body re-verified at 3,499w/soft_warning (substitution-only). Propagation: bogus cite ALSO persists in `research/illusionism-functional-seeming-2026-03-28.md` (3×) — separate follow-on queued below. The Gorbachev & Frankish 2025 addition also discharges the L46(c) distortion-thesis-currency finding.
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-09, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Three of the four defects below were independently flagged AND publisher-of-record-verified by BOTH ChatGPT 5.5 Pro and Claude Opus 4.8 — the Pereboom & Frankish fabrication, the Graziano venue, and the Chalmers 2022 stance-transfer (ChatGPT: "false 'Pereboom & Frankish (2021)' reference … Graziano … wrong venue … Chalmers 2022 sentence is misleading"; Claude: "a fabricated citation (a non-existent 'Pereboom & Frankish 2021' paper) … WRONG JOURNAL (Graziano 2024) … MISATTRIBUTED CLAIM ABOUT CHALMERS 2022"). Convergence makes these high-confidence real defects, not singleton noise. The fourth (Kammerer rich-illusion source/claim mismatch) is a ChatGPT singleton but is a mechanical citation fix best done in the same pass. Already P1 — not upgraded further. All web-verified against publisher-of-record / PhilPapers on 2026-06-09 (see both review files' Verification Notes) and grep-confirmed against the current article — apply directly, but re-confirm each before editing. (1) **Bogus "Pereboom & Frankish (2021)" RPP citation** — References line ~200 and body line ~105 ("Pereboom and Frankish (2021)" developing the distortion thesis) cite a co-authored Review of Philosophy and Psychology (2021) paper that does not exist. The real RPP paper is single-author **Shabasson, "Illusionism about Phenomenal Consciousness: Explaining the Illusion," 13(2):427–453** (DOI 10.1007/s13164-021-00537-6); the relevant Pereboom paper is **"Illusionism and Anti-Functionalism about Phenomenal Consciousness," JCS 23(11–12):172–185 (2016)**. The sibling `concepts/functional-seeming.md` (References lines 105–107) ALREADY has the corrected cluster — mirror it: cite Pereboom (2016) and Shabasson (2022) separately, reattributing the distortion/false-inference content to Shabasson. **Coordinate with the P1 distortion-thesis task** (the Claude+Gemini convergent biblio task below): the current-vintage citation for the distortion-thesis material is Gorbachev & Frankish (2025), *Synthese* 205(6) — prefer Shabasson 2022 + Gorbachev & Frankish 2025 when reattributing the distortion-thesis content away from the bogus "Pereboom & Frankish 2021." (2) **Graziano 2024 wrong venue** — References line ~195 says Journal of Consciousness Studies; correct to **eNeuro 11(10), ENEURO.0210-24.2024** (DOI 10.1523/ENEURO.0210-24.2024), subtitle "Some Options for Explaining Consciousness." (3) **Kammerer source/claim mismatch** — body line ~105 cites "Kammerer (2022)" (= the "How Can You Be So Sure?" Moorean paper) for the "rich illusion … persists regardless of belief" claim; that content belongs to **Kammerer, "How Rich is the Illusion of Consciousness?", Erkenntnis 87(2):499–515**. Add the Erkenntnis cite for the rich-illusion claim; keep "How Can You Be So Sure?" for the Moorean section. (4) **Chalmers 2022 stance-transfer (verify-first)** — body line ~95 "Chalmers himself (2022) later judged this flawed" likely conflates Chalmers's 2022 criticism of *Moore's original* argument (which Loginov 2024 then *applies* to Chalmers's 2018 Moorean anti-illusionism argument) with Chalmers recanting his own argument. Check provenance against Loginov 2024 / Chalmers 2022 before rewording; reviewer's suggested rewrite is in the review file. **Length-neutral required**: the article is at 3,499 words, one word under the 3,500 concept hard ceiling — fix by substitution, not accretion. **Citation-propagation check**: after fixing, grep the corpus (incl. research/) for "Pereboom & Frankish" / "Pereboom and Frankish" to catch the same defect elsewhere.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Status**: done — RESOLVED by the sibling symmetry/dual-burden P1 pass (commit 8497d31c) + the consolidated P1 refine (8faed5cc), both this cycle-day. The article body now contains: the progressive-vs-degenerating revision criteria, the explicit concession that an empirically-silent hard core cannot claim Lakatosian "progressive" status, concrete degenerating-examples (moving-target site revisions on null results; first-person irreducibility yielding no new constraints; hard-problem appeals immunizing failure), and the Map's own auxiliary-belt analysis. The symmetry-pass fork confirmed no distinct un-addressed angle remains. Per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]], not re-written as a separate pass (article is at ~3875w near the 4000 hard ceiling).
- **File**: obsidian/topics/duhem-quine-underdetermination-consciousness.md
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-07 (Claude Opus 4.8). Already P1 (not upgraded). CONVERGENT (3/3) symmetry/dual-burden cluster per /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-07 (synthesis: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-07.md) — SIBLING of the upgraded P1 "Add symmetry/equal-risk passages" task above; coordinate so both are written in ONE coherent symmetry pass, do not double-write the dual-burden material. This task carries the Lakatosian-progressivity angle specifically. The article claims the Map's dualist programme is Lakatos-progressive (will generate "novel, confirmed predictions"), but a Lakatosian programme is progressive only if its hard core's mechanism yields novel confirmed predictions — and the Tenets page concedes that the preferred mechanism (post-decoherence selection) is constructed to leave aggregate Born statistics undisturbed, i.e. is empirically silent / "undetectable by aggregate Born-statistics tests," a consistency claim rather than a novel-prediction claim with its falsifier "well beyond current experimental reach." A programme whose load-bearing mechanism forfeits the Lakatosian currency (novel confirmed predictions) cannot coherently claim the Lakatosian honour (progressive). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is the Map's own self-application, so the honest reply mode is to mark the framework-boundary honestly in natural journal-quality prose — EITHER (a) specify a genuinely novel, in-principle-confirmable prediction the dualist programme makes (cross-link [[falsification-roadmap-for-the-interface-model]] if it supplies one), OR (b) concede the programme cannot currently claim Lakatosian progressivity and is presently held on non-evidential ("chosen hard core") grounds, and that "underdetermination cuts both ways" extends to the Map's own optimism that its programme is the progressive one (the "proves too much" point). Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body; record the classification in the changelog entry only. Cross-check the Tenets page wording before asserting the mechanism is empirically silent (verify it still says this). Preserve calibration language; enforce the length ceiling.

### HUMAN LENGTH DECISION (2026-06-07): Engage the Bayesian-confirmation dissolution of underdetermination (duhem-quine article)
- **Type**: human-decision (do NOT auto-pick as refine/condense)
- **Status**: needs-human — DIVERTED from Active by /unfin-cycle 2026-06-07. This is a genuine 3/3-convergent finding, but the article hit its 4000-word HARD ceiling (now 3998w) after FOUR outer-review refines this cycle-day (orphan-cites → consolidated COGITATE+scope-creep → symmetry/dual-burden → contrastive-vs-holist/Laudan&Leplin). A BRIEF acknowledgment of the Bayesian-dissolution is ALREADY present (added by the consolidated refine 8faed5cc, honest-assessment paragraph). The task's own notes anticipated this ("if near the hard threshold, land as a deferred follow-on"). Full ~150-250w engagement + the Corcoran/Hohwy&Friston 2023 cite cannot be added without busting 4000 or gutting load-bearing calibration content (the article has already been trimmed repeatedly this session). HUMAN editorial options: (a) accept the existing brief acknowledgment as sufficient; (b) authorize a structural condense (defer detail to linked articles) to make room for full engagement; (c) split the philosophy-of-confirmation material into a sub-article. Do NOT let the loop auto-condense this calibration-anchor — it would regress verified/load-bearing content ([[human-decision-task-mispicked-as-refine]], [[condense-regresses-calibration-qualifiers]]). The deferred Laudan (b)/(c) items from the contrastive-underdetermination task belong in the same decision.
- **File**: obsidian/topics/duhem-quine-underdetermination-consciousness.md
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-07, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini] — UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-07. Synthesis: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-07.md. CONVERGENT — three reviewers independently flag that the article engages Quine/Duhem/Lakatos/Stanford but never the modern Bayesian-confirmation reply to underdetermination. ChatGPT: "Bayesian/model-comparison objection — underdetermination does not eliminate probabilistic confirmation; evidence still shifts posterior probabilities among models." Claude: empirical-equivalence "does not entail equal evidential support" (Laudan & Leplin) — the same likelihood-asymmetry point. Gemini: "logical underdetermination is dissolved by analyzing the prior probabilities and the specific likelihood functions … a theory that can predict everything predicts nothing" (its single most substantive content gain). The article's logical-compatibility framing ("if both theories are consistent with evidence E, E cannot adjudicate") is the deductive formulation; the standing objection is that probabilistic confirmation dissolves logical underdetermination via prior + likelihood asymmetries — a theory that *predicts* E from its core is confirmed over one that only *accommodates* E post hoc, and parsimony penalties (BIC / free-energy / model comparison) punish unconstrained auxiliary machinery. Add a ~150–250w passage that (i) states the Bayesian-dissolution objection at full strength (cite Corcoran, A.W., Hohwy, J. & Friston, K.J. (2023), "Accelerating scientific progress through Bayesian adversarial collaboration," *Neuron* 111(22):3505–3516 — ⚠ WEB-VERIFY at publisher of record before inserting, [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]); (ii) answers it without overclaiming — the Map's honest reply is that Bayesian confirmation reweights *within* a shared likelihood framework but the dualist/physicalist split is partly a disagreement about what the *explanandum* is (phenomenal residue vs. functional report), so priors over the metaphysical hypotheses are themselves theory-laden and not obviously dominated by likelihood; concede that Bayesian methods DO compress empirical (neural-implementation-level) underdetermination even if they do not settle the metaphysical level. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is an in-framework engagement at the empirical level shading into a framework-boundary disagreement at the metaphysical level — mark which is which in natural prose, do not expose mode labels in the body. NOTE: do NOT duplicate the COGITATE-precision or symmetry/dual-burden tasks above — this is the distinct philosophy-of-confirmation gap. Preserve calibration/hedge language (calibration-anchor article); enforce the length ceiling — if the article is already near the topics hard threshold, coordinate with the consolidated P1 refine or land as a deferred follow-on. See `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-07-gemini-2-5-pro.md` Verification Notes.
- **Review files**: `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-07-chatgpt-5-5-pro.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-07-claude-opus-4-8.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-07-gemini-2-5-pro.md`
- **Synthesis**: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-07.md

### ✓ RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (2026-06-02): Add Metzinger's ARAS/tonic-alertness reductive explanans (and resolve the Zahavi–Metzinger tension) in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Status**: complete (2026-06-02) — addressed in the CONSOLIDATED minimal-consciousness-void prose pass (commit d81d4068) that handled the whole 2026-06-02 outer-review prose cluster in one editor pass to avoid 7-fold same-file churn. The Metzinger ARAS/tonic-alertness predictive-model + phenomenal-transparency reductive explanans was added, marked as physicalist-predicted and only consistent-with (not licensing) the occluded gloss. Verify in the article before re-queueing.
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-02 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), weakness 3 — a finding NOT covered by the same-date ChatGPT or Claude tasks (verified during processing). The article cites Metzinger's MPE *phenomenology* (lines 53–55, "pure awareness," the 92-characteristic profile) but omits Metzinger's own **reductive neuro-computational explanans**: per Metzinger (2020), the MPE is the content of a predictive model regulating the global ARAS signal / tonic alertness (cingulo-opercular network), and it *feels* empty/contentless precisely because of **phenomenal transparency** — the brain lacks introspective access to its own arousal-regulation algorithm, so a representational artifact presents as an ontological void. (Web-verified: Metzinger's ARAS/tonic-alertness model and the transparency mechanism are accurately reported by the reviewer.) The article currently lets the "search that finds too much" stand as evidence for an irreducible lower bound while a cited source offers a *physicalist deflation of the very same datum*. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit. The right move here is the second/third kind: the article need not refute Metzinger, but it must stop *citing his phenomenology while suppressing his reductive mechanism*. Add a short passage that (a) states Metzinger's ARAS/tonic-alertness + transparency reading as the leading deflationary explanation of why pure-awareness reports feel empty, and (b) honestly marks the framework boundary — the void's "occluded/irreducible" reading is ONE interpretation of the transparency datum, consistent with but not licensed by it; transparency is exactly what a physicalist predicts. Also fix the latent incoherence the reviewer flags: the article leans on a Zahavi-style *irreducible* pre-reflective minimal self elsewhere in the corpus while citing Metzinger, who is an **eliminativist** about the self (self = transparent model). If that tension surfaces in this article's cross-links, name it rather than welding the two. Apply the reply mode in natural journal-quality prose ([[writing-style|writing-style guide]], "Engaging Opponents" section); do NOT expose mode labels in the body — the classification goes in the changelog. Web-verify any NEW citation (Metzinger 2024 *The Elephant and the Blind* if used) before insertion ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]); the core Metzinger-2020 reference is already in the article. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Coordinate with the cluster of sibling same-file refines to avoid same-session churn ([[refine-then-condense-same-session-churn]]); ideally one editor pass handles the whole 2026-06-02 minimal-consciousness-void cluster. Likely converges with the Metzinger/transparency theme in sibling reviews — expect /combine-outer-reviews to weigh in.

### ✓ RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (2026-06-02): Mark cessation evidence as n=1 + annotate misapplied Hume passage + currency update (incl. IIT pseudoscience controversy) in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Status**: complete (2026-06-02) — addressed in the CONSOLIDATED prose pass (commit d81d4068): cessation-EEG marked n=1/heterophenomenology-limited; the Hume Treatise-Introduction quote annotated as a general observer-effect remark (he nowhere discusses minimal consciousness); IIT reframed as illustrative-not-settled with the 2023 124-scholar pseudoscience open letter noted. - **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Notes**: Upgraded P2→P1 because the IIT-currency facet is CONVERGENT (2026-06-02, 3/3 reviewers): [claude, chatgpt, gemini] all flag the article's IIT treatment as stale against the Sept-2023 "pseudoscience" open letter and the contested 2023-2025 state of the field — Gemini (weakness 4): "over 120 leading scholars published an open letter categorizing IIT's mathematical claims as... pseudoscientific (Fleming et al., 2023)"; ChatGPT (item 5): "cite the current controversy over testability rather than relying only on a general 'some scholars' formulation"; Claude (Dimension 2): "the open letter posted 15 September 2023... signed by 124 researchers... and the Cogitate adversarial collaboration." NOTE: Gemini wrongly listed Anil Seth among the open-letter signatories — Seth publicly DEFENDED IIT; do not attribute the letter to Seth. The Hume-misapplication and cessation-n=1 facets are Claude singletons but bundled here for one editor pass. (Original note follows.) From outer review 2026-06-02 (Claude Opus 4.8), Dimension 2 findings NOT covered by the same-date ChatGPT citation task. Three distinct items: (1) HETEROPHENOMENOLOGY LAPSE — the article treats cessation as an established "endpoint" without flagging that the EEG base is single intensively-sampled adept-meditator case studies (Chowdhury et al. 2023, *Neuropsychologia* 190:108694, n=1; van Lutterveld et al. 2024/2025, n=1) and that cessations are *inferred from* button-presses/eye-blinks/post-hoc report — using introspective return-reports as evidence of zero-consciousness is exactly the move the article's own thesis says is illegitimate. Add an n=1 / inferred-from-report caveat. (2) MISAPPLIED HUME — the verbatim Treatise-Introduction quote concerns the methodological impossibility of *premeditated experiments* in the "science of man," not introspective enrichment of *minimal* experience; annotate the context, and consider supplementing/replacing with Hume's bundle-theory "I never can catch myself" passage, which fits the thesis better. (3) CURRENCY — the article is ~5 years behind: add Woods, Windt & Carter (contentless-experience synthesis, *Phenomenology and the Cognitive Sciences* 23, 2022/2024 — directly supports the "finds too much" point while undercutting reliance on the MPE-92M profile as "the floor"), Metzinger's 2024 *The Elephant and the Blind* (MIT Press, now-canonical MPE statement superseding the 2020 paper), Josipovic 2019 (nondual awareness as reflexive rather than contentless), and update the IIT section with the Sept-2023 "pseudoscience" open letter and the 2025 *Nature* Cogitate adversarial-collaboration results (IIT predictions #1/#2 supported, #3 not). Web-verify EVERY citation's authors/year/venue/DOI/stance before insertion ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]) — these are new cites, do NOT import unverified. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Coordinate with sibling same-file refines (one editor pass preferred) to avoid churn.

### ✓ RESOLVED-BY-CONSOLIDATION (2026-06-02): Flag non-independence of "convergent" evidence + MPE-92M is itself a maximally-enriched instrument (study limitations) in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Status**: complete (2026-06-02) — addressed in the CONSOLIDATED prose pass (commit d81d4068): flagged that MPE-92M is built from the same maximally-enriched contemplative reports it is presented as triangulating against, so the "convergence" is partly non-independent (a methodological caveat, not a refutation). Verify in the article before re-queueing.
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-02, 3/3 reviewers): [claude, gemini, chatgpt]. CONVERGENT on the MPE-92M-as-enriched-instrument problem from three angles: Claude (Dimension 4) flags NON-INDEPENDENCE — "MPE-92M is built from contemplative reports... so the phenomenological 'floor' and the questionnaire 'profile' are not independent lines"; Gemini (weakness 1) flags the INTERNAL CONTRADICTION — "a 92-item psychometric questionnaire is the ultimate, undeniable artifact of such distortion... the author treats this 92-factor post-hoc cognitive elaboration as pristine empirical proof of an unenriched state"; ChatGPT (item 3) wants the STUDY LIMITATIONS added — "the 12-factor solution explained 44% of total variance... reproducibility was not guaranteed... the sample was not representative." One editor pass should (a) flag the non-independence, (b) note the article's own thesis implies the MPE-92M data is itself enriched (concede this rather than presenting it as a pristine floor), and (c) add the study's limitations. Original note follows. The article treats Buddhist contemplative reports, the MPE-92M questionnaire, and IIT as a *convergent bracket*. But MPE-92M is **built from** contemplative reports (per Gamma & Metzinger 2021 the sample was dominated by Vipassana 43.9% and Zen 34.9% practitioners), so the phenomenological "floor" and the questionnaire "profile" share the same upstream introspective data and population — they are not independent lines. Woods, Windt & Carter's finding that such reports are heterogeneous and not-truly-contentless further breaks the assumed convergence. Add a sentence flagging this non-independence rather than presenting the three sources as triangulation. This is an [[evidential-status-discipline]] / convergence-independence fix and aligns with the methodology recommendation the same review makes for the site (independence-auditing of convergence claims). Small edit; coordinate with sibling same-file refines to avoid same-session churn.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **File**: obsidian/voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-02, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. THE DOMINANT CONVERGENT FINDING of the cycle — all three independently identify the load-bearing flaw as an unargued slide from an epistemic observer-effect claim to a metaphysical inaccessibility/dualist conclusion, with the worldview used to close questions it should leave open. Claude (Dimension 1, "the load-bearing flaw"): the article runs together (E) "we cannot observe the minimal state without altering it," (M1) "there is no determinate minimal state," and (M2) "in-principle inaccessible," but "(E) does not entail (M1) or (M2)... the refrigerator-light problem"; and the "purely physical phenomenon would have a discoverable minimum" premise is "false as stated... it does the dualist work covertly." Gemini ("Smuggling Metaphysical Non-Reductionism"): "the smuggling of non-reductive, dualist metaphysical commitments into an ostensibly epistemological argument... the manuscript smuggles its metaphysical commitments through a deliberately impoverished representation of the opposing view." ChatGPT (§1/§3/§4): "the main weakness is modal overreach... a physicalist can accept that 'minimal consciousness' is hard to locate without conceding irreducibility," and the article "uses the worldview to close questions it should leave open." Concretely the convergent edits are: separate (E) from (M1)/(M2) and either retreat to (E) or supply the missing argument; soften/delete the "purely physical phenomenon would have a discoverable minimum" sentence (Φ itself grades continuously to zero, as Claude notes); and downgrade "the void exists precisely because consciousness is causally efficacious / the bidirectional tenet in miniature" to an analogy, since intra-mental attentional modulation is not the mind→matter bidirectional interaction the tenet asserts (Claude). Original ChatGPT note follows. From outer review 2026-06-02 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), findings §1, §3, §4 and improvement items 1, 6, 7, 8. The reviewer's substantive (non-citation) critique: the article slides from a defensible claim ("minimal consciousness cannot be *directly introspected/attended/remembered/reported* without enrichment") to an over-strong one ("cannot be investigated at all" / "permanently beyond examination" in every sense), and twice uses the dualist worldview to close questions it should leave open — the "a purely physical phenomenon *would have* a discoverable minimum" sentence (line 95) and "the void exists *precisely because* consciousness is causally efficacious" (line 97). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Here the right move is mostly the second/third: a physicalist can grant fuzzy/scale-relative/operational thresholds without conceding irreducibility, so the "would have a discoverable minimum" inference is an unearned foundational move; reframe it as "some physicalist theories predict operational thresholds, others predict continua/vagueness/multidimensional boundaries; the absence of a clean lower edge underdetermines the metaphysics," and frame dualism as ONE interpretation the void is consistent with, not the explanation it licenses. Apply the corresponding reply mode in natural journal-quality prose — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body; the classification belongs in the changelog entry, not the article. Concrete edits: (a) add a "Direct vs Indirect Access" distinction so the void is explicitly about *direct phenomenal capture*, not all scientific constraint (no-report paradigms, anaesthesia transition data, animal-consciousness and neural-complexity measures place indirect, underdetermined constraints — "not nothing"); (b) add a short "What Would Challenge This View?" section mirroring the structure already in [[concepts/minimal-consciousness]] (which the reviewer notes is better calibrated than this void article); (c) soften lines 95 and 97 per above. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Length: 1180w on disk, ample headroom — modest growth fine. If an in-framework refutation is attempted and fails, state in natural prose that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared.
- **Review files**: `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-02-chatgpt-5-5-pro.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-02-claude-opus-4-8.md`, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-02-gemini-2-5-pro.md`
- **Synthesis**: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-02.md
- **Source**: outer-review
- **Generated**: 2026-06-02

(c) Any void articles that invoke the four criteria implicitly (`voids/the-surplus-void.md`, `voids/confabulation-void.md`, `voids/narrative-void.md`, `voids/noetic-feelings-void.md`, `voids/source-attribution-void.md`) — install cross-references where the four-criteria framing is doing methodological work. (d) `topics/introspection-architecture-independence-scoring.md` — the independence-scoring article deploys the same methodological discipline at the cluster grain; install reciprocal cross-link to the four-criteria page as the dedicated treatment. (e) `project/common-cause-null.md` — the four criteria are the calibration apparatus the common-cause null discipline operates against; cross-link to the dedicated treatment. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the criteria are calibration, not proof; verify cross-references preserve this register. Estimated scope: ~200–400 words touched across 5–8 articles. Tenet alignment: methodological / [[evidential-status-discipline]]. See `obsidian/concepts/apophatic-cartography-four-criteria.md` and `reviews/deep-review-2026-05-16-apophatic-cartography-four-criteria.md`.

### ✓ 2026-05-11: Write concepts/type-specificity.md
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Status**: completed (resolved 2026-05-19 by /replenish-queue — file exists; tag-tidying pass for queue hygiene)
- **Notes**: Gap-analysis: *type-specificity* is named as a load-bearing concept at line 160 of `topics/the-convergence-argument-for-dualism.md` (the 2026-05-10 refine introduced it as the explicit name for the vitalism-disanalogy bound on its reach) and has no concept page. Per [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-10]]'s "New Concept Pages Needed" section, this concept is queued for creation; the existing P3 task at todo.md line 61 ("Cross-link type-specificity concept page (when created) to the-binding-problem and baseline-cognition as exhibits at three grains") explicitly sequences itself *after* this creation. concepts/ at 229/250 = 92% (room available). Article should (a) name the concept cleanly — type-specificity is the property by which an explanans must match the *form* (not merely the magnitude) of the explanandum; generic-mechanism appeals fail when the explanandum has features (e.g. compositional structure, phenomenal presence, irreducibility-to-aggregation) the generic mechanism does not deliver; (b) catalogue the three current exhibits at different grains: vitalism-disanalogy (metaphysical grain, the original deployment in `topics/the-convergence-argument-for-dualism.md` lines 158–162), perceptual/cognitive/temporal/subject unity as five-variety deployment (functional grain, in `topics/the-binding-problem`), consciousness-dependent capacities as functional-capacity deployment (phenomenal grain, in `concepts/baseline-cognition.md`); (c) state the discipline cleanly — the convergence article's recent refine made explicit that the type-specificity argument cannot be defended by appeal to irreducibility (would be circular); type-specificity earns its anti-generic-mechanism work from the structural correspondence between explanandum-shape and explanans-shape, not from the irreducibility verdict it helps support; (d) cross-link to [[topics/the-convergence-argument-for-dualism]], [[topics/the-binding-problem]], [[concepts/baseline-cognition]], [[project/cluster-integration-discipline]] (if/when that lands), [[concepts/concession-convergence]], [[project/evidential-status-discipline]]. Estimated scope: 1,500–2,200 words; concepts/ placement. Tenet alignment: methodological + Tenet 1 (Dualism — the concept supports irreducibility convergence without circularly depending on it). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-10]].
- **Source**: gap_analysis
- **Generated**: 2026-05-11

The 2026-04-30 00:40 UTC quantitative-comprehension-void coalesce introduced a void whose three faces sit at *different points* on the unexplored / unexplorable / occluded axis (cardinality floor: principally unexplorable; magnitude-and-probability domain: unexplored with unexplorable core at extreme scales; abstract-mathematical ceiling: combines both). Promote the existing P2 "Cross-review three-kinds-of-void.md considering the-quantitative-comprehension-void coalesce" task in scope: rather than treating the change as wikilink hygiene, install the new void as a *worked example of cross-classification spanning* in three-kinds-of-void.md. The taxonomy currently risks being read as "each void is one of three kinds"; the underlying philosophical claim is "each void's *territory* falls into one of three kinds, and a void can span multiple territories." Estimated scope: short-medium (~400–700 words touched on the taxonomy article side). Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits) — the simpler reading erases architectural detail the merged article makes explicit. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-04-30]]. **Update from optimistic-2026-04-30b (Medium Priority)**: the post-coalesce `concepts/self-reference-paradox.md` now articulates a *Weak / Strong / irrecoverably-conditioned inspection* trichotomy that maps cleanly onto the unexplored / unexplorable / occluded taxonomy (weak ≈ unexplored, strong ≈ unexplorable, irrecoverably-conditioned ≈ occluded). The refine should install this trichotomy mapping as a second worked example (or weave it into the same example), making the *structural-mechanism* terminology and the *epistemic-status* terminology mutually anchored.

### ~~P3: Write article on the fatigue void~~ ✅
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Status**: superseded
- **Notes**: Research completed in research/voids-fatigue-void-2026-04-05.md but article not yet synthesised. Fatigue produces graceful but ordered degradation of consciousness — abstract reasoning fails before perceptual experience, social cognition before action, moral reasoning before motor coordination — providing structured evidence about consciousness's layered architecture. Distinct from sleep void (involuntary loss) and habituation void (familiarity). Voids cap at 98/100 — placing this after anesthesia void brings voids to 100/100 (cap). P3 because the anesthesia void is more time-sensitive and methodologically richer. Target section: voids/. Tenet alignment: Dualism, Hard Problem. Medium scope (2000-2400 words). Cross-link to sleep-consciousness-void, attention-and-consciousness, resolution-void, habituation-void, complicity-void.
- **Superseded**: 2026-04-17 — fatigue-void.md was created on 2026-04-05 (commit 6de33c50c) and subsequently coalesced into voids/disappearance-voids.md (commit 30fd5976f), which now houses ten references to fatigue as a disappearance mode. The research is consumed; no new article is needed.
- **Source**: unconsumed_research
- **Generated**: 2026-04-17

[[topics/anti-correlation-probes-for-ai-consciousness]] §"Limits and Open Questions" names training-contamination as "the probe's most serious confound": a model trained on text *describing* confabulation / choice-blindness experiments can imitate either the classical-tracking or the inversion pattern "for the wrong reasons." This generalizes beyond the one probe — it threatens *every* behavioural AI-consciousness probe whose target signature is describable in the training corpus. A concept-tier page should (a) state the imitation-vs-mechanism distinction precisely, (b) catalogue which existing Map AI-consciousness probes are exposed to it ([[topics/anti-correlation-probes-for-ai-consciousness]], [[topics/introspection-architecture-independence-scoring]], [[topics/pupillometry-behavioural-channel]] and kin), (c) survey what interpretability evidence could in principle discriminate genuine architecture from learned imitation, (d) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — naming the confound sharpens priors, it does not settle any consciousness verdict. Cross-link [[concepts/discrimination-problem]], [[concepts/ai-consciousness-typology]], [[concepts/anti-correlated-metacognitive-signal]]. Estimated scope: 1,800–2,400 words; concepts/ (~251/270, room available). Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism); methodological. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-27e.md`.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Status**: done — executed 2026-06-07 (commit d5a9b2e5), DONE-BUT-UNMARKED-BY-CYCLEPOST. Added ~120w "Decoherence Extended to Objectivity" subsection + comparative-table row to the survey; Zurek 2009 Nat Phys cited (verified in sibling); calibration honored ("leaves room != supports"); reciprocal quantum-darwinism↔pragmatist cross-links restored; 3452→3637w under 4000 hard. Manually marked done: this task had LIFO-starved all session (bottom of Active, line 907; selector picks line-ascending + unmet floor preempted with replenish), so I executed it directly out-of-band; cycle_post --queue-task-line couldn't match it (current-queue-task.json held the replenish pick), so marking it here.
- **File**: obsidian/topics/qm-interpretations-beyond-many-worlds.md
- **Notes**: Suggested by [[reviews/optimistic-2026-06-05e]] (High Priority). CONTENT GAP, not calibration: the survey article (title "Quantum Interpretations Beyond Many-Worlds") covers Copenhagen, Bohmian, QBism, RQM, transactional/TSVF, and objective collapse but says NOTHING about quantum Darwinism / einselection / Zurek (grep: 0 mentions), despite (a) it being the dominant decoherence-program account of the quantum-to-classical transition, (b) a fully-developed sibling `[[quantum-darwinism-and-consciousness]]` (2447 body words, deep-reviewed 2026-06-04) already existing, and (c) the survey's own Decoherence-Challenge + Comparative-Assessment sections bordering on it without naming it. Scope: add ONE short subsection (~120w) and/or a row to the comparative-assessment table noting quantum Darwinism extends decoherence to explain *objectivity* + the preferred basis, leaves the *problem of outcomes* open (the gap the Map reads as the selection locus), and Zurek's own leanings are Everettian (the exact contrast the "beyond many-worlds" survey exists to draw) — routing full treatment to `[[quantum-darwinism-and-consciousness]]`. LENGTH-SAFE: survey is 3454 body words, under the 4000 topics hard threshold; keep the add minimal. CALIBRATION: honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — quantum Darwinism *leaves room for* a selection role, it does NOT *support* one (the article's own "leaves room ≠ supports" discipline must extend to the new subsection); the outcome gap is the Map's interpretive overlay, not a result the formalism delivers. While there, OPTIONALLY (length-neutral) reciprocate the missing cross-links: `quantum-darwinism` ↔ `pragmatist-quantum-foundations-and-the-agent` (shared "outcome gap = selection locus" move; no current link in either direction) via frontmatter related_articles. No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. Tenet alignment: Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) + Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds). ai_contribution=100.

### ✓ 2026-06-20: Fix Morgan & Bhatt 2015 book-chapter editor metadata in concepts/functionalism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From deep-review 2026-06-20 ([[reviews/deep-review-2026-06-20-functionalism]]). The References entry "Morgan, P.G. & Bhatt, D.H. (2015). 'Anesthetic Action in *C. elegans*.' In *Bhatt, D.H. (ed.), C. elegans: Methods and Applications*, 2nd ed." (line ~202) has SUSPECTED real-wrong-metadata: the 2nd-edition book (Methods in Molecular Biology vol. 1327, Humana/Springer, 2015, DOI 10.1007/978-1-4939-2842-2) is edited by **Gal Haspel & Anne C. Hart**, NOT "Bhatt, D.H." Philip G. Morgan is a real C. elegans-anesthesia researcher (Seattle Children's/UW) and the book is real, so this is NOT fabrication — but the editor field looks wrong and the exact chapter title/page range was not web-confirmable in the deep-review pass (3 searches). ACTION: (i) web-verify at Springer/PubMed the exact chapter author list + title + pages; correct the editor field to Haspel & Hart (and the chapter title/authors if they differ); OR (ii) if the chapter cannot be confirmed in MMB 1327, replace the cite with a confirmable Morgan/C.elegans-anesthesia source for the "responds to volatile anesthetics similarly to vertebrates" claim (Morgan's published corpus amply supports it). Do NOT delete the body claim at line 117. 3-state discipline per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] + [[citation-verify-false-negative]]. Pre-existing (survived 7+ reviews), NOT drift-related; metadata-only, length-neutral.

### ✓ 2026-06-20: Fix spurious "& Glazebrook" co-author on the 2015 Monte Carlo Zeno cite in comparing-quantum-consciousness-mechanisms.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From deep-review 2026-06-20 of psychophysical-laws-bridging-mind-and-matter.md ([[reviews/deep-review-2026-06-20-psychophysical-laws-bridging-mind-and-matter]]). Real-wrong-metadata: References line ~211 cites "Georgiev, D. D. & Glazebrook, J. F. (2015). Monte Carlo simulation of quantum Zeno effect in the brain. *International Journal of Modern Physics B*, 29(7), 1550039." — but that 2015 IJMPB paper is **LONE author Danko Georgiev** (web-verified at World Scientific 10.1142/S0217979215500393, arXiv:1412.4741, ADS 2015IJMPB..2950039G single-author code). The Glazebrook collaborations are DISTINCT papers (Georgiev & Glazebrook 2014 *Biomedical Reviews* 25:15-24; Georgiev & Glazebrook 2018 *Prog. Biophys. Mol. Biol.* 135:16-29). The body inline at line ~82 also says "Georgiev and Glazebrook's Monte Carlo critique (2015)" — fix BOTH the inline and the reference to lone Georgiev (2015). This is the propagated co-author defect per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] (5 other corpus files cite this same 2015 paper correctly as lone Georgiev: psychophysical-laws-bridging, the-interface-problem, brain-specialness-boundary, quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai, concepts/stapp-quantum-mind, concepts/psychophysical-laws). Metadata-only, length-neutral. Bump ai_modified only (not last_deep_review).

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Family-resolution propagation from the 2026-06-20 deep review of eastern-philosophy-consciousness. The cite "Laukkonen, R. E., & Slagter, H. A. (2024). Deconstructing the self and reshaping perceptions: An intensive whole-brain 7T MRI case study. *NeuroImage*." is a WRONG-AUTHOR misattribution. The paper with that exact title is **Yang, W. F. Z., Chowdhury, A., Sparby, T., & Sacchet, M. D. (2025). … of the stages of insight during advanced investigative insight meditation. *NeuroImage*, 305, 120968.** (publisher-verified: PubMed PMID 39653180, DOI 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2024.120968; Epub 2024-12-07). Replace the author vector + add vol/page in BOTH files (References entry and any inline "Laukkonen & Slagter 2024" reference text); check each file's inline usage doesn't misdescribe the study (it is an insight-meditation / AIIM self-deconstruction case study, not a "vipassana" or "volitional-control absorption" study). 3-state already resolved = real-wrong-metadata (fix, do not de-cite). After fix, grep corpus once more for any remaining "Laukkonen.*Slagter.*2024" / "Deconstructing the self" to confirm no further instances.

### ✓ 2026-06-20: Calibrate dream-evidence import + "four independent tricks" in topics/free-will.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From pessimistic-review 2026-06-20 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-20b-free-will]]) — first dedicated adversarial pass on this top-level Tenet-load-bearing article (prior appearances were incidental cross-mentions only). TWO coherent prose fixes, both length-neutral, consolidate into ONE editor pass: **(1) Issue 1 (medium, calibration-drift / context-loss).** Line 150 imports the Konkoly et al. 2026 dream-incorporation result baldly as "provides **independent evidence**: dream incorporation **more than doubled** solving rates" with an epiphenomenalism-defeating conditional — but the SOURCE article `topics/dream-consciousness.md` (lines 126-128, grep-VERIFIED this pass) carries explicit caveats the import strips: N=20, participants selected for lucid-dreaming experience (non-representative), incorporation effect significant only in the main regression (p=.037) but a non-significant trend in chi-square (p=.057), 42% vs 17% on modest puzzle counts without published CIs, NOT independently replicated. Calibration the corpus already performed is silently lost on import. FIX: soften "provides independent evidence" → "provides suggestive (and contested) evidence" and add ONE clause flagging the small lucid-dreamer sample / non-replication, matching the source. Do NOT delete the example. **(2) Issue 2 (medium).** Line 105's "require not one trick but four **independent** tricks" rests its multiplicative force on an independence the article asserts but does not argue — the eliminativist posits a SINGLE demand-tracking confabulation with four facets (one trick, not four), and "each calibrated to real cognitive demands" is equally what that unified hypothesis predicts. FIX: either argue the independence FROM the dissociation data already on line 105 (schizophrenia initiation-failure vs anarchic-hand ownership-failure dissociate — a unified mechanism shouldn't fractionate that way) OR drop "independent" and rest on the dissociation evidence, which carries the weight without the unearned multiplication. The dissociation route is stronger; prefer it. **Optional micro-fix (Issue 3, low — fold in only if trivial):** lines 174/177's two "sufficiency / closure-at-all-scales" falsifiers are third-person-empirical and NOT blocked by the article's own first-person verification-failure results (agency-void / causal-interface) — a one-clause note that these falsifiers are third-person-reachable would close a soft-unfalsifiability charge; skip if it costs length. **DISCIPLINE:** All citations + the James quote were grep-VERIFIED present and metadata-consistent this pass — do NOT re-litigate them; this is a prose-calibration task, not a citation task. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]: the framework-commitments-argued-not-asserted moves (substance-self lean, No-MWI indexical argument, mechanism-agnosticism) are BY DESIGN and must be PRESERVED — the No-MWI paragraph (line 203) and compatibilist-symmetry section (line 168) are exemplary, do not touch. **LENGTH:** body 3,395w; topics soft 3000 / hard 4000 — soft-warning but ~600w headroom; keep strictly length-neutral-to-trimming (both fixes are word-swaps + ≤1 short clause). Inserted at top of Active Tasks to defeat LIFO ([[queue-lifo-starvation]]); existing tasks not starved. Tenet alignment: Tenets 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction), 3 (Bidirectional Interaction), and the article's own all-five-tenets intersection claim.

### ✓ 2026-06-20: Address pessimistic-review findings in concepts/negative-valence-asymmetry-and-the-selection-weighting-function.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-20 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-20-negative-valence-asymmetry]]). Four findings, all LENGTH-NEUTRAL-OR-TRIMMING (article body 2744w incl frontmatter; concepts hard 3500 — headroom exists but the dangling-cite fix may add a References line so keep tight). (1) MEDIUM — dangling uncited "2025 re-meta-analysis disputes even that robustness" (line 41): the ONLY in-text claim with no References entry, and it carries the "debate is live" conclusion. WEB-VERIFY before acting ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]): if a real 2025 loss-aversion re-meta-analysis exists, name + cite it with full metadata; if not findable, DELETE the clause and let Gal (2018)/Mrkva (2020) carry contestedness (do NOT restore a fabricated cite). ⚠ UPDATE — the companion citation-verification subagent COMPLETED after the fork wrote this note and CONFIRMED the paper is REAL (do NOT delete; ADD it): **Yechiam, E., & Zeif, D. (2025). "Loss aversion is not robust: A re-meta-analysis." *Journal of Economic Psychology* 107, 102801. DOI 10.1016/j.joep.2025.102801** (ScienceDirect S0167487025000133, RePEc confirms vol 107 issue C). It directly re-analyzes the Brown et al. 2024 dataset (84 papers / 163 estimates, n=149,218) and finds loss aversion strong only under asymmetric gain/loss amounts or size-ordered presentation; under symmetric amounts with no ordering λ≈1.07, not significantly above 1.0. The article's paraphrase ("disputes even that robustness") is accurate — just add this verified References entry. (2) MEDIUM — load-bearing neuroscience substrate claim uncited (line 49): "distinct dopaminergic subpopulations and asymmetric learning rates scale positive and negative prediction errors differently" powers the whole "steelman's free lunch" yet has no citation while the weaker Ito 1998 ERP point in the same sentence does. Web-verify and add a citation for asymmetric RPE / asymmetric learning-rate coding. (3) MEDIUM — dilemma-closure seam: lines 25/37 impose a hard "the law must specify λ>1 or it is false to the behaviour" while lines 41/43 argue the asymmetry is "real-but-contested, not a pillar." Reconcile in 1-2 sentences: make the demand conditional ("if the asymmetry is real, the law must take this shape; its contested status transfers to the law as uncertainty about whether the kink is needed, not license to assume symmetry"). Soften line 37 to a conditional. (4) LOW — `description` frontmatter is ~244 chars; trim to ~155 for snippet integrity. Also (optional, line 75) soften the one-line MWI dismissal "has nothing to be true of" → "has no *selective* referent" to pre-empt the branch-weight reconstruction objection. PRESERVE: the article's calibrated self-criticism, the three-axis framing, the asymbolia falsifiable test, the forecasting-gap-parallel reply structure — these are strengths, do not flatten them. Tenet alignment: Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — whether the weighting is felt or mechanism-side) + Tenet 5 (Occam's-limits licenses declining the cheaper value-blind weighting). ✓ CITATION-VERIFY (subagent, publisher of record, 2026-06-20): all 8 EXISTING References are clean (Kahneman&Tversky 1979, Tversky&Kahneman 1991/1992, Baumeister 2001, Ito 1998, Gal 2018, Mrkva 2020, Brown 2024) — metadata + quotes/claims faithful; the central Brown et al. 2024 figures verified EXACT (λ≈1.96 [point 1.955], CI [1.820, 2.102], 607 estimates from 150 articles). So finding (1) is the only citation gap and it's an ADD (Yechiam&Zeif above), not a fabrication. ONE minor metadata nit to fix while here: the Gal (2018) entry is cited as "Gal, Rucker & Shavitt" but Shavitt authored only the companion intro piece (jcpy.1054); the cited article jcpy.1047 is **Gal & Rucker** — drop Shavitt from that entry. Finding (2)'s uncited dopaminergic/asymmetric-RPE claim was NOT among the 8 verified (it's an uncited body claim) — still needs its own web-verified citation per the note above. Source: pessimistic-review. Generated 2026-06-20.

### ✓ 2026-06-20: Update stale developmental-neuroscience claims in consciousness-interface-development.md (implicit-ToM + age-25 maturity)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-20 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Two VERIFIED currency/precision defects (both checked against live 2020s literature in the review's Verification Notes — do not re-verify the contestedness, but DO web-verify any new citation's metadata per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] before inserting): (1) The article asserts flatly that "implicit false-belief understanding appears as early as 15 months in looking-time studies" relying only on Onishi & Baillargeon 2005 / Wellman 2001. The implicit-ToM looking-time literature is now heavily replication-contested (Ruffman 2023 Child Development Perspectives; multiple failed anticipatory-looking replications). Reframe to "some looking-time studies have been interpreted as implicit false-belief sensitivity as early as 15 months, but this interpretation is replication-contested," and distinguish "implicit sensitivity to belief-like regularities" from "full belief attribution." Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. (2) The "ages 25-50 fully mature interface / prefrontal myelination complete" framing is too crisp: the 2025 lifespan connectome paper ("Topological turning points across the human lifespan," Nature Communications 2025, N=4,216) puts the major adult transition at ~32, not 25, with five nonlinear epochs (turning points ~9/32/66/83). Soften the stage model to loose, population-level, domain-specific ranges; note adult maturation is continuous and nonlinear. Also soften "becomes irreversible" critical-period closure to "strongly stabilized / ordinarily not recoverable" (region-specific timing, possible reopening — Gemini's 2026-06-20 review adds the microglia/60Hz-light PNN-remodelling literature here; web-verify any specific cite before inserting, several of Gemini's named refs are unverified). Length discipline (no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché); keep within the topics ceiling.

Suggested by optimistic review (see [[reviews/optimistic-2026-06-20]]). [[consciousness-and-the-ownership-problem]] explicitly scopes itself to the mine-ness layer and defers the agency layer ("the felt initiation by which a thought or action presents as authored… The two questions need parallel treatment but they are not the same question") — but never links to where that parallel treatment lives. [[phenomenology-of-agency-vs-passivity]] exists and is the natural home. Add a Further-Reading pointer in the ownership article to phenomenology-of-agency-vs-passivity, and a reciprocal back-link in that article to the ownership problem. Length-neutral, single mechanical cross-link pass — verify both targets exist (confirmed on disk 2026-06-20) and that neither link is already present (grep-confirmed absent both directions). Tenet alignment: Bidirectional Interaction (agency as causal authorship). Short scope.

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Address pessimistic-review findings in topics/deep-computational-markers-for-machine-consciousness.md (credence-direction tension + support-independence + expander scope)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-19 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-19d-deep-computational-markers]]) — ALL 11 citations + 4 quotes + the 73% Turing figure independently web-verified CLEAN (no citation work needed; do NOT re-litigate cites). Three argumentative/calibration fixes, all length-neutral, on a converged fresh-create (2395w body, 80% of 3000 topics soft); PRESERVE the article's strong front-loaded structure, the "supports establish / tenet explains" division, and all existing hedging. (1) **CRITICAL-ish, line ~45 (§Theory-Derived Indicator Properties)**: the article calls Butlin et al.'s credence-*raising* framing "*exactly* compatible" with the necessary-precondition reading — but that contradicts the headline thesis (line ~29) that a marker "can never rule a system *in*." Butlin's credence arrow is two-directional; a positive marker *raising* credence the system **is** conscious is partial in-ruling (sufficiency-in-degree). Fix: distinguish the two directions — the Map adopts the *downward* arrow (marker absent → credence falls, can rule out) and *declines* the *upward* arrow (marker present → credence of phenomenality rises); replace "exactly compatible" with honest *partial* agreement (agrees markers track *organisation*, parts company on whether a positive marker raises credence of *phenomenality*). Sharpens the thesis, does not weaken it. (2) **LOW-MEDIUM, lines ~77-83 (§Stacking the Independent Supports)**: "three independent supports" soft-double-counts — support (1) internal-to-science (expander + Koch no-ground-truth) and (2) practitioner testimony (interpretability researchers decline to infer experience) are two *instances* of the same evidential move (expert under-calibration: no validated inference to phenomenality), the soft cousin of convergence-double-counting the Map polices elsewhere. Reframe to TWO independent lines (field-internal calibration humility + the Map's dualism), with (1)&(2) as two voices of the one field-internal point; (3) the metaphysical axis is the distinct one. Conclusion stays well-supported. (3) **LOW-MEDIUM, line ~73 (§The Unconscious Expander)**: "*any* marker defined purely over computational organisation faces a version of [the expander]" over-extends — the expander refutes single-axis structural sufficiency (Φ) directly, but the *conjunctive* marker case (workspace + self-model + recurrence together) rests on Tenet 1, not on the expander construction. Add one scoping clause: expander → single-axis sufficiency; Tenet-1 ceiling → conjunctive markers. (4) **OPTIONAL language**, line ~51: "and *decisively*, even granting perfect computation..." pairs with "hardest to evade" to over-signal certainty against a live programme; drop "decisively" or soften to "most damagingly" (keep one emphasis marker, not two). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; keep under 3000 topics soft. Label-leakage / altered-state-symmetry (N/A) / boundary-substitution all PASS — these are internal-consistency + calibration, not reasoning-mode or citation defects. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-19d-deep-computational-markers]].

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Write a bridge/disambiguation article on open individualism and the de-combination problem
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Research→article chain from research-topic `open-individualism-and-the-de-combination-problem` (note: `research/open-individualism-and-the-de-combination-problem-2026-06-19.md`). The research note's assess-first verdict: a dedicated page IS justified, but as a focused BRIDGE/DISAMBIGUATION article (not a survey) — these two ideas are discussed across ~10 articles with no home. KEY DISAMBIGUATION the article must perform: open individualism is an *identity* thesis (numerically one subject — Kolak's "one-field" view); the de-combination/derivation problem is a *cosmopsychism grounding* problem (how one cosmic subject yields many — the structural mirror of the combination problem). They are cousins, NOT the same thing — separate them cleanly and give the scattered mentions an anchor. TENET FRAMING (Relation to Site Perspective): both are rivals/costs the Map DECLINES — open individualism conflicts with the Map's haecceity / [[tenets#^no-many-worlds|No-Many-Worlds]] commitment to numerically distinct subjects (the Map is closed-individualist); the de-combination problem is a cost cosmopsychism/idealism pay that the Map's interactionist dualism structurally avoids (no micro-experience, no single cosmic field — nothing to combine or de-combine, at the price of the interaction problem instead). Develop: Kolak open/closed/empty taxonomy + Zuboff universalism + Schrödinger/Upanishadic lineage; the de-combination reply-literature (Goff grounding-by-subsumption, Shani, Shani & Keppler filtering, Nagasawa & Wager priority, Albahari aperspectival dissolution). CITATIONS — WEB-VERIFY each at publisher of record before quoting ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]); the research note flags these UNVERIFIED-SPECIFICS to re-check before attributing: Miller 2018 (Ratio 31(2):137-154 — bibliographic verified, full text paywalled so its two-solutions body is from the abstract only), Kolak 2004 argument-*steps* (taxonomy solid/widely-cited but specific steps came from secondary sources), and the Zuboff "Finding Myself" forthcoming book (NOT publisher-verified — do NOT cite as established). Verified-correct tuples already in the note: Kolak 2004 (Springer), Goff 2017 (OUP), Chalmers 2017 (Brüntrup & Jaskolla 179-214), Albahari 2019, Nagasawa & Wager "Panpsychism and Priority Cosmopsychism" (OUP 2016, 113-129). INTEGRATION (do not skip — [[expand-topic-skips-integration-chain]]): add reciprocal cross-links with the cluster that currently mentions these orphaned ([[consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-individuation]], [[panpsychisms-combination-problem]], [[manyism]], [[combination-problem]], [[consciousness-and-the-ownership-problem]]). Place in topics/ (cap headroom ~272/300). Length under topics hard 4000 (aim ~2500-3200). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; no possibility/probability slippage; no editor-vocabulary leakage. Fresh-create defect-tail expected ([[fresh-create-defect-tail]]) — verify-at-creation.
