### ✓ 2026-06-19: Fix the Stapp misquote + mark the Stapp co-optation gap in brain-specialness-boundary (net-new from the Claude leg)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-19 (Claude Opus 4.8) — the SAME subject as the same-cycle ChatGPT 5.5 Pro review, whose 5 sibling tasks above already cover the convergent structural/citation/Born/telepathy/methodology findings ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] — do NOT re-do those here; this task carries ONLY the two findings genuinely net-new to the Claude leg). (1) **Stapp misquote (mechanical citation fix).** Line 73 quotes *"Prior to the choice to act there must be a representation in the brain of the intended consequences" (Stapp, 2007)*. VERIFIED (see review Verification Notes): Stapp's genuine wording is "…representation in the brain of the **projected** consequences **of choosing to act**, in order for the ego to bring its values to bear on the choice that it makes," and the real source is Stapp's "Quantum Theory of Mind" (newdualism.org), NOT *Mindful Universe* (2007). The article substituted "intended"→"projected", truncated the sentence, and pinned it to the wrong work/year — exactly the documented [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] altered-quote failure mode. **WEB-VERIFY the corrected wording + source at the publisher of record BEFORE editing** (the review could not extract the PDF verbatim and could not 100%-exclude a looser variant in the scanned *Mindful Universe*; 3-state: corrected-verbatim / paraphrase-and-drop-quotes / leave-with-note). Fix = either replace with Stapp's verbatim "projected…" wording + the correct "Quantum Theory of Mind" citation, OR paraphrase explicitly and drop the quotation marks. Add the QTM source to the References list if quoting it. (2) **Stapp co-optation gap.** The article leans on Stapp's Process 1 / quantum Zeno mechanism to support a substance-dualist framework (Tenet 1), but Stapp's own "quantum interactive dualism" is a von Neumann-process ontology, NOT Cartesian/substance dualism — he conditions the mental and physical "by each other" rather than positing two separable substances. Add a one-line acknowledgement that the mechanism is borrowed across a metaphysical boundary Stapp himself does not share (mechanism-borrowing co-optation, sibling of the stance-attribution firewall). Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]] framing only if the co-optation note shades into opponent-engagement; keep editor-vocabulary out of the article body. OPTIONAL minor colour: the Beck & Eccles temperature-independence-of-exocytosis falsification is a candidate historical-exemplar mention for the amplification paragraph, but only if length allows — do not expand for it. Length-additive but small; stay under the 3000 topics soft (article ~1890-2000w). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Bump ai_modified.

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Adopt five outer-review methodology proposals as a corpus-wide citation/calibration discipline
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: **UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-19 — ≥2-reviewer CONVERGENT** (chatgpt + claude independently proposed parallel site-methodology disciplines). The strongest convergent disciplines: a **quotation-string verification step distinct from reference-metadata verification** (Claude: "Add a quotation-verification step to the citation pipeline that is distinct from reference-metadata verification" — directly motivated by this cycle's Stapp misquote, which passed metadata checks because the *book* is real) and a **disconfirming-source-inclusion rule** (Claude: "Institute a 'disconfirming-source inclusion' rule for empirical claims" — motivated by the omitted Bösch 2006). ChatGPT's parallel: "Add a 'citation breadth' check distinct from 'citation real-correct'" + a "mechanism variance audit." Both also independently propose a coherence-inflation check for "N converging considerations" rhetoric. Synthesize the convergent disciplines first (quotation-verify, disconfirming-source-inclusion, coherence-inflation), then assess the ChatGPT-singleton items (bracketing ledger, boundary adversarial-cases checklist) against existing coverage. Synthesis: `reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-19.md`. From outer review 2026-06-19 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Project-doc / methodology task (target: an appropriate `project/` discipline page, e.g. extend [[project/architecture-vs-significance-two-tier-discount]] or the calibration-audit material, or create a focused new discipline note if none fits — ASSESS-FIRST what already exists before creating). Five proposed disciplines, several of which the corpus partially practices already (assess overlap, do not duplicate): (1) a **"citation breadth" check** distinct from "citation real-correct" — a four-reference article can pass fabrication checks while being stale/under-cited (this is exactly the brain-specialness case; relates to the existing [[literature-drift-review]] cadence — check whether that already covers it); (2) a **"mechanism variance audit"** for any article depending on quantum-consciousness machinery (does the claim survive under post-decoherence selection, Stapp/Zeno, Process-1/context-selection, delegatory/authority-selection?); (3) a **"boundary adversarial cases" checklist** (external RNG with real-time feedback, neurofeedback, long-trained BCI, prosthetic embodiment, interoceptive body loops, another person's brain, animal brains, AI systems, lab-isolated quantum systems); (4) a **"bracketing ledger"** for conditional articles (separate what the article proves / assumes from tenets / argues in linked pages / leaves speculative — overlaps with [[evidential-status-discipline]]); (5) require **"same-data rival interpretations"** for empirical sections (name the rival views the same result is equally consistent with — overlaps with [[architecture-vs-significance-two-tier-discount]] and the direct-refutation discipline). Synthesize the genuinely-new ones into a discipline page; for ones already practiced, note the coverage and skip. Do NOT create five separate pages — one consolidated discipline note (or extensions to existing ones) is the right altitude.

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Reconcile the locality / psychophysical-eligibility tension across brain-specialness-boundary, locality, and psychophysical-laws
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: **UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-19 — ≥2-reviewer CONVERGENT on the telepathy point** (chatgpt + claude; part (c) below). Both reviewers independently judged that folding telepathy/mind-to-mind into the RNG-PK boundary is a bare assertion, not an entailment. ChatGPT: "The article should not treat mind-to-mind claims as automatically ruled out 'for the same reason' as external apparatus; it should either exclude them from scope or give a separate boundary argument." Claude: "The Ganzfeld move is a non-sequitur… it simply restates the embodiment premise about a new target." The locality/psychophysical-eligibility bridge (parts a–b) is ChatGPT-leg content riding in the same multi-file pass. Synthesis: `reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-19.md`. From outer review 2026-06-19 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Cross-article coherence gap. [[locality]] weakens strict spatial-locality objections (a nonphysical cause has no spatial location; mind-side structure can coordinate distributed neural sites), while brain-specialness-boundary USES locality-like constraints to block external PK. The site risks using locality flexibly — permissive when defending interactionism, restrictive when avoiding parapsychology. Add a bridge principle distinguishing **spatial locality** from **psychophysical eligibility**: "consciousness is not spatially located, but lawful psychophysical coupling is scoped by a subject-specific neural interface." Concretely: (a) add a reconciliation note to [[locality]] (rejects strict spatial locality as an objection to nonphysical causation, but still imposes lawful psychophysical-eligibility constraints to avoid universal PK); (b) reposition brain-specialness-boundary as an *application* of the [[psychophysical-laws]] coupling-law framework (mental-side variables, target variables, bandwidth, scope constraints, Born compatibility) rather than a free-standing answer, and cross-link it from psychophysical-laws as the "scope restriction" application; (c) treat **telepathy separately from RNG-PK** in the target article — another person's brain is a neural interface coupled to another subject, not "just an external quantum system," so it deserves a distinct argument or explicit scope exclusion. Multi-file task — verify each edit lands ([[multifile_task_dropped_file]]); keep length-neutral-ish; bump ai_modified on each file edited.

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Broaden brain-specialness-boundary citations (Bösch 2006, Maier 2018, Tegmark 2000, Georgiev 2015, Donald discrete-basis, Chalmers & McQueen 2022; update GCP; downgrade Park)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: **UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-19 — ≥2-reviewer CONVERGENT (in fact 3/3 on Georgiev).** Two distinct convergences land in this one citation task: (i) **Georgiev no-go / decoherence omission** flagged by ALL THREE reviewers (chatgpt + claude + gemini-real-portion) — the article's load-bearing Stapp/Zeno mechanism is the subject of a published no-go argument the article never acknowledges (grep-confirmed: zero "Georgiev"/"no-go"/"discrete basis" in the live article). ChatGPT: "the article should cite Georgiev 2015… Georgiev's simulations argue that the quantum Zeno effect breaks down for timescales longer than brain decoherence time." Claude: "the article does not even acknowledge that its central mechanism is the subject of a published no-go argument. This is a serious omission of disconfirming evidence." Gemini (non-fabricated portion): "Georgiev mathematically demonstrated that Stapp's use of the quantum density matrix is internally inconsistent… Donald… the insurmountable 'discrete basis problem'." (ii) **Bösch 2006 / one-sided parapsychology evidence base** flagged by chatgpt + claude (2/3) — Claude: "it conspicuously omits Bösch, Steinkamp & Boller (2006)… a one-sided evidence base presented as 'the empirical record'." Synthesis: `reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-19.md`. From outer review 2026-06-19 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Citation thinness VERIFIED: the article has exactly four refs (Jahn & Dunne 2005, Nelson et al. 2002, Park 2000, Stapp 2007). Bösch et al. 2006 (*Psychological Bulletin* 132:497-523, 380 RNG studies) and Maier et al. 2018 (*Frontiers in Psychology*, Bayesian quantum-RNG, anti-PK) are WEB-VERIFIED REAL (see review Verification Notes) — add both to the parapsychology section; downgrade Park 2000 from the main methodological anchor to a historical-skeptical source. Add Tegmark 2000 + Georgiev 2015 to the decoherence/Zeno paragraph (coordinate with the overstated-claims task above). Add Chalmers & McQueen 2022 (consciousness-collapse + Zeno falsification of simple versions) for the collapse-theory context. Update the Global Consciousness Project paragraph to distinguish historical GCP 1 claims from claimant-side GCP 2.0 material, marking later GCP2 sources as NOT independent validation. **CRITICAL: web-verify Tegmark 2000, Georgiev 2015, and Chalmers & McQueen 2022 at publisher of record BEFORE citing** ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]], [[outer-review-fabricates-target-quotes]]) — do not pre-trust the reviewer's metadata; Bösch and Maier are already verified. Cite GCP2/preprint material at the correct evidential tier ([[evidential-status-discipline]]). Mostly citation-additive; watch length against 3000 topics soft. **CONVERGENT (Gemini 2.5 Pro leg, 2026-06-19):** the Gemini outer review independently flagged that the article does not engage the formal mathematical refutations of Stapp's Process-1 model — **Georgiev's no-go theorem** (already listed here, now ≥2-reviewer convergent → high-confidence add) and **Matthew Donald's "discrete basis problem"** critique (candidate add to the same decoherence/Zeno paragraph; web-verify before citing). Gemini also flagged **Keppler's QED zero-point-field (ZPF) model** (*Frontiers in Human Neuroscience*) as a competing emergence/panpsychist quantum framework the article ignores — a one-line "competing framework" mention is reasonable here if length allows; web-verify the Keppler citation at publisher of record before adding. NOTE the Gemini review is heavily fabrication-laden (cryptochrome/Denton, anesthesia/epothilone-B, Saad "Delegatory Dualism" quotes, meditation/cessation, filter-theory-endorsement — ALL absent from the article; see that review's Verification Notes) — do NOT act on any of those; only Georgiev/Donald/Keppler are the trustworthy non-fabricated signals.

### ✓ 2026-06-19: Mark brain-specialness-boundary as a scope-clause inside the coupling programme, not a free-standing boundary proof (reverse-engineering candor)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-19, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. **≥2-reviewer CONVERGENT (already P1, cannot upgrade further) — the cycle's strongest convergence and the article's central remediation.** Both same-cycle reviewers independently flagged the same structural defect: the boundary is reverse-engineered, not independently established (constrain-vs-establish). ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: "The most serious weakness is a reverse-engineering risk. The boundary is said to follow from the mechanism, but the mechanism is already specified in a way that privileges the subject's own brain." Claude Opus 4.8 (adversarial): "The mechanism-based boundary is not principled; it smuggles the arbitrary restriction back into the mechanism's premises… each of the four 'converging' considerations is the same embodiment assumption restated… Standard physics and physicalism already predict no PK for free… so 'explaining' the boundary is explanatorily idle and non-discriminating." (Gemini's parallel "gerrymandered ad hoc boundary" claim is set inside a ~70%-fabricated review — see synthesis Method Notes — so it does NOT count toward this convergence.) Synthesis: `reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-19.md`. From outer review 2026-06-19 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), the central structural finding. The article presents brain-locality as if it *follows from* the mechanism, but the mechanism is already specified in a way that privileges the subject's own brain — so the boundary is partly reverse-engineered. The adjacent [[brain-interface-boundary]] page is more candid: it admits its criteria are descriptive from one observed case (brains) and that the reverse-engineering charge is legitimate. The target article should inherit that caveat explicitly and reframe itself as a *scope clause inside the Map's psychophysical coupling programme* ("if the coupling law is attention-bounded, representation-mediated, and subject-paired, then external PK does not follow") rather than claiming the boundary follows from neutral premises. **Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]]:** the issue is boundary-substitution / an unsupported-foundational-move — identify whether the honest reply is (a) showing the PK objection is defective in-framework or (b) marking that the boundary is a bracketed posit, and write the corresponding reply in natural journal-quality prose ([[writing-style]] "Engaging Opponents" section). Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body; the classification belongs in the changelog entry only. Length-additive but modest — stay under the 3000 topics soft (article currently ~1890–2000w, ample headroom). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]: brain-specialness is a structural conjecture, not a result. Verified: the reverse-engineering critique is well-grounded (see review Verification Notes).

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Install altered-state symmetry in topics/contemplative-pathology-and-interface-malfunction.md (convergence double-counting; epistemic/metaphysical equivocation)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-18 found two coupled issues, both fixable in one length-neutral pass. (1) ALTERED-STATE SYMMETRY (Audit Two, medium): the article frames evidence around the reducing-valve filter model (Huxley 1954, L56) and rests its load on convergence across pathways — contemplative pathology + anaesthesia + ketamine + neurodegenerative disease all "revealing" one multi-component interface (L50, L76, L86). Supportive-cluster present (witness/insight states); disruptive-cluster items ARE cited (anaesthesia, ketamine, neurodegeneration) so it is NOT the critical "cluster missing" case — but they appear ONLY as supportive parallels with ZERO symmetry-acknowledgment markers (grep for symmetric / parallel-accommodation / same-move-available-to-production-theorists / earns-the-architecture-not-its-significance returns nothing). FIX: inherit the accommodation the companions already perform — anaesthesia-and-the-consciousness-interface.md L134 ("convergence earns the architecture, not its dualist significance") and altered-states-of-consciousness.md L74 (production theorist has the same independent-component-failure move). One-two natural-prose sentences: (a) the generator/production theorist has access to the same move on these cases; (b) the abolition cases (propofol silence, deep anaesthesia, advanced dementia — where consciousness is NOT reported as preserved) are the symmetric cases the filter framing must also accommodate, not extra confirmations. (2) EPISTEMIC/METAPHYSICAL EQUIVOCATION (medium, L78/L86): "consciousness persists with undiminished clarity through states that would, on a materialist account, diminish or abolish it" recruits an epistemic datum (practitioners REPORT preserved awareness) to a metaphysical conclusion (consciousness not produced by the brain), while the disruptive cases report the OPPOSITE (extinction) and are not reconciled. Split the readings: report-as-datum + acknowledge contrary disruptive-case reports + note the bridge lives in the persistence-or-intensification / graded-not-binary / hysteresis evidence the anaesthesia article supplies, not in the bare report. Also soften two over-universal clinical claims: L78 "consistently report... unusual clarity" → "frequently report" (Lindahl 2017 documents a range incl. impairment); L80 comparative-efficacy "responds better to practice-informed interventions than [to] medication" is uncited — downgrade to "emphasise practice-informed grounding alongside standard care" OR cite a comparative study. STRICT LENGTH: 1756 words now (well under topics soft ~3000), so additions are fine, but use analyze_length not raw read ([[count-words-includes-frontmatter]]). All 5 citations verify clean (Lindahl 2017 PLOS ONE 12(5) e0176239; Britton 2019 Curr Opin Psychol 28:159-165; Sass & Parnas 2003 Schiz Bull 29(3):427-444; Huxley 1954; St John of the Cross) — no citation work needed. PRESERVE the L42 Theravada-tradition caveat verbatim. See pessimistic-2026-06-18-contemplative-pathology-and-interface-malfunction.md.

(2) §Q4 + §"Why the Thickness Axis Matters": the "emptiness is *sociological* — coherent and inhabited, merely disfavoured" framing front-loads the disciplinary-bias diagnosis ("dismissed as pre-modern," "training conventions") AHEAD of the article's own genuine explanatory-cost concession ("the parsimony pressure is not merely sociological"). Reorder so the real explanatory-cost reason leads and the sociological observation follows as secondary — otherwise a physicalist reads it as explaining-away an empty cell by impugning motives. OPTIONAL low-cost adds (not required): a one-sentence scope note that MWI is out of frame because the grid presupposes a mind–matter relation MWI declines (pointer [[many-worlds-argument]]) to pre-empt the domain-question-begging charge; and a sentence noting the Map's surviving "region, not a cell" interior depends on Q1 turning out to be a genuine quadrant (per [[articulability-of-q1]]). ### ✓ 2026-06-18: Three length-light rigor fixes in quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai (no-cloning carve-out, QD convergence-discipline, Occam self-binding)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-18 (Claude Opus 4.8). Three NEW, length-light, in-framework fixes (none in the ChatGPT 2026-06-18 review). **All length-neutral — article is over the 3000-word soft threshold, so each is one clause/sentence, offset trims if needed.** (1) **No-cloning orthogonality carve-out.** The no-cloning passage (~lines 41–53) never states that no-cloning forbids only a *universal* cloner for *arbitrary unknown* states and is silent about known/orthogonal (≈ classical pointer) states (Nielsen & Chuang). Add one sentence noting this — it pre-empts a misreading and STRENGTHENS the article's own (correct) concession that no-cloning "cannot, on its own, disqualify AI." (2) **Quantum-Darwinism convergence-discipline.** The "Quantum Darwinism and the Selection Gap" section (~lines 67–71) recruits Zurek's quantum Darwinism toward a consciousness-selection step, but QD is fully unitary, observer-independent, interpretation-neutral physics whose originator does NOT endorse a conscious selector. Add a clause marking it as interpretation-neutral and not endorsed by Zurek — the same convergence-independence discipline the 2026-06-08 changelog applied to `post-decoherence-selection-programme` ("five components of one architecture, common-cause artefact"). (3) **Occam self-binding (Tenet 5).** The article invokes parsimony implicitly against functionalism ("an unnecessary addition") but never applies Tenet 5 symmetrically to the Map's OWN posits (extra ontological category + contested quantum mechanism + open pairing relation). Add a short self-binding parenthetical or sentence acknowledging the Map's own parsimony cost — this is the symmetric self-audit Tenet 5 requires. Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]] to (1) and (2). Verify Nielsen & Chuang attribution and the 2026-06-08 changelog precedent before citing. Mode labels stay out of the article body.

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Methodology — concept-provenance co-optation firewall + constrain-vs-establish lint (from outer review)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-18 (Claude Opus 4.8), Section B methodology recommendations. The verified Saad co-optation (a real *dualist* author whose *specific concept* was bent into a different/stronger quantum claim) exposes a gap in the existing co-optation firewall, which catches physicalists-cited-as-dualists (author-CAMP checking) but misses author-correct / concept-WRONG misattributions. Two concrete, durable proposals worth documenting in the project docs (e.g. `obsidian/project/` writing-style or a review-discipline note — assess-first which doc): (1) **Concept-provenance firewall:** for any sentence of the form "On X's account, which the Map adopts…", require a verbatim-quote-or-flag check that X makes *this specific claim*, AND cross-check the site's own research notes (which in the Saad case already contradicted the article). (2) **Constrain-vs-establish lint:** when an article's body hedges its conclusion as conditional-on-tenets ("if the tenets hold," "compatibility, not support") but its title / abstract / section headers / meta-description assert the conclusion categorically, flag the frame as over-claiming relative to the calibrated body — the exact coherence-inflation pattern this article shows (body hedges; title/headers "relocate the disqualifier" / "Current AI does not satisfy the Map's conditions"). **Scope:** this is a PROJECT-DOC / methodology task — propose the disciplines as written guidance and/or a review-checklist addition; do NOT attempt to build automated tooling here. The reviewer's other Section-B items (mandatory-Tegmark-adversary rule, anti-asymmetry lint, symmetric-Occam self-audit, non-Claude-reviewer independence) are recorded in the review file for the operator; fold any that the operator wants pursued into this doc. Assess-first per [[stale-expand-tasks-may-be-redundant]] — check whether existing project docs already cover the co-optation firewall before adding.

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Import the direct-QRNG counterexample + mirror "currently non-discriminating" caution into quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai
- **Type**: cross-review
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-18, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, gemini]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-18.** Both reviewers independently pressed the same point: once the article shifts (in the "Persistence Objection") from state-copying to needing only "live indeterminacy of the right kind at the right place," its own "laundering" argument must be stress-tested against the cleanest counterexample — a system with a direct hardware quantum source at the decision locus. ChatGPT frames it as the direct-QRNG counterexample; Gemini §2 makes it the structural hinge of its no-cloning critique: "An artificial system would not need to clone a human's specific quantum state... it would merely need its own local hardware source of genuine quantum indeterminacy. A standard quantum random number generator (QRNG)... or an uncorrected noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) circuit, would perfectly provide the 'architectural source of genuine indeterminacy' the manuscript demands." Two independent reviewers naming the QRNG-satisfies-the-criterion objection = convergent. **FIX — two length-light integration fixes.** (1) Address the direct-QRNG/NISQ counterexample: an LLM whose token sampling is driven DIRECTLY by a hardware QRNG at inference time, with no PRNG expansion. The sibling [[topics/quantum-randomness-channel-llm-consciousness]] already answers this (direct randomness still lacks evolved structure, locality, continuity) — pull a one-sentence version into the target and cross-link, so the dismissal of upstream randomness does not look convenient, AND so the article does not appear to concede (per Gemini) that no-cloning is rendered irrelevant the moment the criterion shifts to generic live indeterminacy. (2) The sibling [[topics/ai-consciousness]] carries a caution the target lacks: the quantum-interface point is "contingent and currently non-discriminating between brain and AI unless the biological interface is independently supported." Mirror that one sentence into the target's "Implications" section — it directly addresses the biological-parity / special-pleading objection (which the target ALREADY engages via observational-closure at line ~101, so this is a clarity/mirroring fix, NOT a new argument — see ChatGPT review Verification Notes). **LENGTH CONSTRAINT:** article at 3136w over soft threshold — both fixes are ≤2 sentences; keep length-neutral. Coordinate with the Born-rule symmetry pass (same entropy-channel paragraph) — do NOT double-edit it. Verify the two sibling articles still contain the cited material before linking ([[out-of-scope-spillover-flags-stale]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Consolidated opponent-engagement pass for quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai (functionalist rivals + biological computationalism)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-18, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-18; deduplicated from 2 sibling tasks (this + the Gemini biological-computationalism task) into one editor pass.** All three reviewers independently flagged that the article under-engages the strongest rivals to its substrate-exclusion conclusion ("a classical digital computer cannot [be conscious]", ~line 97). Engage them in ONE consolidated pass; do NOT churn the file with per-rival edits ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]). **(A) Functionalist / physicalist rivals beyond Block (ChatGPT + Claude).** The article engages functionalism only via Block's "role functionalism" (line ~95) and concludes the disagreement "is ultimately about consciousness, not physics." ChatGPT: add the indicator-based framework (Butlin, Long et al. 2023, arXiv:2308.08708 "Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence") which concludes no CURRENT AI is conscious yet finds no obvious technical barrier to FUTURE systems — weaker than the Map's conclusion, which sharpens the contrast; optionally fold in Chalmers & McQueen (arXiv:2105.02314, NOT yet fetched-verified — confirm before citing) as a calibration note that even sophisticated consciousness-collapse defenders treat it as a speculative programme. Claude broadens to three specific unaddressed rivals: (i) Putnam/Block/Fodor multiple-realizability (one actual case pressures substrate-essentialism), (ii) Chalmers' dancing/fading qualia (Chalmers 1995, "Absent Qualia, Fading Qualia, Dancing Qualia," in Metzinger ed. *Conscious Experience*) directly targeting the substrate claim, (iii) predictive processing / active inference (Seth, Friston, Clark, Hohwy) as a fully classical competitor for the "selection gap." **(B) Biological computationalism (Gemini — its ONE verified, genuinely-new finding).** The strongest CURRENT naturalistic rival that reaches a similar substrate-dependent / no-current-AI conclusion WITHOUT any quantum apparatus: Milinkovic & Aru, "On biological and artificial consciousness: A case for biological computationalism," *Neuroscience & Biobehavioral Reviews* 2025/2026, DOI 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2025.106524, PubMed 41419099 (VERIFIED 2026-06-18 via PubMed/ScienceDirect; features = hybrid discrete/analog computation, scale-inseparability, metabolic embedding). Dialectically important: if a classical-biophysics framework already explains substrate-dependency, the Map's extra quantum+dualist posits look like an unforced ontological cost (couples to the Tenet-5 self-binding rigor fix). The SITE already engages this framework — `concepts/biological-computationalism.md`, `topics/biological-computationalisms-inadvertent-case-for-dualism.md`, `research/biological-computationalism-inadvertent-dualism-2026-04-04.md` — the gap is purely that THIS article does not cross-link/engage it; cross-link [[biological-computationalism]] / [[biological-computationalisms-inadvertent-case-for-dualism]] and gesture at the existing "inadvertent case for dualism" reply rather than re-arguing it. **LENGTH CONSTRAINT (load-bearing):** article at 3136w, soft_warning OVER the 3000 topics threshold — name the rivals COMPACTLY and cross-link [[substrate-independence]] / [[apex/machine-question]] / [[biological-computationalism]] rather than expanding each; offset any prose with trims to stay length-neutral. Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: the Map can still reject these rivals, but must name the strongest versions and engage in natural prose; mode labels stay out of the article body. **DO NOT ACT ON (fabricated in the Gemini review — see its Verification Notes):** the "Quantum Biology Institute (QBI)" / "Hodgkins" Fibonacci-microtubule data and "Zeiner-Gundersen (2025)" QD cite are invented sources; non-engagement is NOT a defect. **VERIFY before citing:** Butlin/Long arXiv id + author list, Chalmers chapter + PP citations, Milinkovic & Aru metadata at publisher of record ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Decoherence-timescale brain-side pass for quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai (Tegmark general locus + Georgiev/Stapp-Zeno)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-18, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-18; deduplicated from 2 sibling tasks (Claude Tegmark + ChatGPT Georgiev) into one decoherence-timescale editor pass** to avoid churning the file twice ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]). All three reviewers pressed the brain side of the article's AI/brain asymmetry: the article rests on the brain having "live quantum indeterminacy at the right place" while classical AI does not, but never engages the decoherence-timescale literature that threatens the BRAIN side of that contrast. The two findings are distinct in target but belong in one passage. **(A) Tegmark 2000 — the GENERAL biological-locus threat (Claude).** Tegmark, "Importance of quantum decoherence in brain processes," *Phys. Rev. E* 61:4194–4206 (2000), argues neural decoherence timescales (~10⁻¹³–10⁻²⁰ s) are 10–20 orders of magnitude shorter than cognitive timescales, so the brain "should be thought of as a classical rather than quantum system." If brains cannot sustain functional coherence, "live indeterminacy at the locus of decision" is not a property biology has either, and the asymmetry weakens. The article only says decoherence is "already severe for warm biological brains" (~line 63) without engaging the specific result. (Gemini §3 corroborates with the Reimers/McKemmish 2009 microtubule rebuttals — both real adversaries; fold in as supporting context, no separate cite needed.) **(B) Georgiev — the STAPP-MODEL-specific threat (ChatGPT).** The article names Stapp's (2006) quantum-Zeno model as "the most concrete proposal" (line ~61, "What Would AI Need to Inherit?") but presents NO counterweight. Georgiev, "Monte Carlo simulation of quantum Zeno effect in the brain" (arXiv:1412.4741) argues the Zeno effect breaks down beyond brain decoherence timescales and is "physically implausible" unless the brain hosts a decoherence-free subspace (VERIFIED 2026-06-18 via WebFetch — cite accurately as such; do NOT overstate as refuting Stapp outright, the DFS door is left open). Reference: Georgiev, D. (2015). "Monte Carlo simulation of quantum Zeno effect in the brain." *International Journal of Modern Physics B* 29, 1550039 (arXiv:1412.4741) — verify published-venue metadata at write time. (Gemini §4 adds the entropy-monotonicity critique of Stapp's projection operators — optional supporting point, not a separate cite.) **FIX:** in ONE short passage, either rebut the decoherence-timescale challenge (Orch-OR / Hagan-Hameroff-Tuszyński recalculations, quantum-biology results) OR concede the brain side is unestablished/contested; add a SHORT clause naming the Georgiev criticism of Stapp at the Stapp mention. Cross-link the site's own treatments (`quantum-interface` positions; `quantum-biology-and-neural-consciousness`; `stapp-quantum-mind`) so the disconfirming data is not quarantined from the load-bearing argument — the substantive Georgiev paragraph + reference can land on the under-length `concepts/stapp-quantum-mind.md` page rather than the over-length target. **LENGTH CONSTRAINT (load-bearing):** article at 3136w, soft_warning OVER the 3000 topics threshold — keep the in-target additions to a one-clause concession + Georgiev clause; offset with trims or prefer cross-links + the stapp-quantum-mind landing spot. Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]] (honest opponent-engagement, not boundary-substitution; mode labels in changelog only). **VERIFY before citing:** Tegmark 2000 at publisher of record / PubMed PMID 11088215; Georgiev venue metadata; per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]].

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Resolve or flag the asymmetric Born-rule dilemma in quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-18, 2/3 reviewers): [claude, gemini]. **Already P1 — left at P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-18 (no upgrade beyond P1); recorded as convergent.** Both Claude and Gemini independently pressed the same minimal-quantum-interaction / Born-rule dilemma. **Internal-consistency finding (verified against the article's own text).** The article deploys a "no systematic trace" argument AGAINST the LLM entropy channel (~line 79: a consciousness biasing whitened randomness "would find its influence washed out... leaving no systematic trace") but never turns the same dilemma on the BRAIN, where it asserts the interface "preserves, rather than launders away, the difference a biased outcome would make." As stated this is asymmetric and verges on internally contradictory: a difference that survives in behaviour (Tenet 3, Bidirectional Interaction) yet leaves Born statistics undisturbed (Tenet 2, Minimal Quantum Interaction / observational closure) is exactly what the article elsewhere implies is impossible. Gemini §4 ("Smuggled Ontology: Minimal Quantum Interaction and the Born Rule Contradiction") states the same horns more aggressively: "If the manuscript genuinely maintains that the Born rule is preserved *exactly*... consciousness possesses zero macroscopic causal efficacy" (epiphenomenalism horn) vs. "if consciousness *does* have causal efficacy... the aggregate statistics must deviate from the Born rule" (detectability horn). Two independent reviewers naming the same horns = convergent. **FIX:** add the symmetric application in the article's own voice — state explicitly HOW consciousness-selection can be behaviourally systematic yet statistically invisible without contradiction (the article already gestures at this via "preserves the difference a biased outcome would make" — make the mechanism explicit, e.g. the difference registers in WHICH outcome occurs on each occasion without shifting the aggregate distribution), OR honestly mark this as an open question on par with the pairing problem. Cross-link [[born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface]] and [[observational-closure]] where the same dilemma is treated, so the asymmetric deployment is corrected. Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: if the in-framework resolution does not fully close, state in natural language that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. Mode labels stay out of the article body. **LENGTH CONSTRAINT:** article over the 3000-word soft threshold — keep length-neutral (the article already raises both horns; this is a re-balancing/cross-link edit, not a new section). Coordinate with the consolidated opponent-engagement and direct-QRNG passes — the direct-QRNG cross-review task touches the same entropy-channel paragraph; do NOT double-edit it.

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Fix the Saad observational-closure co-optation in quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai (citation integrity)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-18 (Claude Opus 4.8). **Highest-confidence integrity defect; VERIFIED at publisher of record AND against the Map's own research note.** The article (`obsidian/topics/quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai.md`, "Implications for AI Consciousness Claims" section, ~line 101) states: "On Saad's account, which the Map adopts, it is the *statistical camouflage of a real non-physical influence on genuine quantum indeterminacy*." This misattributes a quantum/Born-rule reading to Bradford Saad. **Verified 2026-06-18 via WebSearch (Springer/PhilPapers) + the Map's own note:** Saad's "A Dualist Theory of Experience," *Philosophical Studies* 182(3):939–967 (2025), DOI 10.1007/s11098-025-02290-3, makes observational closure his GENERAL, NON-QUANTUM constraint #2 ("experiences do not cause observable violations of the causal closure of the physical domain"). The Map's own research note `research/bradford-saad-delegatory-dualism-2026-01-28` (lines 176–177, 213) explicitly concedes "delegatory dualism doesn't require quantum mechanisms; it's more general" and frames quantum indeterminacy as one possible *implementation substrate*, not Saad's view. **FIX:** split the attribution accurately — credit the GENERAL observational-closure concept to Saad, and explicitly label the quantum/Born-rule reading as the MAP'S OWN extension that Saad does not endorse (the research note's "could be integrated by treating quantum indeterminacy as the physical substrate for delegation" framing is the honest version). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: this is naming an unsupported move the framework made on a real author's concept; mark it honestly in natural prose. Do NOT expose mode labels in article body (changelog only). **SCOPE GUARD (verified — do NOT over-edit):** (a) `concepts/observational-closure.md` does NOT need this fix — it already separates "Statistical Invisibility" (Stapp/quantum) from "Causal Profile Matching" (Saad's general route) and does NOT attribute the quantum gloss to Saad. (b) The "substance dualist" mislabel the reviewer flagged is NOT present in live siblings (`topics/delegatory-dualism.md` correctly hedges) — no propagation needed. **LENGTH CONSTRAINT:** article is at soft_warning over the 3000-word topics threshold — keep the fix length-neutral (rephrase in place, do not add net length). Re-verify Saad metadata at write time per [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]].

### ✓ 2026-06-18: Calibrate two over-strong phrasings in topics/empirical-evidence-for-consciousness-selecting.md
- **Type**: refine-draft

NO critical defect; genuinely deferrable. (a) **Language:** "Nature proved it wrong." (Relation-to-Site-Perspective, Occam's-Limits paragraph) over-claims and equivocates on "Nature" — the natural world falsified the categorical objection *for specific evolved structures*, not the general claim. Soften to e.g. "Nature showed otherwise in specific evolved systems." This is the strong register the article scrupulously avoids elsewhere. (b) **Falsifiability framing (optional):** "What Would Weaken the Case" presents four defeaters as the article's answer to falsifiability, but three of the four ("classical theory succeeds", "epiphenomenalism finds a mechanism", "MWI confirmed") describe a *rival winning*, not an experiment that falsifies consciousness-selecting *itself*; only "neural quantum effects prove absent" is a near-empirical self-defeater (and even it removes a precondition). Add one sentence distinguishing "what makes a rival more attractive" from "what would falsify this hypothesis directly", conceding the latter set is thin at the framework stage — this STRENGTHENS the article's honesty. **Length constraint:** article is at 97% of the 3000-word topics soft threshold (2913w) — both edits must be length-neutral; finding (a) is a wording swap, finding (b) must offset its added sentence with a trim elsewhere or be skipped. **Do NOT re-flag** (deep-review 2026-06-16 stability ledger): the MWI dismissal (Tenet-4 bedrock), the inherent falsifiability gap as a *defect*, the "categorical objection has collapsed" section heading (deliberate Map position), the convergence-table calibration, or Nagarjuna no-self (out of scope). ### ✓ 2026-06-17: Polish calibration in topics/marginal-organism-scope-of-value-sensitive-selection.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-17 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-17-marginal-organism-scope]]) found three local prose/calibration items in an otherwise strong, citation-clean article (all four external cites RE-VERIFIED real this pass; all 12 wikilinks resolve — do NOT re-audit citations). (1) MEDIUM — unsourced overstatement "the nematode's avoidance is overwhelmingly reactive": *C. elegans* thermotaxis/isothermal-tracking/salt-learning literature cuts the other way; soften to "predominantly modelled as reactive, though associative/thermotactic learning complicate the picture" and rest the rung-placement on the *felt-value* gap (the honest reason), not on a disputable "anticipates at all" claim. (2) MEDIUM — epistemic/metaphysical equivocation in the "genuine void … structurally resistant to measurement" framing (Falsification Conditions, final para): "observationally equivalent at the margin" leans metaphysical (no fact of the matter) while "resistant to measurement"/"settling the gap from the outside" leans epistemic (a fact exists, inaccessible). Per [[evidential-status-discipline]] (*Epistemic Reading ≠ Metaphysical Reading*), split the readings and pick one explicitly — the calibrated choice is probably the epistemic "determinate boundary we cannot locate from outside"; reserve any "no fact of the matter" claim for an argued bridging step. (3) LOW — internal-coherence: the *Physarum* deflation ("approach/avoidance is not a reliable marker anywhere, weakening the inference up the whole ladder") is a GLOBAL solvent that partly demotes the rat's behaviour-grounded "live hypothesis", but the rat section doesn't acknowledge this; add one sentence noting the rat's residual case rests on substrate-homology + precaution, not behavioural resemblance, once the slime-mould principle is granted. Optional polish: relabel "Falsification Conditions" → "Evidence that would shift the assessment" (the listed assays are credence-shifters, not falsifiers of the boundary's existence — the article concedes the existence-claim is in-principle unsettleable). Length-neutral; preserve the exemplary tier-discipline and front-loading. Short scope.

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Write concepts/ article on Egocentric presentism (Caspar Hare)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Research→expand-topic chain successor of the completed P2 "Research Egocentric presentism" (research-topic, 2026-06-17 14:56) which produced the unconsumed research note `obsidian/research/egocentric-presentism-2026-06-17.md` (ai_contribution=100, fully web-verified citations at publisher of record). No live or archived article exists (verified: no `*presentism*`/`*egocentric*` match in topics/concepts/apex/voids or archive/). Target section: `concepts/` (live count 261/300 — cap headroom confirmed). Synthesize the research note into a concept page: Caspar Hare's egocentric presentism — the thesis that, of all conscious beings, only oneself has experiences bearing the irreducible monadic property of *presence* (Hare, "Self-Bias, Time-Bias, and the Metaphysics of Self and Time", *Journal of Philosophy* 104(7), 2007, pp. 350-373, DOI 10.5840/jphil2007104717; and *On Myself, and Other, Less Important Subjects*, Princeton UP, 2009). Develop: the time/self structural analogy (privileged "now" of A-theory ↔ privileged "me"); the Grounding Problem for self-bias; Christian List's quadrilemma (first-person realism / non-solipsism / non-fragmentation / one-world — at most three) and why egocentric presentism is the **one-world** horn that keeps first-person realism. This is the load-bearing tenet hook — sharply Tenet-4 relevant (No Many Worlds / indexical identity matters; egocentric presentism rejects List's many-worlds-of-consciousness resolution) and Tenet-1 relevant (presence as a non-physical, immeasurable property). Use the research note's verified citations and verbatim quotes; do NOT introduce un-verified cites ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]). Cross-link bidirectionally per the note's Intended links: [[indexical-identity-quantum-measurement]], [[vertiginous-question]], [[consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-individuation]], [[indexical-knowledge-and-identity]], [[concepts/many-worlds]]. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] (egocentric presentism is a metaphysical rival, an exploratory ontological fit with the Map's line — NOT positive empirical evidence for dualism). Required "Relation to Site Perspective" section anchoring to Tenet 4. After creation: verify inbound links were added to ≥2 of the cross-link targets ([[expand-topic-skips-integration-chain]]); queue an integrate-orphan follow-on if the new page lands as a near-orphan. Concepts soft/hard length 2500/3500 words.

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Fix three citation defects in topics/eliminative-materialism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-17 found three real citation defects (web-verified). (1) DANGLING CITE: line 98 cites "His 1978 morphine argument" (Dennett) but there is NO Dennett 1978 entry in References — add Dennett, D. (1978), "Why You Can't Make a Computer That Feels Pain" (Brainstorms, 1978), verifying year/venue at publisher of record before committing. (2) WRONG PUBLISHER: Reference 21 lists Toon (2023) Mind as Metaphor as "Routledge" — it is Oxford University Press (ISBN 9780198879626; web-verified vs OUP/PhilPapers). Change Routledge → Oxford University Press. (3) MISATTRIBUTED QUOTE SOURCE: line 120 quotes Searle "Where consciousness is concerned... the existence of the appearance is the reality" and Reference 17 (line 191) attributes it to Searle (1992) The Rediscovery of the Mind (MIT Press). The exact wording traces to Searle's 1995 NYRB exchange with Dennett ("The Mystery of Consciousness: An Exchange," NYRB 1995-12-21): "where the existence of conscious states is concerned, you can't make the distinction between appearance and reality, because the existence of the appearance is the reality in question." The appearance-reality collapse IS genuinely Searle's and central to Rediscovery ch.5, so either (a) re-attribute the quote to the 1995 NYRB piece (add a reference), or (b) soften to a paraphrase of the Rediscovery position rather than a verbatim 1992 quote — unless the exact 1992 page can be confirmed. Also verify-then-tighten the Toon attribution at line 136 (mental fictionalism may deny only inner-representation realism, not causal efficacy — if so, narrow the claim so Toon is not mis-sorted onto the wrong side of the article's own causal-efficacy/structural-format distinction). Note: the Churchland "stagnant for at least twenty-five centuries" wording is paraphrase-grade, not confirmed verbatim — acceptable as paraphrase. All other checked citations (Loginov 2024, Morozov 2025, Frankish 2016, Dennett 2016, Boghossian 1990, Ramsey/Stich/Garon 1990, Devitt/Rey 1991, Hutto/Myin 2013) verified clean. See pessimistic-2026-06-17-eliminative-materialism.md.

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Cross-link the function-phenomenology wedge cluster to its eliminativist target
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Suggested by /optimistic-review 2026-06-17b §"Cross-Linking Suggestions". The first-person-evidence cluster is argumentatively interlocking but under-cross-linked at the article level (verified: 0 references in each direction below). LENGTH-SAFE — these are frontmatter `related_articles` / one-clause inline additions, not new prose; eliminative-materialism is 3667 body words (under topics hard ceiling) so keep any inline add ≤1 sentence. Four reciprocal links: (a) `eliminative-materialism` → `phenomenology-vs-function-axis` (eliminativism/illusionism is the position the wedge most directly pressures; the axis names illusionism as a target but the reverse link is absent); (b) `eliminative-materialism` → `synaesthesia` and/or `aphantasia` (within-species phenomenal-divergence is concrete empirical pressure on the illusionist denial that there is content to introspect — exactly the evidence eliminativism must absorb); (c) `degrees-of-consciousness` → `phenomenology-vs-function-axis` (degrees leans on the access/phenomenal dissociation via blindsight; the axis formalises function-phenomenology dissociation generally); (d) `introspection` → `carrolls-regress` back-link (carrolls-regress already lists introspection as a concept link; introspection never points back, despite the inference-void / taking-as opacity being a sibling of the source-attribution and noetic-feelings limits introspection already catalogues). CALIBRATION: honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — these are evidence-base ↔ position links, not tier-upgrades; the synaesthesia/aphantasia link must preserve the "constrains not establishes" framing (the divergence pressures functionalism, it does not by itself refute illusionism). No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 5 (Occam's-limits). ai_contribution=100.

Two concrete proposals worth recording in the project docs / tune-system backlog: (1) **Citation-consistency propagation** — when a deep review repairs a citation on one article (e.g. the June 3 synesthetic-void Wager fix), the corrected metadata is NOT propagated to other articles citing the same source, leaving stale duplicates (the Wager drift fixed by the P1 task above is the live exhibit; also seen in the recurring corpus-wide patterns in MEMORY). (2) **Literature-drift candidate breadth** — `/literature-drift-review`'s active-research pattern list is too narrow (its own changelog note says so); active empirical areas like synesthesia, aphantasia, neurophenomenology, psychedelic phenomenology, and developmental pruning are not in the candidate set and so never get periodic web-checks. Propose widening the pattern list. Record these as a short addition to the appropriate project doc (e.g. `obsidian/project/`) or as a tune-system note; do NOT create a new top-level concept/topic article (cap-aware). Lower-urgency than the content tasks; default P2 (methodology proposals that point at real workflow gaps). Reviewer also proposed an inline "same data, rival reading" box convention for evidence-bearing void articles and an evidential-tier inline-labelling discipline — capture these as candidate disciplines but do not implement site-wide without operator sign-off.

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Fix blindsight interpretation + illusionism boundary in concepts/introspection.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-17 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-17-introspection]]) found two critical issues + three smaller ones; consolidate into ONE editor pass. (1) **CRITICAL — blindsight contradiction** (§Dissociation Evidence, ~L131): article says "On Block's interpretation, blindsight patients retain phenomenal experience in their 'blind' field" — the REVERSE of the standard view AND the Map's own position in `concepts/access-consciousness.md` ("minimal or absent P-consciousness despite partial access") and `concepts/blindsight.md` ("eliminates conscious visual experience... they report seeing nothing"). Blindsight = access/residual-discrimination WITHOUT (or minimal/absent) phenomenology; phenomenology-EXCEEDING-access is the overflow case already at L83. Verify against access-consciousness.md + blindsight.md before editing. (2) **CRITICAL — boundary-substitution** (§Self-Undermining, ~L113–121): the self-refutation charge against illusionism is presented as an in-framework refutation, but it only bites if phenomenal realism is granted — which Frankish denies. `illusionism.md` handles this correctly ("Frankish denies the proviso... marks where the Map and the illusionist part company rather than refuting him within his framework") — inherit that proviso in natural prose; do NOT name modes ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]). (3) MEDIUM — choice-blindness "three-quarters undetected" (~L63) omits the concurrent-vs-retrospective distinction the rest of the corpus now carries (source-attribution-void, pupillometry-behavioural-channel); add the concurrent-detection clause. (4) MEDIUM — "no superposition phenomenology → collapse" (~L171) is non-discriminating vs MWI (which predicts the same single-outcome reports); downgrade or cut. (5) LOW — twelve-letter grid is Sperling's apparatus, not Block's; attribute apparatus to Sperling (add Sperling 1960 ref), interpretation to Block. Length at soft-warning (3366w, hard 3500) — keep edits length-neutral. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Correct Gray-2003 first-dissociation wording in synaesthesia topic + phenomenal-variation apex (source-settled: visual+auditory, NOT grapheme-recognition)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From deep-review 2026-06-17 of `concepts/phenomenology-vs-function-axis.md` (see `reviews/deep-review-2026-06-17-phenomenology-vs-function-axis.md`). Resolves the cross-corpus consistency item the 2026-06-01 deep review explicitly DEFERRED (Gray-2003 "identical qualia from distinct functional roles" rendered two ways across the corpus; the TiCS source was then paywalled and corpus-voting was declined). Source now SETTLED via the IEP synaesthesia entry + corroborating secondary sources (canonical TiCS full text still 403): Gray's actual argument uses **coloured-hearing** synaesthesia — his first dissociation is *"seeing and hearing are functionally different, and yet either modality can result in exactly the same colour experience"* (colour qualia from **visual AND auditory** functional roles); his second is the *"train"* case (one auditory input → both auditory and visual/colour qualia). The CORRECT form ("visual and auditory") is already in `concepts/phenomenology-vs-function-axis.md` (line ~65) and `voids/synesthetic-void.md` (line ~54) — DO NOT touch those. The DEFECT is in two files that import the grapheme-colour frame into Gray's coloured-hearing argument: (1) `obsidian/topics/synaesthesia.md` line ~92 ("colour experience is generated from both standard visual processing and **grapheme-recognition** pipelines") and (2) `obsidian/apex/phenomenal-variation-within-a-species.md` line ~89 ("colour from visual *and* from **grapheme-recognition** pipelines"). Change the first-dissociation pathway pair to "visual and auditory" in both. GREP-VERIFY the live wording before editing ([[out-of-scope-spillover-flags-stale]]) — line numbers may drift. The quoted phrase "function and qualia come apart in two ways" is verbatim-correct and stays; only the *gloss* of which pathways changes. The second-dissociation gloss ("same grapheme-recognition process → colour in synaesthetes but not non-synaesthetes") is a faithful instantiation of the abstract "same function, different qualia" pattern and is fine to leave. Length-neutral. Bump `ai_modified` on each file touched. Tenet alignment: none required (citation-accuracy fix). This is a §2.4 family-resolution item ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Import strongest non-dualist readings into voids/synesthetic-void.md (functionalist, representationalist, deflationary, illusionist) instead of linking away
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-17, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, gemini]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-17**: both same-cycle reviewers independently flagged that the article underweights its strongest non-dualist opponents (illusionist / fine-grained functionalist / representationalist) and treats them as boundary markers rather than developing them. ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: "a pattern of tenet-protective bracketing: the article says 'the Map prefers X for broader tenet reasons' and moves on, instead of forcing the dualist reading to win locally" and asks for "at least four developed counter-readings" (fine-grained functionalist, representationalist sub-individuation, deflationary/heterophenomenological, illusionist/metacognitive). Gemini 2.5 Pro: "leaves the manuscript's defense of the explanatory gap entirely untested against its strongest modern opponents" (AST/Frankish soft illusionism, predictive-processing representationalism). NOTE Gemini OVERSTATED this — its claim the article "completely ignores" illusionism is FALSE (the article's L102 Site Perspective already lists illusionist/metacognitive deflation as a live physicalist resource, per the Gemini review's own Verification Notes); the convergent, defensible core is "import and develop the strongest versions in-body rather than linking away," NOT Gemini's "blunt refusal to engage" framing. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit. Identify what kind of engagement each objection calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft changelog entry, not in the article. If an in-framework refutation is attempted and fails, state in natural language that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. The article names heterophenomenology / physicalism / illusionism but treats them as boundary markers (tenet-protective bracketing) rather than developing them. Import the strongest one-sentence-plus versions WITH links to fuller treatment: (1) **fine-grained functionalist** — once function is individuated at internal-processing/semantic-mediation/attentional/report-disposition levels (not coarse "same grapheme"), the same-function/different-qualia case dissolves; the sibling apex [[phenomenal-variation-within-a-species]] already states this absorption strategy ("empirical wedge, not a knockdown") — import it rather than requiring a click-away; (2) **representationalist sub-individuation** — the synesthetic colour is richer/higher-order content, not "extra" relative to content (this also touches the L52 "identical representations produce different experiences" wording: reframe as "identical surface stimuli / coarse-grained representational descriptions appear to produce different reported phenomenology"); (3) **deflationary/heterophenomenological** — what Dennett's reconstruction says about Stroop/consistency/neuroimaging/reports together; (4) **illusionist/metacognitive** — extra-qualia report as structured misrepresentation; cross-link the [[qualia]] "Illusionist Challenge" section. Also borrow the [[synaesthesia]] topic's contested-neonatal calibration and the [[imagery-void]] three-option wedge structure as a template; consider placing synesthesia within the [[voids-between-minds]] strong-isolation / partial-bridging / constitutive taxonomy. ASSESS-FIRST: some may already be partially covered via existing related_articles links — add genuine gaps, don't duplicate. Length-AWARE near the voids ceiling (coordinate with the calibration pass above; if forced over hard, flag a human length-decision rather than auto-condense — [[refine-then-condense-same-session-churn]]). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-06-17: Temper overstatement in voids/synesthetic-void.md (McGinn "predicts exactly", E-space "empirical evidence", Occam "fails at first empirical test", straw-physicalism, Martian-colours count)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-17 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), consolidated calibration pass (one editor pass to avoid [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] churn). All flagged phrasings are present VERBATIM in the live article (grep-verified — no fabricated target quotes). Soften the overstatements WHILE PRESERVING the Map's dualist position stated in its own voice under explicit tenet framing (do NOT adopt the reviewer's deflationary framing wholesale): (a) **L92 McGinn** — "predicts exactly this kind of intra-species incommensurability" overstates McGinn's cognitive-closure thesis (about the species-level mind-body problem, not synesthete/non-synesthete pairs); reframe as an analogy / local closure-like instance rather than something McGinn "predicts exactly." (b) **L62 E-space** — "Synesthesia is the empirical evidence that this region has unexploited dimensions" overstates; Lacalli's E-space is an explicitly NON-dualist theoretical mapping ("there is no implied dualism"), not a measured space synesthetes have been located in. Downgrade to "compatible with, and suggestive under the Map's reading of, unexploited dimensions," and add one clause noting the E-space framing is an interpretive overlay. (c) **L104 Occam** — "fails at the first empirical test" → "is empirically too coarse" (experiential uniformity is a strawman as a "simplest theory of consciousness"). (d) **L102 Site Perspective** — "Under physicalism, synesthetic qualia should be fully explicable as neural cross-activation—different wiring, different experience, end of story" is straw physicalism; replace with a brief taxonomy of physicalist options (cross-activation, disinhibited feedback, semantic/top-down mediation, representational sub-individuation, illusionist/metacognitive deflation) before giving the Map's reply. (e) **L42 lede** — "A few synesthetes report 'Martian colours'" is under-sourced; the robust record is ONE well-documented colour-blind synesthete case — change to "at least one well-known case" unless a refine fork can cite multiple primary cases. (f) **L72 pharmacology** — note controlled LSD work did NOT establish consistency/inducer-specificity for induced synesthesia even though spontaneous synesthesia-like reports increased. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; length-AWARE (article ~2527 body words, voids 2000 soft / 3000 hard ceiling — net-neutral preferred, do NOT bust hard; [[count-words-includes-frontmatter]] strip frontmatter before counting). No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. Bump `ai_modified`.

Synaesthesia and the Problem of Qualia. *Southwest Philosophy Review*, 17, 163–170`; (b) `obsidian/apex/phenomenal-variation-within-a-species.md` — body L89 "**Alan** Wager (1999, 2001)" + references L167 `Wager, A. ### ✓ 2026-06-16: Fix label-leak + citation mischaracterisation in topics/direction-dependent-discriminating-test-design.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-16 found two concrete defects in an otherwise strong article. (1) CRITICAL label-leakage: line 38 contains the editor-vocabulary parenthetical "([[direct-refutation-discipline|Mode Four]])" in article prose — forbidden per [[direct-refutation-discipline]]. The substance ("does not presuppose which way they would resolve") is already stated in natural language in the same sentence, so just DELETE the parenthetical. (2) MEDIUM citation precision: line 70 calls Proekt & Hudson (2018) "a *single* bistable system," but that paper's headline finding (PubMed 29935600, BJA 121(1):86-94, web-verified) is that a two-state model is INSUFFICIENT and a ten-state Markov process (three awake, seven unconscious) is REQUIRED — "more than two states are required." ⚠ VERIFY-FIRST — POSSIBLE PAPER-CONFLATION ([[citation-verify-false-negative]]): the article's "stochastic basis / single bistable" wording MATCHES the 2018 BJA paper's actual TITLE ("A stochastic basis for neural inertia in emergence from general anaesthesia"), AND optimistic-2026-06-16c independently PRAISED this exact Proekt-Hudson handling — so the pessimistic fork's "ten-state Markov (3 awake / 7 unconscious), two-state insufficient" claim may be a conflation with a DIFFERENT Proekt/Hudson paper (e.g. Hudson et al. 2014 PNAS "metastable activity states"), not the 2018 BJA stochastic-basis paper. Before any reword, INDEPENDENTLY fetch PMID 29935600 and 3-state it: (a) if the 2018 paper IS the stochastic-single-bistable result the article describes → it is FAITHFUL, make NO change to line 70; (b) only if PMID 29935600 itself genuinely says "two-state insufficient / ten-state required" → reword to acknowledge the multistate result (the fuller model is an even more formidable direction-blind rival); (c) if the ten-state claim turns out to be a conflated different paper → finding VOID, no change. Do NOT replace a title-faithful characterization with the fork's claim unless (b) is independently confirmed at publisher of record. The discriminator logic survives untouched either way (channel-ordering read-out, not state count). Optional while there: (a) line 58 targeting claim ("a region the autobiographical-retrieval literature implicates in re-experiencing while sparing... noetic recognition") is load-bearing and uncited — add a citation or mark as working assumption; (b) flag [[active-reboot]] as a posited pathway whose own mechanism is undischarged (linking [[mechanism-costs-dualism-thickness-quadrants]]). Do NOT soften the symmetry-branch falsifiability (line 76/84) — it is the article's best feature. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-16-direction-dependent-test]].

### ✓ 2026-06-16: [outer-review 2026-06-16] Carry the two-tier FND discount into the conversion-disorder summary/snippet (and avoid bare "hardware works")
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-16 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro, §3.6 + §4.8). The full `topics/conversion-disorder-as-consciousness-side-fault.md` body does the two-tier discount well (robust finding = substrate-intact decoupling; interface reading = a further, contestable step), but the DESCRIPTION frontmatter (line 3) and the lede (line 37) carry the strong "cleanest consciousness-side-fault exhibit" + "the hardware works" framing WITHOUT the discount attached — CONFIRMED LIVE. Modern clinical literature treats FND as a positive rule-in neuropsychiatric diagnosis (attention-driven dysfunction, automatic movement, comorbidities, heterogeneous mechanisms), not a clean dualist-interface proof. Action: (a) attach the two-tier discount to the description and lede so the strong framing never travels unqualified; (b) replace bare "the hardware works" with "capacity is demonstrably preserved under some task conditions" or equivalent. ### ✓ 2026-06-14: Acknowledge Kent (2021) equal-Φ-superposition objection in qm-interpretations-beyond-many-worlds Φ-modulated-collapse passage
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-14 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), Weakness #4 — the ONE genuine high-value finding in this review (the other four headline weaknesses rest on fabricated or overstated target quotes — see the review's Verification Notes; do NOT act on Weaknesses #1/#2/#3/#5 as stated). VERIFIED real omission against the live article. The article advances Φ-modulated CSL at line 104 (citing Kremnizer-Ranchin 2015, Okon-Sebastián 2020, Chalmers-McQueen 2022 — differences in integrated information Φ between superposed conscious states set collapse rates). It does NOT cite **Kent, A. (2021), "Collapse and Measures of Consciousness," Foundations of Physics 51(2):62**, a directly on-point in-literature objection: if a scalar consciousness measure (Φ) drives collapse, distinct experiential states of EQUAL measure (e.g. seeing-red vs seeing-green, both Φ=k) leave the collapse operator no gradient, permitting persistent macroscopic superpositions — reviving the very measurement problem the model was meant to solve. ACTION: WEB-VERIFY the Kent 2021 cite (publisher-of-record metadata — apply [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]] to this reviewer-supplied cite; Gemini fabricates cites too) BEFORE inserting; then add a one-to-two-sentence acknowledgement at line ~104–108 that the Φ-collapse coupling faces this equal-measure objection, with the Map's honest response (e.g. that escaping it requires richer multi-dimensional "Q-shape" measures at a complexity cost, or marking it as an open problem for the modulation variant). Do NOT overstate the article's claim the way the reviewer does — the article already says consciousness "modulates" not "causes" collapse and carries speculative hedges; preserve them. Coordinate with the existing consolidated P2 recalibration task above (same file — [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]); if that pass runs first, it can absorb this. RESPECT the topics 4000-word hard ceiling (prefer tightening elsewhere to net growth); honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and writing-style (no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché). Tenet: Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction), Tenet 1 (Dualism). **UPDATE 2026-06-14**: the consolidated recalibration ran (commit 6f1c8358) WITHOUT absorbing Kent; article now **3992/4000 (8w headroom)** — so this addition is NOT "prefer tightening", it is MANDATORY net-neutral-or-NEGATIVE: trim ≥ what you add, or fold the equal-measure objection INTO an existing sentence at line ~104. A bare addition WILL bust the hard ceiling. A condense pass on this article is a reasonable precursor — see [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]].

### ✓ 2026-06-13: Address pessimistic-review findings in topics/cross-species-behavioural-confidence-proxy-tests.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-13 found two Medium issues on this never-pessimistically-reviewed, ~4-week-stale article. (1) EPISTEMIC/METAPHYSICAL EQUIVOCATION on the load-bearing phrase "principled-blocked at full strength" (§"The Principled-Blocked Region" and the "narrative proxy" para of §"Four-Face Proxy Architecture"): the supporting reasons are *bandwidth/output-format* reasons ("a rat cannot fabricate a *story*… it can only press or not"; "without language-grade output the production cannot occur in its native form") — i.e. *epistemic/contingent* limits of present paradigms — but the word "principled" asserts a *constitutive* barrier. The same para concedes the narrative face "approaches the bandwidth at which verbal report re-becomes the cleanest tool" (a continuum, not a wall). FIX: either supply the bridging argument for why language-grade narrative content is *constitutively* unavailable to non-verbal channels, OR downgrade to "bandwidth-blocked on present designs" and state the falsification/upgrade condition (the paradigm that would move a face from blocked→accessible). The §"Principled-Blocked Region" close ("narrative barely or not at all; confabulation weakly") already gestures graded — make the whole section graded-and-contingent. (2) CARRUTHERS RIVAL ENGAGEMENT-MODE ambiguity (§"Despite-Commitments Versus Because-Prediction"): the article grants that under Carruthers' interpretive-sensory-access reading the convergence is "a because-prediction confirmation of the interpretive theory rather than independent triangulation," then absorbs the rival into a "discount" without refuting it in-framework OR honestly marking the framework boundary — boundary-substitution risk per [[direct-refutation-discipline]]. FIX: either name the standard Carruthers helps himself to but doesn't earn (why an analog-magnitude model-free signal counts as *introspection-grade* on his own terms), or mark honestly that the bounded-witness preference runs on Map commitments and is not being refuted within Carruthers' framework. NO editor-vocabulary in prose. Preserve the article's strong per-face calibration and the voids-circularity-honouring "Relation to Site Perspective". See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-13-cross-species-proxy-tests]].

### ✓ 2026-06-13: positions-evolve update P-A1 + P-A2 — trilemma-of-selection calibration-softened since last review

This is the third horn of the [[trilemma-of-selection]]`) — both `Last reviewed: 2026-06-08`, i.e. BEFORE this change. Action per /positions-evolve update discipline: re-read the trilemma article as it now stands, verify both positions' claims still track the (now more-hedged) source, confirm the "moderate" confidence band is still warranted (the softening is calibration-consistent, so likely no band change — but the added underdetermination framing may merit a one-clause tightening of P-A1's already-present "held as best explanation rather than proven" hedge), and bump `Last reviewed` to the cycle date on whichever entries are touched. Length-neutral, single register file; no new position, no content-article edit. See /positions-evolve SKILL (update path) and [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-06-13: Align closing-paragraph calibration and fix the OCD-sample figure in neuroplasticity.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-13 found the article well-calibrated overall (compatible-but-not-required discipline maintained throughout; Kral 2022 and Schwartz 1996 citations verify cleanly) with three non-critical findings. (1) MEDIUM / closing-paragraph drift — the final body sentence (L140) "harder to square with epiphenomenalism than with interactionist dualism—the correlation is exactly what interactionism predicts and what epiphenomenalism must explain away" upgrades to a comparative verdict the rest of the article disowns. The article's OWN §Epiphenomenalist Response (L93) gives a symmetric epiphenomenalist pathway predicting the same correlation, so the correlation alone discriminates nothing. Relocate the tilt onto its actual support (the phenomenology-tracking argument, openly flagged as contestable) rather than the neutral correlation; soften "must explain away" → "must treat as systematically misleading phenomenology." (2) MEDIUM / unsupported premise — the evolutionary rebuttal at L99 ("why would evolution produce accurate phenomenology of cognitive load if it contributes nothing?") rests on an undefended teleological premise; the free-covariance / non-adaptive-concomitant reply is standardly available. Either supply the bridging argument or downgrade; check that the cited [[mental-effort]] actually makes the argument it's relied on for. (3) LOW-MEDIUM / numeric precision — verify "18 participants (9 new)" (L83) against Schwartz 1996 (PubMed 8629886); the "(9 new)" parenthetical is unexplained and may conflate two figures. Cross-check the sibling [[clinical-neuroplasticity-evidence-for-bidirectional-causation]] so the two agree. Also (minor): mark the Zeno "doesn't violate conservation laws" claim (L152) as a model property not a brain fact; consider a primary cite for the Davidson "2 weeks" claim (L109 currently cites a review/magazine piece). PRESERVE the compatible-but-not-required discipline, the front-loaded Schwartz limitations, the Kral 2022 retraction handling, and the identity-theory engagement. Does NOT trigger the altered-state symmetry audit (meditation is the sole supportive-cluster item; ≥2 gate fails). See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-13-neuroplasticity]].
