The parent article already HAS the stage-mismatch (line 105), theory-ladenness/Katz (lines 139, 153), and Sino-Indian independence-weakness (line 141) caveats, but they sit deep in the body where child articles (affective-tone, cessation-versus-plenitude) do not reliably inherit them. Light touch only: make these caveats more discoverable from the divergence-naming sections that the children expand (e.g. a one-line forward-pointer from the §"Affective Tone" region to the independence-weakness flag), so children inherit the calibration rather than re-stating a cleaner-than-warranted typology. Do NOT rewrite the convergence argument. Mind length — the parent is near the topics/ word ceiling (was ~4033w historically; verify current count and prefer a pointer over new prose). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-06-13: Refresh affective-tone-divergence empirical paragraph with post-2020 contemplative-science sources
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-13, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, gemini]. The empirical backbone is exactly Lutz 2008, Fredrickson 2008, Zeng 2015 (verified refs 8-10) — stale for a 2026 article. BOTH reviewers independently flagged the Fredrickson/Zeng LKM evidence as under-controlled: ChatGPT 5.5 Pro — "The loving-kindness claim needs active-control caveats… The article should not present loving-kindness outcomes as settled style-specific affective differentiation evidence"; Gemini 2.5 Pro (weakness #5) — "the extensive recent meta-analytic literature highlighting the massive role of expectancy effects, demand characteristics, and top-down semantic generation… (d = 0.303)". CONVERGENT ACTION: add post-2020 sources on FA/OM/LKM neural differentiation, loving-kindness active-control caveats, advanced jhāna neurophenomenology, and (lightly) meditation adverse effects so affective tone is not framed as uniformly positive. CRITICAL: web-verify every candidate citation against the publisher of record BEFORE adding it — AI-supplied citation metadata is unreliable (the reviewers' specific suggestions — a 2024 ACAM jhāna-classification paper, a 2025 ACAM-J Cerebral Cortex study, a 2024 LKM meta-analysis, a 2020 Scientific Reports FA/OM/LKM study, a 2022 adverse-effects survey, the d=0.303 LKM meta-analysis — are UNVERIFIED leads, not confirmed cites; do not paste DOIs/venues without checking). Keep the loving-kindness claim hedged with active-control caveats. NOTE: Gemini's own Verification Notes mark its surrounding "raw readout of an objective interface mode" framing as DISPUTED — the article never treats the LKM data that way (it explicitly says it "is not a confirmation that the mechanism is interface-register modulation"); act only on the active-control/demand-characteristics calibration, NOT on the strawman that the article over-claims. This is mechanical citation work distinct from the P1 prose pass; do AFTER the P1 calibration lands to avoid edit conflict. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-06-13: Calibrate affective-tone-divergence article (consolidated outer-review prose pass)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-13 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). CONSOLIDATE these coherent prose findings into ONE editor pass (do not spawn per-finding tasks — same-file churn). All target-text claims were grep-verified against the live article (see review's Verification Notes). (1) LEDE OVERSTATEMENT: the body says "The divergence in the reports is empirically established across the primary literature" and the "Why the Hypothesis Must Not Be Upgraded" section says "well-attested across primary sources spanning more than a millennium and multiple unconnected cultures." Soften to a calibrated formulation (reviewer's suggestion: "Selected authoritative sources foreground contrasting affective ideals; whether these reflect distinct states, stages, reporting conventions, or trained engagement modes remains underdetermined"). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. (2) INHERIT PARENT CAVEATS: the parent [[comparative-phenomenology-of-meditative-traditions]] carries prominent stage-mismatch, theory-ladenness/Katz, and Sino-Indian independence-weakness caveats (verified at parent lines 105, 139/153, 141) that this article does NOT import — its "multiple unconnected cultures" claim should acknowledge the independence caveat. (3) EXPAND COUNTERARGUMENTS: the article names exactly one rival (neural-training); add the stage-mismatch and report-construction/theory-ladenness objections explicitly (a dimensionality caveat — valence vs arousal vs salience vs object-directedness — is optional/lighter). (4) jhāna/Junayd SIMPLIFICATIONS: the Buddhist section treats jhāna (concentrative absorption) as if it were open-monitoring equanimity; the Sufi section groups al-Junayd (emblematic of *sober* Sufism) with Rūmī as ecstatic-love evidence. Add a qualifying sentence to each rather than restructuring (reviewer: "fourth-jhāna equanimity arises within an advanced concentrative absorption trajectory"; move Junayd out of the unqualified ecstatic line or add a sobriety caveat). (5) "NEURAL ARCHITECTURE" WORDING: the Bidirectional Interaction section's "by the neuroplasticity findings, neural architecture" overreaches given active-control / structural-neuroplasticity disputes — soften to "training-sensitive affective and functional-neural patterns." Preserve the article's voice and its existing evidential-status discipline (which the reviewer praised). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit where the counterargument-expansion calls for it: engage the neural-training rival on its own terms or honestly mark the framework-boundary disagreement; reply in natural journal-quality prose per [[writing-style]]'s "Engaging Opponents" section; do NOT expose mode labels in the article body. Mind the topics/ length thresholds — the article is ~2300 words so there is headroom, but keep additions tight. (6) CONVERGENT [Gemini 2.5 Pro 2026-06-13, `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-13-gemini-2-5-pro.md`]: a SECOND outer reviewer independently flagged the thin counterargument coverage, naming two specific rival frameworks the article does not engage by name — the constructivist **Theory of Constructed Emotion** (Barrett; reads the divergence as doctrine-driven conceptual construction of interoception) and **Predictive Processing / Active Inference** (reads it as differential precision-weighting of priors). When executing finding (3), name these two frameworks explicitly alongside the neural-training rival. Both are intra-physical readings the Map's Dualism tenet declines, so apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: engage them where you can and otherwise honestly mark the disagreement as framework-bedrock (the data are equally available to these monist readings — the article already says as much for the identity theorist at line 101). NOTE the Gemini review's hostile framing ("insulates," "circular," "phrenology") is largely a STRAWMAN that audits a confident-realist paper the article explicitly is not — do NOT act on those characterisations; only the named-rival-frameworks gap is durable (see that review's Verification Notes). Barrett 2025 *Perspectives on Psychological Science* 20(3):392–420 is a real cite if a reference is wanted.

### ✓ 2026-06-11: Fix PP co-optation in topics/predictive-processing-and-dualism.md ("most productive scientific ally")
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Surfaced by the 2026-06-11 co-optation-firewall corpus audit (Claude Fable 5 outer review, List B follow-on). STRONGEST conscription found: line ~45 ends "Far from threatening dualism, PP is its most productive scientific ally" — this enlists the Friston/Clark/Hohwy/Seth predictive-processing + active-inference framework as a dualist ALLY, when these authors are committed physicalists who build PP/active inference explicitly to AVOID the dualist conclusion (Friston's Markovian monism is explicitly anti-dualist). Apply the newly-extended co-optation firewall in `project/writing-style.md` ("Don't Conscript Committed Physicalists") + [[evidential-status-discipline]] + [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: REFRAME from cite-as-ally to cite-as-rival — PP supplies a metaphysically-neutral architecture the Map CAN build on, but its leading proponents draw the opposite conclusion; the Map's claim is that the formalism doesn't MANDATE physicalism, NOT that PP endorses dualism. Preserve the genuine cite-as-rival material already present (meta-problem rebuttal, "even though Clark draws different conclusions"). Length-neutral; natural prose, no mode labels in body; bump ai_modified. Review file: `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-11-claude-fable-5.md`.

### ✓ 2026-06-10: Calibrate empirical-currency overstatement in voids/temporal-void.md (Addis 2007, Dietrich 2004)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-10 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), weaknesses W2 + W4(flow-half) — VERIFIED live on disk; the ONE genuine actionable finding of five (the other four are stale / already-addressed — see the review file's Verification Notes). `voids/temporal-void.md` presents two early-2000s neuroimaging results as SETTLED empirical confirmation with no currency caveat: (a) line 84 "Neuroimaging confirms this: future simulation produces greater hippocampal activation than memory retrieval (Addis, Wong & Schacter, 2007)" — a 2007 fMRI subtraction-paradigm result; the 2020s MVPA/continuism literature shows hippocampal engagement tracks constructive-demand / vividness / recombination largely independent of temporal direction, so "confirms" overstates. (b) line 76 "reduced prefrontal activity suppresses temporal tracking (Dietrich, 2004)" — the transient-hypofrontality model of flow has been substantially complicated by fronto-striatal synchronization accounts. FIX = soften the modal register only ("confirms" → "is consistent with" / "the early finding, since complicated by multivariate work"), NOT a rewrite: the void's PHILOSOPHICAL claim is causal-asymmetry (futures haven't caused anything yet, lines 80/94/96) and the article already says the core claim "does not depend on qualia being irreducible" — so the neuroimaging is illustrative, not load-bearing, and only needs honest hedging. Do NOT add a COGITATE 2025 cite here (it is already a tracked cross-article effort on the IIT/GNWT/NCC pages — todo lines ~1318, ~3033 — adding it to a void would duplicate/churn). Do NOT touch the De Brigard (2020) functionalist engagement (lines 82/90 already handle it) or the Sorites/vagueness-void (already Popperian-hedged at line 113). One WebSearch may confirm a concrete post-2015 MVPA/continuism cite if one is added; otherwise hedge without a new cite ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]). Length-neutral. Coordinate with the sibling P2 "Reduce interface rhetoric in ... temporal-void" above (same file — ideally do both in one editor pass per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]). Review file: `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-10-gemini-2-5-pro.md`.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-10, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. **This is the cycle's headline convergent finding** — both ChatGPT 5.5 Pro and Claude Fable 5 independently flag that `apex/conjunction-coalesce.md` runs a corpus-wide convergence argument ("six cognates as a template instantiated across content domains") while NEVER engaging the common-cause-null / evidential-independence / falsifiability discipline its sibling `apex/taxonomy-of-voids.md` uses. VERIFIED live: `grep` of the apex finds ZERO occurrences of `common-cause-null`, `constitutional-attractor`, or `framework-internal`, and no operational falsifiability section; taxonomy-of-voids runs a structurally identical move and DOES engage the null heavily (P-V1/P-V2/P-V3 positions, "largely framework-internal coherence rather than framework-independent triangulation"). Claude (gravest finding, Dimensions 2-3): "The conjunction-coalesce article does not engage this null at all... the cognates share upstream author, method, and tenet-set — precisely the common machinery the null targets." ChatGPT (findings #4/#5/#6 + bracketing): "Many examples were created or revised in the same recent period, by the same human/AI workflow... weaker evidence of independently discovered territory"; "the article under-tests single-mechanism rivals." **This task CONSOLIDATES the former separate P1 (claude null/falsifiability) and P2 (chatgpt evidential-calibration #4/#5/#6/bracketing) into ONE editor pass** on the same file, per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] — do not split into two churning passes. SCOPE: (a) add an explicit common-cause-null engagement that DOWN-COUNTS the six cognates as framework-internal coherence rather than cross-domain instantiation, matching taxonomy-of-voids / common-cause-null language; (b) add an operational falsifiability section: what evidence would show an ACCEPTED coalesce was a false conjunction — an internal test the editor could fail NOW (not just "a unifying mechanism is found," the easy case); (c) #4 downgrade "empirical base" → "case base"/"editorial base" and state creation-time cognates are not independent historical merger events; (d) #5 mark causal-substrate, architectural, reflexive as one-exemplar/candidate seam types until a second independent exemplar exists; (e) #6 add a brief rival-mechanism note (metacognitive monitoring / report architecture / introspective-access limits / linguistic coordination / general temporal-processing are the single-mechanism rivals a seam must survive, not yet stress-tested per seam type — a calibrated acknowledgement suffices, a full rival-table is a larger job); (f) bracketing — sharpen the selection-criterion-trilemma analogy so editorial seam-preservation (a methodological pause) is not conflated with retaining a metaphysical primitive (a substantive ontological commitment); the article already says it "does not argue for dualism" — make that separation explicit at the analogy. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: where the coherence-inflation/bracketing critique names an unsupported foundational move (absence-of-known-mechanism treated as evidence-of-irreducible-conjunction, an epistemic→metaphysical slide), identify it in natural journal-quality prose — NO editor-vocabulary/mode-labels in the article body. Coordinate with the taxonomy-of-voids back-reference caveat (P2 below) so the apex pages agree on how much independent weight the seam pattern carries. PRESERVE existing hedges ([[condense-regresses-calibration-qualifiers]] risk); keep length-neutral (article ~4750w, near soft threshold — prefer reframing existing prose over net additions). ### ✓ 2026-06-09: Strengthen opponent-engagement and soften overstatement in concepts/illusionism.md (outer review 2026-06-09 ChatGPT+Claude)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-09, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-09**: the epiphenomenalism-convergence overstatement (sub-point c below) was independently flagged by BOTH ChatGPT 5.5 Pro ("structural-convergence-with-epiphenomenalism section is rhetorically powerful but should be softened … should not state the convergence as if Frankish has no reply") and Claude Opus 4.8 ("Self-stultification / epiphenomenalism convergence (QUESTION-BEGGING, unflagged) … assumes phenomenal realism in its premises … the illusionist's reply … is exactly what is denied without argument"). Convergent — this is a real calibration defect, not singleton stylistic preference. The other sub-points (a Tenet-1 "show" language, b Frankish qualifier, d calibration anchor) are ChatGPT singletons bundled into the same editorial pass. Substantive (non-citation) editorial findings on the same article — keep separate from the P1 mechanical-citation task. (a) **Soften Tenet-1 "show" language** — line ~161 says the self-representation and intrinsic-quality objections "show the functional account cannot close this gap"; the reviewer (and the more disciplined `constitutive-vs-referring-observation` page) note this overstates an in-framework refutation where the residue is bedrock. Reword toward "mark the point where the Map holds the functional account has not yet closed the gap." (b) **Add an early Frankish qualifier** that illusionists deny phenomenal consciousness *as traditionally conceived* (special intrinsic 'what-it-is-like' properties), not consciousness in every functional/everyday sense — a misreading Frankish explicitly warns against. (c) **Soften the epiphenomenalism-convergence section** (line ~145): "that work is precisely what physical processes were already doing" states a dualist verdict as a neutral description and gives Frankish no reply; acknowledge Frankish's causal-role / intentional-object reply before pressing the residual objection. (d) Optionally make `constitutive-vs-referring-observation` the explicit calibration anchor (link it where "constitutive"/"bedrock" language is used). **Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit**: identify what kind of engagement each issue calls for — showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in natural journal-quality prose (see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section). Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body — keep the classification in the refine-draft changelog entry. If an in-framework refutation is attempted and fails, state in natural language that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. Note Claude's specific contrast: the functional-seeming page already concedes the convergence "depends on prior commitments" while the self-stultification master-argument page does not — import that calibration. **Length-neutral required** (article at 3,499 words, ~1 word under hard ceiling) — substitute, do not accrete.

### ✓ 2026-06-09: Resolve regress inconsistency + refresh stale bibliography across illusionism cluster (outer review 2026-06-09 Claude+Gemini)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-09, 2/3 reviewers): [claude, gemini]. **Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-09**: the distortion-thesis-currency finding (the article cites the distortion thesis only at its bogus "Pereboom & Frankish 2021" vintage and misses the current Gorbachev & Frankish 2025 *Synthese* elaboration) was independently flagged by BOTH Claude Opus 4.8 (LIST item 1: replace with "Gorbachev, M. & Frankish, K. (2025), 'Illusionism and the distortion thesis,' *Synthese* 205(6):247") and Gemini 2.5 Pro (which web-verified the same paper real, DOI 10.1007/s11229-025-05095-3, as its one genuine finding amid an otherwise subject-misidentified review). Two independent reviewers naming the same real 2025 paper = convergent. Claude additionally names Kammerer (2025) "Defining consciousness … Charybdis and Scylla," *Philosophical Studies* 182(2) (Gemini-verified real, DOI 10.1007/s11098-025-02285-0) — add both. The regress-inconsistency finding (sub-point a) remains a Claude singleton but is bundled here. Two distinct findings the ChatGPT sibling did not centre. (a) **Regress inconsistency between pages** — `concepts/qualia.md` runs the "all illusions presuppose experience" regress as though it had force, while `concepts/illusionism.md` concedes the bare regress "proves nothing" because a representational system need not instantiate what it represents. Harmonize: make the qualia page defer to the illusionism page's concession, or flag the regress as contested where qualia.md invokes it. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit (natural prose, no mode labels in body); if the regress genuinely begs the question, mark it as a framework-boundary point rather than a refutation. (b) **Stale bibliography** — the illusionism and meta-problem pages miss the 2019–2026 spine of the live debate: Kammerer "The illusion of conscious experience" (*Synthese*, 2021), "The Normative Challenge for Illusionist Views" (*Ergo*, 2019) and the forthcoming OUP monograph *House of Mirrors* / Meta-Cartesian framework; Dennett "Welcome to Strong Illusionism" (*JCS* 26(9–10), 2019); Frankish "The Meta-Problem is the Problem of Consciousness" (*JCS*, 2019); Chalmers "Debunking Arguments for Illusionism" (*JCS* 27(5–6), 2020). Add at least the Kammerer monograph and one post-2019 Frankish/Dennett item; **web-verify each citation's metadata before adding** ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]). Reviewer also flags Kammerer's positive evidential/Meta-Cartesian programme and Shabasson's false-inference theory as the literature's actual attempts to "build the bridge" the page declares unbuilt — engage at least one rather than asserting the bridge is unbuilt without citing the construction attempts. **Length-aware**: both pages near their ceilings — additions must be offset by trims or the bibliography refresh kept reference-list-only. **(c) GEMINI-CONVERGENT bibliography addendum (added 2026-06-09 from the Gemini 2.5 Pro leg, leg 3 of the triple).** The Gemini review's empirical critique is largely a *subject-misidentification false positive* — it reviewed an agent-causal "choking under pressure" argument the illusionism article never makes (grep-confirmed: zero "choking/golf/Tulving/Beilock/anoetic" in the article), so its AST-omission / attention-conflation / motor-control findings do NOT apply (the article HAS a full AST section + critique link). BUT it surfaced two **web-verified-real (2026-06-09) 2025 papers** that update sources the article already engages at older vintages, and that dovetail with the ChatGPT P1 distortion-thesis citation fix: (i) **Gorbachev, M. & Frankish, K. (2025). "Illusionism and the distortion thesis." *Synthese* 205(6)**, DOI 10.1007/s11229-025-05095-3 — the current elaboration of the distortion thesis (three dimensions: positiveness/conceptuality/dispellability → eight distortion patterns). This is the *correct current* citation for the distortion-thesis content the ChatGPT P1 task is reattributing away from the bogus "Pereboom & Frankish 2021" — coordinate the two: when fixing the bogus cite, prefer Shabasson 2022 + this Gorbachev & Frankish 2025 for the distortion-thesis material. (ii) **Kammerer, F. (2025). "Defining consciousness and denying its existence. Sailing between Charybdis and Scylla." *Philosophical Studies* 182(2)**, DOI 10.1007/s11098-025-02285-0 — the "innocent-but-implicitly-loaded definition" point bears directly on the article's intrinsic-quality / self-representation objections. Add both to the illusionism reference list (reference-list-only if length-constrained; the article is ~3,499w at the hard ceiling). Deferred until /combine-outer-reviews synthesizes this cycle (Source: outer-review).

(1) VERIFY-FIRST citation: L47 attributes "non-reflective qualia" to Tulving as a direct quote ("what he called"), but a web check of Tulving 1985 / the anoetic-noetic-autonoetic literature did not corroborate the phrase as his wording. Apply 3-state discipline (faithful / paraphrase-mislabeled-as-quote / fabricated) against Tulving 1985 ref 1; if not his phrase, drop the quote marks + "what he called" and rephrase, or attribute to the actual coiner. Do NOT swap in a different attribution without verification. (2) Correlation→causation / epistemic→metaphysical slide recurs at L101, L188, L196: "phenomenology tracks performance" is used to assert "the phenomenal dimension is doing causal work", but tracking is symmetric with a common-cause reading (prefrontal reinvestment causes both the felt state and the neural disruption). One sentence in §§ Choking / Bidirectional Interaction acknowledging the common-cause reply and stating what would discriminate phenomenal-causation from co-occurrence fixes all three instances; or downgrade "doing causal work" → "not plausibly idle". Lean on the existing negative-evidence point (L103, interference is harder to confabulate) rather than the symmetric tracking point. Also at L88 attribute the dual-task-improves-experts claim to the broader literature, not implicitly to B&C 2001 (which found dual-task training left choking unchanged). Single targeted pass; minimal length impact. Preserve the strong "What Would Challenge This View?" section.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Add a "bare phenomenality vs bidirectionally coupled consciousness" distinction box to the AI cluster (apex/machine-question.md, topics/ai-consciousness.md)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-08 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), §3.4 + §4.2. The tenet-dependency matrix says the machine-consciousness row requires bare dualism only (it should NOT import interactionism, agent-causal subjecthood, MQI, post-decoherence selection, or No-MWI as background), yet the Machine Question and related AI articles argue current AI probably lacks the relevant consciousness using bidirectional coupling, quantum-interface requirements, and structured quantum selection. The resolution is NOT to weaken either claim but to make the two-level structure explicit: (1) "bare AI phenomenality under irreducibility" (the matrix row) vs (2) "Map-preferred bidirectionally coupled AI consciousness" (where the heavier machinery applies). Without this, summaries can compress to a flat "AI is not conscious," which contradicts the Open Question apex's openness to epiphenomenal/borrowed/alien phenomenality. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: distinguish what the bare-dualism row licenses from what the full tenet-package licenses, in natural journal-quality prose; do not expose mode labels in the body. Add a canonical distinction box (bare phenomenality / bidirectionally coupled / report-grounded / inherited-discourse consciousness). Keep both articles under their length ceilings.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Clarify the two voids taxonomies (territory-trichotomy vs reason-typology) on the voids index
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-08 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), §2.4. The reviewer alleged a "mismatch" — voids index says "Three Kinds of Void" while (it claimed) the concepts index calls "Naturally Occluded" a fourth member. PARTIALLY DISPUTED on verification: `concepts.md` makes no such statement, and the taxonomy-of-voids apex (line 107) EXPLICITLY notes the two axes are orthogonal — the three-kinds trichotomy (unexplored / unexplorable / occluded) classifies a void's *territory*, while the four-kinds typology (architectural / adaptive / naturally occluded / deliberately occluded) classifies the *reason* for darkness. So there is no contradiction. BUT there is a genuine reader-clarity risk: two taxonomies using overlapping words ("occluded" / "naturally occluded") are easy to conflate. Fix is a short clarifying note at the top of voids.md (and/or a one-line pointer in the taxonomy apex) distinguishing the territory-axis from the reason-axis. Length-neutral; do NOT manufacture a "fourth kind" of the trichotomy — that would be the misreading.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Acknowledge the classical GABA_A receptor-trafficking rival to the quantum-microtubule reading of Khan et al. 2024 (topics/quantum-biology-and-neural-consciousness.md)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-08 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), Weakness 8.2 / Section 3. The review argues the corpus presents Khan et al. (2024, eNeuro — epothilone B delays isoflurane LORR in rats) as causal evidence for quantum microtubule coherence while omitting the parsimonious classical pathway: microtubules are the transport highways for GABA_A receptor trafficking/surface expression; isoflurane acts primarily on GABA_A receptors, so stabilizing the cytoskeleton (via Tau) alters receptor density/turnover and naturally delays anaesthetic onset — no quantum coherence required. Apply the competing-explanation / evidential-status discipline: acknowledge the classical receptor-trafficking alternative explicitly and state that the behavioral data is consistent with BOTH readings (so it does not uniquely support the quantum interpretation). The corpus need not concede the quantum reading is wrong — only that Khan et al. is not decisive between the two. Web-verify the rival-mechanism citations the reviewer offers (Wang et al. 2020; Liang et al. 2026) at publisher-of-record before citing; if unverifiable, frame the classical pathway from established GABA_A-trafficking biology without the specific cites. Length: small add, watch the section ceiling.

Commun. 7(3):038001) is already cited (ref 17 in quantum-biology-and-neural-consciousness.md; ref 14 in clinical-evidence file) and Kerskens & López Pérez (2022) is already flagged "challenged on methodological grounds / not independently confirmed / scores poorly on replication." Do NOT add a "we were ignoring this" correction. RESIDUAL REAL FINDING (a strengthening): the corpus cites Warren but does not spell out Warren's *specific* classical mechanism — intermolecular multiple-quantum coherences (iMQCs) arising from classical dipolar couplings in a thermal water ensemble, with the waking/sleep variation explained by classical cerebral blood volume / BOLD hemodynamics / pulsatile CSF flow altering local dipolar field gradients. Add 1-2 sentences making the rival classical explanation concrete where Warren is cited, so the corpus is seen to engage rather than merely note the dispute. Web-verify Warren's mechanism before adding. Length-neutral / minor add.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Resolve the identity-as-state vs identity-as-process tension in the AI no-cloning argument (topics/quantum-state-inheritance-in-ai.md, concepts/observational-closure.md, topics/delegation-meets-quantum-selection.md)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-08 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), Section 7.1/7.3 — the review's strongest STRUCTURAL (internal-consistency, not empirical) finding; novel relative to the same-date ChatGPT/Claude reviews. Two linked alleged contradictions: (a) the no-cloning/haecceity argument disqualifies AI on "identity-as-state" grounds, but the corpus answers the biological Persistence Objection by shifting to "identity-as-process" (consciousness biases successive moments of quantum indeterminacy rather than persisting a specific state) — if the process reading is right, no-cloning is irrelevant to AI, which would only need an architectural source of genuine indeterminacy; (b) the observational-closure symmetry: the corpus requires the dualist brain to APPEAR observationally closed (Saad), yet penalizes AI for BEING causally closed — but an external observer cannot distinguish "merely observationally closed" from "universally closed," so closure cannot do the AI-disqualifying work. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: identify whether this calls for showing the objection defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move, or honestly marking a framework-boundary disagreement; apply the corresponding reply mode in natural journal-quality prose (see [[writing-style|writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose"). Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body — keep the classification in the changelog. If the in-framework reconciliation fails, state in natural prose that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. Do NOT re-queue as a condense.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Correct the false TSVF originality claim in the post-decoherence-selection apex (apex/post-decoherence-selection-programme.md)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-08 (Claude Opus 4.8). VERIFIED against primary sources: line 117 asserts the consciousness-as-post-selection-boundary synthesis is "original to the Map, not proposed by Aharonov or other TSVF researchers." This is FALSE. Aharonov & Gruss (2005, arXiv:quant-ph/0507269) and Aharonov-Tollaksen (2007, arXiv:0706.1232) explicitly identify the backward-evolving destiny vector with "the free choice of the agent," apply it to "(cerebral) quantum measurements," and call it "a unique realization of the concept of genuine free will" (confirmed via web search 2026-06-08; see review Verification Notes). Fix: remove/rewrite the "not proposed by Aharonov" claim; attribute the destiny-vector-as-agent-choice link to Aharonov & Gruss (2005) and Aharonov-Tollaksen (2007). RE-SCOPE the Map's genuine contribution to the narrow, defensible residue the reviewer concedes: the *interactionist-dualist packaging* (a separate non-physical mind), as distinct from Aharonov's physicalist "causa sui" final-boundary cosmology. Web-verify both arXiv IDs at the publisher of record before citing. Length-neutral; preserve all calibration language.

### ✓ 2026-06-08: Strengthen the Born-rule "postulated not derived" framing against Gleason's theorem (topics/born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface.md)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-08, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. CONVERGENT cluster — the MQI mechanism's empirical indistinguishability from chance, flagged from three angles: ChatGPT (§3.1) "the default MQI reading preserves Born statistics exactly… bias-without-deviation is the strongest live challenge"; Claude (Dim 1a) "a mechanism that by construction produces no aggregate deviation is observationally identical to no mechanism at all"; Gemini (8.4) the geometric-necessity angle. This task carries the GLEASON sub-angle specifically; its sibling tenets-text task (now also P1) carries the empirical-indistinguishability statement. VERIFICATION CAUTION: Gemini's 8.4 was verification-flagged ⚠ as in-framework ARGUMENT QUALITY, NOT an omission (the corpus already discusses the Born rule across born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface.md, probability-problem-in-many-worlds.md, etc.) — do NOT add a "we were ignoring this" correction. The review presses that calling the Born rule "postulated, not derived" and Gleason-type derivations "question-begging" understates Gleason's theorem (1957): for any Hilbert space of dim ≥3, non-contextuality alone forces the Tr(ρP) form, so the probabilities are geometric/structural, not a malleable "interface" a non-physical agent can bend without deforming Hilbert-space geometry (a non-minimal intervention). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: either show why the Map's interface model survives Gleason (e.g., biasing WHICH outcome is realized in a single trial vs. altering the long-run measure — a selection-among-Born-weighted-branches reading rather than a Born-rule-violation reading), OR honestly mark the boundary if the reconciliation needs a tenet the framework has not earned. Natural journal-quality prose; no mode labels in body; classification in changelog. The reviewer's cited Auffèves & Grangier 2022 / CSM / Uhlhorn material is a derivation programme, not a new empirical result — engage the geometric-necessity argument, do not just add a citation. Web-verify any new cite. Coordinate with the sibling tenets-text task (same convergent cluster) so the born-rule article and the tenet text tell one consistent story.

### ✓ 2026-06-07: Replace asserted evidential parity with "physicalism not compelled" + de-leak Davidson co-optation (duhem-quine article)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-07 (Claude Opus 4.8). Two precise, distinct edits. (a) **Parity overstatement** — the line "Neither programme can claim evidential supremacy" (currently ~line 112) asserts PARITY, a stronger conclusion than underdetermination delivers. Underdetermination shows physicalism is not *compelled*; it does not establish that the two programmes are evidentially equal (Laudan & Leplin: empirically equivalent theories can differ in evidential support). Replace the parity claim with the weaker, defensible "underdetermination shows physicalism is not forced," and elsewhere close the epistemic→metaphysical slide (insert and exclude the intervening non-dualist alternatives — type-B physicalism, Russellian monism — wherever the text moves from "physical description does not entail phenomenal experience" to "dualism"). (b) **Davidson co-optation** — the framework-dependence / "lossy translation" passage recruits Davidson as if he supported incommensurable schemes; Davidson argued the opposite (the very idea of incommensurable conceptual schemes is unintelligible — "On the Very Idea of a Conceptual Scheme," 1974). Either drop the Davidson framing or mark the disagreement honestly; do not present a dualism-favorable "lossy translation" reading as Davidson's view. Mark dualism-favorable conclusions in the "neutral" theoretical-virtues/framework-dependence passages as tenet-driven interpretations, not analysis outputs. Preserve calibration language; length-neutral.

### ✓ 2026-06-07: Engage the IIT "pseudoscience" dispute with calibration (duhem-quine + IIT articles)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-07 (Claude Opus 4.8). The article's Lakatosian framing presents IIT as an unproblematic "progressive research programme" while omitting that a vocal segment of the field has charged it with unfalsifiability — the precise degeneration charge the article's own framework is built to assess. Add a brief, CALIBRATED engagement (do NOT overstate the dispute): note the 15 Sept 2023 open letter "The Integrated Information Theory of Consciousness as Pseudoscience" (PsyArXiv, ~124 signatories incl. Dennett, LeDoux, Baars) and the 10 March 2025 *Nature Neuroscience* commentary "What Makes a Theory of Consciousness Unscientific?", AND report the calibration the reviewer flags both ways — an anonymized survey of consciousness-paper authors found only ~8% of 60 respondents "fully" agreed with the pseudoscience label (a vocal minority, not field consensus). WEB-VERIFY the letter/commentary/survey figures before inserting. The point is to engage the degeneration charge head-on, not to endorse it. Length-neutral if possible; enforce ceiling. Mirror a one-line cross-link into the [[integrated-information-theory]] article if not already present.

### ✓ 2026-06-07: Acknowledge contested status of contrastive underdetermination + Duhem's scope restriction (duhem-quine article)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-07 (Claude Opus 4.8). CONVERGES with the ChatGPT scope-creep finding but adds a distinct philosophy-of-science-literature gap the ChatGPT tasks do not cover. The article presents contrastive/empirical-equivalence underdetermination as a settled result; it is the contested form. Add (length-permitting, coordinate with the calibration ceiling): (a) cite Laudan & Leplin (1991), "Empirical Equivalence and Underdetermination," *Journal of Philosophy* 88(9):449–472 — their point that empirically equivalent theories can still differ in evidential support is load-bearing against the article's later "neither programme can claim evidential supremacy" parity move; WEB-VERIFY the citation before inserting. (b) Note Laudan's "Demystifying Underdetermination" and that Quine himself later downgraded underdetermination to "not much more than a conjecture." (c) Add one sentence acknowledging Duhem explicitly confined confirmation holism to physics (excluding physiology, certain chemistry, logic/mathematics) — and address why that does not defeat the "deeper in consciousness science" thesis rather than leaving the omission looking evasive. Apply [[evidential-status-discipline]]: scope every downstream use of "underdetermination" to the form actually licensed (holist is secure; contrastive is contested). Preserve calibration/hedge language; enforce the length ceiling. May share a pass with the consolidated P1 ChatGPT refine if length permits.

### ✓ 2026-06-07: Add symmetry/equal-risk passages to duhem-quine article (apply Duhem-Quine to the Map's own hard core)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-07, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini] — UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-07. Synthesis: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-07.md. CONVERGENT symmetry/dual-burden cluster — all three reviewers independently flag that the article demands predictive/falsifiable rigour of physicalism while exempting the Map's own interface model. ChatGPT (equal-risk / under-applied): "the Map's own hard core needs the same auxiliary-belt analysis … the article should say which revisions would be progressive and which degenerating." Claude (progressivity incoherence / "proves too much"): a programme whose mechanism is "constructed to leave aggregate Born statistics undisturbed … cannot coherently be claimed Lakatos-progressive." Gemini: "The Dualist Interface theory predicts absolutely nothing about the topology of the brain … a theory that can predict everything predicts nothing." SIBLING of the Claude P1 "Confront the Lakatosian-progressivity incoherence" task below (same cluster, distinct angle) — coordinate so the two are written in ONE coherent symmetry pass per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]], do not double-write the dual-burden material. The article admits underdetermination "cuts both ways" but does not run the symmetry test on the Map's own commitments. Add: (a) a "What would be degenerating for the Map?" subsection (e.g. repeated null results for quantum-sensitive neural signatures followed only by moving-target site revisions; first-person irreducibility claims that never generate new constraints; hard-problem appeals used to immunize every empirical failure); (b) one sentence in the quantum-interface passage stating Duhem-Quine does not license indefinite quantum retreat; (c) note that first-person evidence's theory-ladenness threatens dualist/introspective methods too, not only physicalism; (d) apply unconceived-alternatives risk to the Map's own physicalism-vs-dualism taxonomy (neutral/Russellian monism, panpsychism, illusionism, etc.). May be done in the same pass as the consolidated P1 refine if length permits; otherwise a follow-on. Watch the length ceiling — coordinate with the calibration constraint.

### ✓ 2026-06-07: Consolidated refine of duhem-quine-underdetermination-consciousness.md — scope-creep + COGITATE precision
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-07 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Already P1 (not upgraded). CONVERGENT items inside this consolidated pass — flagged by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-07 (synthesis: reviews/outer-review-synthesis-2026-06-07.md): item (c) **COGITATE decoding error** is 3/3-reviewer convergent (Claude calls it "a factual error in the load-bearing exhibit … must be fixed first"; Gemini §2.2 supplies the same corrected GNWT-challenge structure) — treat as the highest-confidence fix in this pass; item (a) **scope creep / underdetermination→parity** overlaps Claude's "master fallacy (not ruled out → positively supported)" and Gemini's "epistemic double standard," 3/3 convergent. CONSOLIDATE these coherent prose findings into ONE editor pass (do not split into churning per-finding tasks): (a) **Scope creep** — separate empirical underdetermination among neuroscientific models from conceptual/metaphysical underdetermination and the Map's tenet-bound inference; add a short passage ("underdetermination does not erase evidential gradients") noting COGITATE produced graded constraints even without eliminating either theory. (b) **GWT→GNWT** — use "GNWT (Global Neuronal Workspace)" in the COGITATE empirical section (lines 38/62/70); reserve "GWT" for the broader family. (c) **Decoding summary (line 62)** — replace "IIT better predicted face orientation decoding, while GWT better predicted object category decoding" with a precise sentence: category decoding appeared in both posterior and prefrontal regions (posterior stronger/more sustained), orientation decoding largely posterior with limited/absent PFC evidence. WEB-VERIFY against Nature 642:133-142 before rewriting. (d) **Seth quote (line 64)** — attribute the "theories are just too different…" quote to its actual source (Anil Seth, press coverage) or paraphrase the Nature abstract; do not leave it as "the investigators." (e) **"Put underdetermination to empirical test" (line 62)** — soften to "illustrates Duhem-Quine holism" (COGITATE tested biological-implementation predictions while cores stayed revisable). (f) **Symmetrize the explanatory-gap auxiliary framing** — present physicalism's "gap is epistemic/closable" and dualism's "gap is ontological/principled" as rival auxiliary commitments. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit where the PCS critique is softened: acknowledge PCS is a standing physicalist strategy (not a post-failure patch) and ask whether it is explanatorily progressive, in natural journal-quality prose — do not expose mode labels in the article body. Preserve calibration/hedge language (this is a calibration-anchor article); enforce the length ceiling.

### ✓ 2026-06-06: Calibrate Mapping Mind-Space — epistemic/metaphysical slide, unbridged convergence, overbroad challenge-dismissal
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-06, up to 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. The strongest convergent cluster: (a) **tenet-protective "Dualism makes the void real"** = 3/3 (ChatGPT "the article's clearest overstatement"; Claude "constitutional artifact, not a finding"; Gemini "tenet-protective asymmetry... epistemologically circular"); (d) **unbridged dimension-type convergence** = 3/3 (ChatGPT "not convergence unless the article supplies a bridge argument"; Claude "fail the evidential-independence standard"; Gemini "unbridged heterogeneous dimension convergence"). (b) acquaintance/knowledge conflation and (c) overbroad "Current evidence meets none of these" = 2/3 (ChatGPT + Claude/Gemini echo). UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews. This is the PRIMARY editor pass for the converged structural fixes — keep the other mapping-mind-space refine tasks (co-optation, Carr/literature, counterargument-import) as distinct passes per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] but sequence this one first. Originally from ChatGPT 5.5 Pro, consolidating the coherent prose findings into ONE editor pass per [[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]] (do NOT split into per-sentence tasks). All four are verified against the live article text in the review's Verification Notes: (a) **tenet-protective overstatement** — the Relation-to-Site-Perspective sentence "Dualism makes the void real" plus "under reductionism, mind-space is ... surveyable in principle" vs "physical methods alone cannot survey it" presents reductionism as antecedently surveyable and dualism as antecedently vindicated. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is an unsupported-foundational-move / tenet-perimeter pattern (the same boundary-substitution three prior outer reviewers converged on). Identify what the move actually earns: epistemic mind-space limits arise under physicalism, functionalism, and representationalism too, so the dualism-specific ontological claim is tenet-CONDITIONAL, not evidential. Mark it conditional in natural journal-quality prose; keep mode labels OUT of the article body (editor-internal only — note the classification in the refine changelog entry, per [[writing-style]]). (b) **acquaintance/knowledge conflation** — the Flatland line "cannot know those dimensions exist" overstates; soften to "may lack first-person acquaintance with" and note hidden dimensions can be inferred indirectly (psychophysics, neural decoding, comparative ethology). (c) **overbroad challenge-dismissal** — "Current evidence meets none of these" should become a graded ledger (no evidence dissolves the strongest acquaintance gap; SOME evidence supports partial structural mapping). (d) **unbridged convergence** — the "Convergent Arguments for Missing Dimensions" section treats neural/phenomenal/functional/physical dimension-types as independent convergence with no bridge argument; reframe per [[evidential-status-discipline]] ("ban unbridged convergence") as analogies/partial frameworks of DIFFERENT dimensional kinds, not one convergent thesis. PRESERVE load-bearing calibration hedges; length-neutral (voids article, watch the soft threshold). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché.

### ✓ 2026-06-06: Fix Carr citation + engage omitted 2016-2026 mind-space mapping literature in Mapping Mind-Space
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-06, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Two convergent sub-findings. (i) **Carr/Sjöstedt-Hughes source-tier** (ChatGPT + Claude): ChatGPT — "Carr and Sjöstedt-Hughes... should not sit in the same evidential register as neuroscience"; Claude — "Carr is a parapsychology-adjacent figure (Society for Psychical Research)... presenting it as a convergent physics argument without flagging its fringe status overstates its evidential weight." (ii) **Empirical staleness / omitted live mapping literature** (ChatGPT + Claude): both flag the article omits the 2016-2026 externally-measurable mind-space mapping literature (ChatGPT — "multidimensional frameworks of consciousness exist... the article largely bypasses that landscape"; Claude — "ignores the entire 2016–2026 literature that treats mind-space as externally mappable"). Gemini independently raised both but as FALSE wholesale-omission charges (the article cites Carr/Arsiwalla); its leg does NOT count toward convergence here. UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews. Originally from Claude Opus 4.8, Findings 5 + 8 — VERIFIED this cycle (Carr Nova 2021 chapter, Birch 2020 five dimensions confirmed by web-search). Two parts. (a) **Carr citation + source-tier (mechanical)**: References entry 9 "Carr, B. (2015) ... PhilArchive" — the canonical published form is **Carr (2021), "Making Space and Time for Consciousness in Physics," Ch. 7 in *Perspectives on Consciousness*, ed. P. Dennison (Nova Science Publishers), pp. 319-350**. NOTE for the editor: the PhilArchive and Nova versions are the SAME essay (the Claude reviewer slightly overstated by implying PhilArchive is a "weaker variant" — Verification Notes corrects this), so update to the published citation but do not treat it as a different source. Also flag Carr's SPR / parapsychology-adjacent standing so the "higher-dimensional physics" line is not presented as a peer of the neuroscience citations. (b) **Empirical staleness**: the article's thesis anchors are Nagel 1974 / McGinn 1989 / Shanahan 2010 and it omits the live externally-measurable mind-space mapping literature that is the direct counter-thesis. Add and ENGAGE (don't just list) the strongest of: Bayne/Hohwy/Owen "Are There Levels of Consciousness?" (TiCS 2016); Birch/Schnell/Clayton "Dimensions of Animal Consciousness" (TiCS 24(10):789-801, 2020 — five dimensions, "consciousness profile"); Butlin/Long et al. "Consciousness in AI" (arXiv 2308.08708 / TiCS 2026); Hernández-Orallo *The Measure of All Minds* (2017); Yampolskiy "The Universe of Minds" (2014, builds on Sloman). The article CAN reply that these measure functional/behavioural proxies not phenomenal mind-space (per the dualism tenet) — but it must MAKE that argument rather than omit the literature (apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: the framework-boundary reply in natural prose). VERIFY each citation at the publisher of record before insertion ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]). Watch the soft length threshold (voids article) — engage selectively, don't bloat.

### ✓ 2026-06-06: Fix conceptual origin-misattribution + co-optation of naturalist authors in Mapping Mind-Space
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-06, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. Co-optation of naturalist authors toward a dualist reading they reject is a 3-of-3 convergent finding — Claude (most detailed: Shanahan Wittgensteinian/anti-metaphysical, McGinn "naturalistic but not constructive," Schwitzgebel crazyist symmetry), ChatGPT ("Shanahan... is not evidence that mind-space is a non-physical ontological domain"; Nagel/McGinn "do not establish that physical methods alone cannot survey mind-space"), Gemini ("Shanahan... deliberately and explicitly avoids the metaphysical pitfalls of dualistic thinking"). UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews. Originally from Claude Opus 4.8, Findings 2-4 — VERIFIED in the review's Verification Notes (Sloman origin + Birch/Carr confirmed by live web-search this cycle). Distinct from the ChatGPT "Calibrate" task (that one is the prose epistemic/metaphysical-slide pass; this one is author-attribution accuracy). (a) **Origin-misattribution (mechanical, high-confidence)**: body line 46 credits "Murray Shanahan's 'space of possible minds'." The phrase + research program originate with **Aaron Sloman, "The Structure of the Space of Possible Minds," in *The Mind and the Machine: Philosophical Aspects of AI*, ed. S. Torrance (Ellis Horwood, 1984), pp. 35-42** — Shanahan himself credits Sloman. Re-credit Sloman (1984) as originator with Shanahan (2010) as development; ADD Sloman to the References list. (b) **Co-optation of Shanahan/McGinn/Schwitzgebel** — the article enlists three naturalists toward a dualist reading they reject. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: where naturalist authors are cited for their epistemic-limit arguments, mark in natural journal-quality prose that their own frameworks are anti-dualist (Shanahan: Wittgensteinian/anti-metaphysical; McGinn: "naturalistic but not constructive," rejects dualism; Schwitzgebel: crazyist symmetry, credence in dualism "not much above 50%") — cite the epistemic limit honestly but do not present these authors as tilting toward the dualist/high-dimensional destination. Keep mode labels OUT of the article body (editor-internal; note in the refine changelog per [[writing-style]]). Length-neutral; preserve load-bearing hedges; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. (The origin-misattribution Shanahan→Sloman sub-finding (a) is Claude-singleton-mechanical; keep it bundled in this same editor pass.)

### ✓ 2026-06-06: Refine concepts/weak-measurement-and-post-selection.md — fix falsifiability + realist-reading equivocation
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-06 found two refine-grade issues on ONE article (consolidate into a SINGLE pass; do not split). Citations verified clean — do NOT re-de-cite. (1) FALSIFIABILITY (line 122): the article advertises "specific empirical constraints" but the lone concrete test — conscious vs. automated measurement producing "identical Born-rule statistics" — is unfalsifiable BY CONSTRUCTION (theory predicts statistical identity, so a difference "would bear on" it but can never appear). Reframe the Born-statistics point as a *consistency/no-signalling constraint* the framework must respect, NOT a forward test that could confirm it; foreground the genuine handle — the realist-vs-statistical weak-value interpretation debate (line 120) — as THE falsification dependency. Keep the honest untestability admission. (2) EPISTEMIC/METAPHYSICAL EQUIVOCATION (line 76 "increasingly difficult to dismiss as mere formalism"; echoed in intro line 32 + description "naturally described as quantum systems being shaped by both past preparation and future outcomes"): experiments settle only the OPERATIONAL fact (reproducible pointer shifts, uncontested across all 3 readings); the realist time-symmetric-ontology reading is a METAPHYSICAL addition the experiments don't adjudicate (Ferrie & Combes 2014, already cited, argue the statistical reading). Soften line 76 to state genuine open-ness across realist/statistical/operational; hedge the "naturally described as" framing to "on the realist reading of TSVF, described as". Secondary: (a) align intro/description with body — a SINGLE weak measurement is imprecise; the value emerges only from ENSEMBLE averaging (line 36), so the description's "extracts partial quantum information" over-compresses; (b) add ONE sentence acknowledging the post-decoherence move (line 100) trades quantum-mechanistic content for decoherence-robustness, stating what residual quantum element remains once selection is over classically-distinguishable states. PRESERVE the exemplary lab-to-nature honesty (lines 84-94, 108) and the collapse-vs-boundary distinction (line 98). Length-neutral (~2655w, well under threshold). See pessimistic-2026-06-06.md.

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix dropped FND-framing propagation in topics/neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture.md (line ~109)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: DROPPED PROPAGATION SIBLING (multifile-task-dropped-file pattern). The 2026-06-04 Claude Opus 4.8 outer-review VERIFIED FACTUAL ERROR — conversion disorder / FND is NOT a "descending motor channel disconnecting"; modern FND literature reports INCREASED amygdala↔(pre-)SMA functional connectivity + active symptom-generation via aberrant precision-weighting, NOT a severed/decoupled channel — was fixed in two siblings (✓ conversion-disorder-as-consciousness-side-fault; ✓ clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence "increased, not severed, amygdala-motor coupling") but this file was named on the propagation list and NOT fixed. Confirmed live at line 109: "Conversion disorder **disconnects the same descending motor channel through purely psychogenic functional decoupling**—no lesion required—and produces the inverse presentation: conscious selection without action". FIX: rephrase so the article's load-bearing point survives (conversion disorder isolates the SAME descending motor channel from the opposite direction as alien-hand — conscious selection without executed action) WITHOUT asserting the backwards "disconnect/decouple" mechanism. The correct framing: FND produces motor symptoms through ACTIVE top-down generation (an abnormally precise motor prior actively generates the symptom), so the descending pathway's *output* is functionally overridden/captured, not severed — the channel-separability point (same channel, opposite-direction failure, mechanistically disjoint from structural lesion) still holds, but via active functional capture not disconnection. Citations to web-verify at publisher of record before adding any (full author + given name, [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]): Voon et al. 2010 Brain (amygdala↔SMA Granger-causal); Edwards, Adams, Brown, Pareés & Friston 2012 Brain 135(11):3495-3512 (Bayesian account / aberrant precision). Length-neutral; preserve [[evidential-status-discipline]] (compatibility-grade not vindication-grade) and the compound-signature framing at line 171. Cross-check the description-line metaphor too. See [[multifile-task-dropped-file]].

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Mark the convergence-vs-parsimony asymmetry in concepts/epistemology.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-05 (first ever on this 1594-word hub; last deep-reviewed 2026-05-14) found a latent INTERNAL TENSION: the article dismantles parsimony as lacking "epistemic warrant in metaphysical domains" (line 67, "The parsimony assumption") while elevating convergence as conferring "warrant that no single line could provide alone" (line 83, Fourth point under "Epistemology Under Dualism"). Both are theory-virtue / IBE meta-inferences; a critic reads this as selective epistemology (reject the simplicity virtue when it cuts against dualism, embrace the convergence virtue when it cuts for it). FIX (length-neutral, 1-2 sentences): distinguish the two — convergence is evidential agreement among arguments with DIFFERENT premises, whereas parsimony is a prior over ontologies; state which precondition convergence meets that parsimony fails. SECONDARY, same pass: (a) line 83 "each provide independent evidence" overstates — the knowledge argument, conceivability, and explanatory gap arguably share one underlying intuition (omission of the qualitative), so hedge to "partially independent" and note the argument-from-reason is the genuinely distinct leg (rational causation, not qualia); settle the detail at [[epistemology-of-convergence-arguments]]. (b) line 91 "certain about the existence and character of experience" over-reaches given documented introspective unreliability about CHARACTER — split existence-warrant (strong) from character-warrant (calibrated, fallible). (c) line 97 "stands or falls on whether its epistemological foundations hold" invites the Popperian falsifiability challenge unanswered — add a falsification condition + link [[tenet-falsification-conditions]]. (d) line 67 "demonstrates that this argument fails" → "argues that this argument fails" (softer for a contested claim). Preserve the strong causal-closure-as-stipulation move and the two-channels framing — those are the article's core contributions. No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05-epistemology]].

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix misattributed Batthyány & Greyson 2020 citation in concepts/filter-theory.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Surfaced by the 2026-06-05 recovery-order-asymmetry deep-review (Sepúlveda/Batthyány sweep). `concepts/filter-theory.md` References entry 1 (~line 208) reads `Batthyány, A. & Greyson, B. (2020). Terminal lucidity: A review and a case collection. *Archives of Gerontology and Geriatrics*, 88, 104016.` This is a CHIMERA/misattribution: the title "Terminal lucidity: A review and a case collection" belongs to **Nahm, M., Greyson, B., Kelly, E. W., & Haraldsson, E. (2012)**, *Archives of Gerontology and Geriatrics* 55(1), 138–142 (the 83-cases-over-250-years review) — NOT Batthyány & Greyson 2020, and the "88, 104016" volume/article is also wrong for the Nahm paper. Batthyány & Greyson's actual 2020 paper is the dementia terminal-lucidity study in *Psychology of Consciousness: Theory, Research, and Practice* 8(1):1–8 (DOI 10.1037/cns0000259), a DIFFERENT title. FIX per [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]] 3-state discipline: READ the in-text usage in filter-theory.md FIRST — if the body cites this for the terminal-lucidity review/83-case-collection finding, correct to Nahm et al. 2012 (AG&G 55(1):138–142); if it cites it for the dementia-lucidity study, correct to Batthyány & Greyson 2020 (Psychology of Consciousness 8(1):1–8) with the correct title. Web-verify both before choosing; do NOT guess. Metadata/attribution fix; preserve the in-text claim at its correct source. Length-neutral.

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Sharpen named-opponent engagement in concepts/carrolls-regress.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-05 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05e]]) found two structural issues in an otherwise strong, citation-clean concept page (Carroll *Mind* 4(14):278–280 and Polanyi *Philosophy* 41(155):1–18 both web-verified exact; Gödel "predates by 36 years" correct; no label leakage; ~1,639 body words, well under concept envelope — NOT a length task). (1) MEDIUM epistemic/metaphysical equivocation in "The Inferentialist Reading" (¶51–53) and the Dualism tenet-connection (¶71): the regress (inferentialist reading) establishes at most an *articulation/formalisation* limit ("no augmentation of *explicit* rules reproduces taking-as"), but the prose slides to a *constitutively non-formal mental operation* ("primitive operation of consciousness," "one face of the irreducibly mental") which is what the Dualism link requires — the speculation marker is attached to the Gödel resemblance, not to this epistemic→metaphysical bridge. Fix per [[evidential-status-discipline]]: split the two readings explicitly and supply the bridging argument (why an articulation-limit is evidence of a constitutively non-formal operation rather than an unformalised-but-physical one), addressing the "syntactic fact ≠ cognitive claim" objection — or downgrade. (2) MEDIUM boundary-substitution in the Brandom paragraph (¶63), the only engagement with the strongest contemporary opponent: the reply "social practice is itself a tissue of individual takings" *presupposes* individual-first priority, the exact premise Brandom denies — it does not refute Brandom inside his framework. Per [[direct-refutation-discipline]], either supply the in-framework argument (the community's conferral of an inferential licence is itself a taking-the-move-as-licensed, so the regress relocates not dissolves — without presupposing individualism) OR downgrade honestly to framework-boundary marking ("the disagreement turns on a prior commitment, not a refutation within Brandom's framework"). The Wittgenstein paragraph (¶59) already does this correctly — make Brandom inherit its discipline. Also soften "provably insufficient"/"shown to be incomplete by formal argument" (¶69) → "argued"/"on the inferentialist reading" (the reading is contested, so not *proven*); and scope the "self-stultifying" charge (¶69) to the agent-act reading (a formal calculus has no cognition to presuppose). LENGTH-NEUTRAL preferred. PRESERVE the up-front honesty ("later moves rest on the inferentialist reading being defensible, not uncontested") and the cousins-not-siblings Gödel calibration. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05e]].

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Address dualist-machinery overclaims in topics/consciousness-and-collective-phenomena.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-05 found 3 issues, all confined to the dualist-machinery sections (the phenomenology sections — Zahavi/Schmid/Tomasello/effervescence — are strong, accurate, well-cited; PRESERVE them, fixes are surgical). (1) HIGH — epistemic/metaphysical equivocation in the interface argument ([[evidential-status-discipline]]): "the specific reason groups **cannot** be conscious" (line ~158) and "Without a physical interface, the Map's framework provides no mechanism for group consciousness" (line ~70) upgrade a *model-relative conditional* (on the Map's interface model, groups lack the posited quantum substrate → the framework PREDICTS no group consciousness) into an *unconditional metaphysical impossibility*. Split the readings: make the claim model-relative ("the Map's framework predicts groups are not conscious"); reserve "cannot" only if explicitly marked as a within-framework consequence, not an independent impossibility proof. (2) MEDIUM — circularity in "The Instructive Asymmetry" (lines ~146-156): the asymmetry is DERIVED from the interface premise, so "the absence of group consciousness... supports [Dualism]" uses the framework's own assumption as evidence; the observation is equally explained by the null hypothesis (groups just aren't the kind of thing that's conscious, on any theory) so it does not discriminate. Downgrade "supports" → "is consistent with / does not embarrass"; inherit the restraint the Occam paragraph (line ~164) already shows. (3) MEDIUM — unfalsifiable "prediction" framing (line ~158): "predicts exactly the phenomenological structure observed" — the structure is reported via individual testimony, identical under a group-mind hypothesis; reframe "predicts/confirms" → "is consistent with and economically accommodates," or state what would disconfirm. ALSO: (a) No-Many-Worlds paragraph (line ~162) asserts rather than argues that branching "undermines the indexical weight" — supply the bridging step or honestly mark as framework-boundary per [[direct-refutation-discipline]] (currently nearer boundary-substitution than in-framework refutation). (b) IIT dismissed only on Map-internal grounds (line ~66) — add one sentence noting IIT ITSELF assigns low Φ to loosely-coupled groups (Tononi/Koch), engaging it on its own terms (mild boundary-substitution fix). (c) ape clause (line ~126): cut or hedge the "neural-quantum interfaces" leap — the Tomasello evidence is silent on quantum substrates (non-sequitur). (d) line ~160 "too direct and too pervasive to dismiss as correlation" → soften ("difficult to dismiss"); owe a sentence on why the epiphenomenalist correlate-only story fails. Galton citation is CORRECT (1907 Nature 75:450-451) — do not "fix" it. Length: 3359w incl. frontmatter, well under topics thresholds; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05d]].

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix Sarasso (2015) citation-scope overreach in topics/self-stultification-as-master-argument.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review (2026-06-05, [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05c]]) found a citation-scope overreach in the Epiphenomenalism section's ketamine worked exhibit (~line 83). The text states ketamine reports "demonstrate consciousness maintained its causal connection to **memory encoding** during the dissociated interval" with "(Sarasso et al. 2015)" attached to the surrounding claim. Sarasso et al. (2015, *Current Biology* 25(23):3099-3105) measured **perturbational complexity index (PCI)** — wakefulness-like cortical complexity under ketamine vs slow-wave collapse under propofol/xenon — NOT memory encoding. The memory-encoding/report-correspondence inference is the Map's own interpretive layer (legitimate as argument, not attributable to Sarasso). FIX: keep Sarasso (2015) cited only for the wakefulness-like-complexity clause (already correctly scoped there) and reframe the memory-encoding sentence as the Map's inference *from* the retrospective reports, not as demonstrated by the cited study. Mirror the correct scoping already used in the sibling `topics/anaesthesia-and-the-consciousness-interface.md` (which cites Sarasso for complexity only). SECONDARY (low, fold in): the Eliminativism section's two-horn dilemma ("relabelling OR self-stultification returns deeper") omits the naturalised-epistemology third horn — add one clause acknowledging it and pointing the burden back to the norm-grasping/implementing distinction. LENGTH-AWARE: article is 3160w (soft_warning, soft 3000 / hard 4000); both edits are net-neutral — do NOT push it over the hard threshold. NO label leakage, NO reasoning-mode substitution failure, altered-state symmetry audit does not apply (no filter/transmission convergence framing). Web-verify Sarasso scope before editing if unsure ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]). See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-05c]].

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix fabricated "Marzbani et al. (2022)" CBT citation corpus-wide (wrong author + unsupported numbers)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Flagged + web-verified by /deep-review 2026-06-05 of concepts/bidirectional-interaction.md (already FIXED there). THIRD fabricated-author citation on the clinical-neuroplasticity cluster (siblings of the Marueckova/Randeniya fixes), and it survived 5 prior reviews because author+year matched. The cite "Marzbani, H., et al. (2022). Neural effects of cognitive behavioral therapy in psychiatric disorders... *Frontiers in Psychology*, 13, 853804" is a CHIMERA: (a) WRONG AUTHOR — DOI 10.3389/fpsyg.2022.853804 is by **Yuan, S., Wu, H., Wu, Y., Xu, H., Yu, J., Zhong, Y., Zhang, N., Li, J., Xu, Q., & Wang, C. (2022)**, web-verified via Frontiers + PMC9112423; "Marzbani, H." is a real but UNRELATED author (Marzbani, Marateb & Mansourian 2016 neurofeedback review, *Basic & Clinical Neuroscience* 7(2):143-158). (b) UNSUPPORTED NUMBERS — the claim "302 depressed subjects, 18 experiments, CBT decreases right amygdala via top-down vs SSRI bottom-up" does NOT match Yuan et al. (actual: 13 studies, ~87 MDD subjects, prefrontal/precuneus focus, no right-amygdala / top-down-vs-bottom-up contrast). Fix in TWO files: (1) `obsidian/concepts/mental-effort.md` ~line 106 body + ref ~line 181 — correct author to Yuan et al. (2022) and re-ground the claim (drop "302 subjects"; the genuine top-down/bottom-up dual-process asymmetry IS supportable from the broader treatment-mechanism literature, hedge it). (2) `obsidian/research/clinical-neuroplasticity-bidirectional-causation-2026-03-21.md` lines 78-84 + ref 215 — this is the PROPAGATION SOURCE; correct it to stop reinjection. See the calibration-preserving fix already applied in bidirectional-interaction.md as the template. `archive/concepts/mental-causation.md` carries it too (archived, low priority). See [[reviews/deep-review-2026-06-05-bidirectional-interaction]], [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]], [[citation-verify-false-negative]] (distinguish wrong-author from fabricated).

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Add FDG-PET metabolic-cost counterevidence to edge-states reducing-valve argument
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-05 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), the review's one DISTINCT high-value finding (its Weakness 1), VERIFIED present-as-gap this cycle (grep: "metabol"/"glucose"/"PET"/"FDG" appear NOWHERE in the article). The Reducing Valve / psychedelic-paradox argument (body ~lines 83–91) leans only on BOLD-fMRI DMN *decrease* to argue the brain is "doing less work" / "less filtering yielding more experience". It omits the FDG-PET glucose-metabolism literature showing the psychedelic state is metabolically *expensive* (global CMRglc INCREASE), which is a direct physicalist counterweight to the "filter doing less work → less energy" inference the reducing-valve picture invites. Add a short caveat in the Reducing Valve section acknowledging the BOLD-decrease/metabolic-increase tension and that DMN BOLD reduction is a localized network reconfiguration, not whole-brain downregulation — then argue the filter reading on its own terms rather than letting the energy-cost objection stand unaddressed. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit (an in-framework rival the article currently leaves untouched) and honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] (the BOLD data does not discriminate filter from production accounts — inherit the [[filter-theory]] "one pattern, not seven confirmations" caveat). **MUST web-verify before citing** ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]): the review's specific "12.5% global CMRglc increase under DMT/harmine" figure and "metabolic hyperfrontality under psilocybin" are UNVERIFIED reviewer claims — confirm the actual 2024/2025 FDG-PET source and figures, or state the qualitative point without the unverified number. This OVERLAPS the existing Claude P2 "REBUS/DMN/NDE empirical currency" task (its DMN item b) and the ChatGPT P2 consolidation — COORDINATE: fold the FDG-PET point into ONE edge-states empirical pass with those rather than churning the file a fourth time ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]); if `/combine-outer-reviews` has landed for this cycle, follow its consolidation. Net-additive; check voids length headroom (2000/3000/4000 soft/hard) and condense if it busts soft. See `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-05-gemini-2-5-pro.md` Verification Notes.

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix Gładziejewski misattribution in edge-states-and-void-probes

Both fields are WRONG: the author is **Paweł Gładziejewski** and "From Altered States to Metaphysics: The Epistemic Status of Psychedelic-induced Metaphysical Beliefs" was published in *Review of Philosophy and Psychology* (DOI 10.1007/s13164-023-00709-6), online 2023, vol 16(1) pp. 175–197. Change "Piotr" → "Paweł" and "(2024)" → "(2023)" in both the body and the reference list; optionally add vol/page detail to the reference. Verified independently this cycle (PhilPapers GADFAS-2; Springer s13164-023-00709-6) — apply directly, no re-verification needed. Mechanical citation fix; keep it separate from the prose-level edge-states consolidation below ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-05: Fix Metzinger co-optation in edge-states-and-void-probes
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-05 (Claude Opus 4.8) — the review's gravest accuracy finding, VERIFIED this cycle against Metzinger's open-access 2020 primary source (*Philosophy and the Mind Sciences* 1(I), DOI 10.33735/phimisci.2020.I.46). The article (Zero-Person Perspective section, line ~93–97, and the "Epistemic openness" bullet, line ~65) borrows Metzinger's MPE vocabulary — "unobstructed/unpartitioned epistemic space", "zero-person perspective", "completely open to the possibility of any epistemic state" — to support the dualist inference that consciousness is "not identical with its contents". This INVERTS Metzinger's own framing: he advances MPE explicitly as a NATURALISTIC minimal-model explanation, hypothesising that pure awareness "really is the content of a predictive model, namely, a Bayesian representation of tonic alertness" realised by brain properties that "cannot exist without the brain"; he is an eliminativist about the self (*Being No One*, 2003) and (per the review, corroborated via secondary sources, not the primary 2024 text) names an "E-fallacy" warning AGAINST reading metaphysics off phenomenology. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit (co-optation = counterargument absorption): state Metzinger's naturalistic MPE conclusion explicitly, then make the Map's disagreement *as* a disagreement in his own framing, rather than borrowing his vocabulary for the opposite conclusion. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — zero-person/MPE UNDERdetermines dualism (it is equally a physicalist self-model-offline story). Also: the "completely open to the possibility of any epistemic state" phrase is presented in quotation marks (line ~65) WITHOUT a page cite; either supply a 2024-book page cite or de-quote it to a paraphrase ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]). Distinct from the ChatGPT-generated consolidation task below — Metzinger is not covered there. Keep mechanical; do not balloon the section (voids 2000/3000/4000 thresholds). See `reviews/outer-review-2026-06-05-claude-opus-4-8.md` Verification Notes.
