- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-04d found three medium/low issues (the empirical spine and tenet-alignment are otherwise sound; do NOT over-edit). (1) **Selectivity overreach** — intro line 41 "Something distinguishes experiencers from non-experiencers, and that something remains unexplained" is stronger than the body's own line-68 caveat (recall bias + unmeasured physiological variation are sufficient mundane distinguishers); soften to "incompletely explained" and forward the recall-bias point so the truncation-resilient intro carries the caveat. (2) **Altered-state symmetry-acknowledgment under-named** (audit gate PASSES, disruptive cluster IS engaged in the Asymmetry section — this is NOT the critical double-counting failure) — add 1-2 sentences in "The Asymmetry Problem" naming the *parallel accommodation* the production theorist has: the supportive cluster (NDE + terminal lucidity + psychedelic expansion) carries the evidential weight of ONE pattern, not several independent confirmations, and the production framing accommodates it via the functional-vs-structural-suppression move the article already cites approvingly at line 120 (Mashour). (3) **Epistemic→metaphysical drift** — "more complex than production models" (epistemic, well-supported) is recruited at lines 39/140 to assert "isn't produced by neural activity" (metaphysical); a richer production model (the gamma surge is the live candidate) satisfies the epistemic reading without conceding the metaphysical; soften the Dualism paragraph gloss / name the enriched-production-model escape. Also: "Filter theory **dissolves** this paradox" (line 114) overclaims given the Asymmetry section then narrows it — change to "reframes/addresses"; and the MQI paragraph (line 148) "quantum interface may become more fluid as neural constraints dissolve" has no mechanism and runs against decoherence physics (dying tissue is MORE decoherent) — downgrade to explicit speculation or cut. PRESERVE the Asymmetry Problem honesty, the "What Would Challenge This View?" falsifiers, and all accurate citations. See pessimistic-2026-06-04d.md.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Re-scope the falsifiability list in concepts/interactionist-dualism.md (mis-scoped to a demoted mechanism)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-04c (argument-defensibility / Popperian register; deep-reviews don't cover this). The "Selection, Biasing, and Falsifiability" section (lines ~140–142) answers the unfalsifiability worry with a 4-falsifier list, but falsifiers #3 (Zeno effect can't be mentally triggered) and #4 (anaesthesia is classical) are tied to the **Stapp/Zeno mechanism** — which the article ELSEWHERE explicitly declines to commit to (lines 134, 150: "does not commit to which proposal is correct"; "takes no position on which quantum mechanism is correct") and in fact DEMOTES in favour of post-decoherence selection ("precisely because it does not require coherence to survive the decoherence the objection invokes"). So refuting Zeno (#3/#4) would falsify a mechanism the Map ranks *behind* its preferred reading and leave the position untouched — the list overstates the POSITION's falsifiability. Only #1/#2 are position-level falsifiers, and those are the hard in-principle ones. INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY: companion `topics/comparing-quantum-consciousness-mechanisms.md` grades Stapp/Zeno "Falsifiability: Moderate" and says the discriminating test is "beyond current technology" — in tension with this article's confident "speculative, not unfalsifiable." FIX (calibration/scope, NOT deletion): re-scope the list so #1/#2 read as position-level and #3/#4 as explicitly mechanism-specific (bearing on a sub-mechanism the Map prefers not to lean on), and soften the closing to match the sibling's "Moderate"/"beyond current reach" framing. Also tighten line 202 "predicts exactly what NCC discovers" → consistent-with (the para is already honest that correlation data is non-discriminating; fold the NCC-side falsifiability tension into this same pass — no separate task). PRESERVE the genuine point that explanatory-coherence claims stand independently of mechanism falsifiability. Keep under concepts length thresholds (article is 3099w body). See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-04c]].

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Down-register "logical defect" framing in concepts/delegatory-causation.md (epistemic↔logical equivocation; corpus-internal inconsistency)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /pessimistic-review 2026-06-04 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-04b]], Issue 1). GROUNDED, load-bearing. The "Epiphenomenalism Parallel" subsection (L132, L134) and "Honest Limitations" (L180) frame the self-stultification asymmetry against epiphenomenalism as a "**logical defect**" / "**defeats its own evidence base**" / near "**consistency requirement**" — but the corpus's own canonical pages explicitly classify this exact argument as the OPPOSITE: `self-stultification.md` L65 ("Logical contradiction ... Self-stultification is *weaker*: the position *can* be true. What it cannot be is *rationally held*") and L171 ("doesn't prove a position false ... a *weaker* conclusion than falsification"); `epiphenomenalism.md` L95 ("epistemically self-undermining—NOT logically contradictory, since it could still be true, but impossible to hold rationally"). So delegatory-causation.md is the outlier recruiting an EPISTEMIC defeater (cannot be rationally believed) to assert a stronger LOGICAL/constitutive claim. This is epistemic↔metaphysical equivocation in register-shifted form (orthogonal to hedge density — the paragraph is otherwise well-hedged) AND a genuine internal inconsistency (the `[[self-stultification]]` link the reader follows denies what the hub asserts). It is load-bearing: the "logical defect/consistency requirement" framing is precisely what makes delegation's advantage "a constraint of a *different kind*" (L130). FIX: down-register "logical defect" → "epistemic self-defeat / cannot be rationally endorsed (though it may still be true)"; soften "consistency requirement" → rational-credibility framing, matching `self-stultification.md` vocabulary verbatim; add a one-clause cross-link making explicit this is the epistemic, not logical, defeater. PRESERVE the substance (the asymmetry is a harder constraint than parsimony-preference — still true on the epistemic reading) and the strong tu-quoque dialectic (L130) and ensemble-level-epiphenomenalism honesty (L148); do NOT touch the verified "self-undermining" vocabulary at L170 (that phrasing is fine). LENGTH-NEUTRAL edit (concept page, well under threshold); no new sections; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché. The three load-bearing citations (Saad 2025, Schaffer 2000, Torres Alegre 2025) and DeWall 2008 are all web-verified CORRECT — do NOT re-touch them. Optional while here: the two Saad block-quotes (L95/L103) were not line-verified against the Springer OA PDF this pass (low suspicion) — line-check if convenient. Tenet alignment: Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — the asymmetry grounds why delegation beats epiphenomenalism).

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Note Schrödinger's monist reading in voids/plurality-void.md opening (optional)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review (pessimistic-2026-06-04-plurality-void.md) found ONE low-severity calibration nuance on an otherwise CONVERGED article. The opening (line 31) cites Schrödinger ("Consciousness is a singular of which the plural is unknown.") to anchor the *plurality void* — the limit on conceiving multiple subjects. But Schrödinger's actual claim in *Mind and Matter* (1958) is the positive Vedantic-monist thesis that there is only ONE consciousness and apparent plurality is illusion (Maya), not a flagging of a cognitive limit. The article's narrower use (singularity is the datum) is DEFENSIBLE since he affirms singular experience. OPTIONAL one-clause acknowledgement that he drew a monist conclusion from the same datum; OR decline and leave as-is. LICENSE TO DECLINE: the 2026-04-28 deep review marked this article at convergence with an explicit "no changes" stability note; do NOT churn it for a low-severity nuance unless the edit is genuinely one clause and length-neutral (article is at 1696/2000, length-safe either way). All 8 citations re-verified clean this session (no fabrication/wrong-author/wrong-result). Equivocation + label-leakage checks both clean. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-04-plurality-void]].

The mismatch is close to analytic. ### ✓ 2026-06-04: Write concept page on the constitution-vs-doing-work bridge
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by /optimistic-review 2026-05-29 §"High Priority". Both [[concepts/pain-asymbolia]] and [[consciousness-and-causal-powers]] (§"Phenomenal Biasing") relocate the Map's real disagreement with predictive-processing rivals away from any shared dissociation/phenomenon and onto "the bridge from sub-personal valence-prior assignment to felt awfulness" — predictive processing treats that bridge as constituted by computational role; the Map treats the felt quality as adding causal work the sub-personal prior alone does not perform. This constrain-vs-establish move now recurs in multiple articles with no canonical home. The page should (a) define the constitution-vs-causal-work distinction precisely, (b) show why conceding "shared explanandum" at the level of a dissociation does NOT concede the dispute, (c) catalogue the Map articles that deploy the move (pain-asymbolia, consciousness-and-causal-powers, and kin), (d) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — the page defines a dialectical locus, it does not adjudicate it. Cross-link [[concepts/predictive-processing]], [[concepts/mental-causation-and-downward-causation]], [[concepts/epiphenomenalism]]. Estimated scope: 1,800–2,400 words; concepts/ (~245/270, room available). Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction).

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Name the falsifiability asymmetry in topics/quantum-darwinism-and-consciousness.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-04 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-04-quantum-darwinism-and-consciousness]]) found ONE low-severity clarity gap; article is otherwise converged and well-calibrated (all 7 citations verified — Zurek 2009/2022 web-checked at publisher of record, no defects; no editor-label leakage; no overclaims). The Falsifiability section states in two places, 38 lines apart, facts that are two faces of the same structural point but are never connected: (a) line 113 "its influence must be statistically invisible at the ensemble level (matching Born-rule predictions)" and (b) line 113 "a confirmed deviation from Born statistics... would refute the framework", while line 75 separately concedes the vacuity risk if selections are "statistically indistinguishable from unbiased Born-rule sampling". These are consistent only under an unstated asymmetry: the hypothesis PREDICTS no ensemble deviation, so it is REFUTABLE (a deviation kills it) but NOT symmetrically CONFIRMABLE (its prediction is null). Fix: add one clause making the asymmetry explicit — the proposal is refutable but not symmetrically confirmable, which is exactly why the line-75 vacuity worry and the line-113 falsifiability claim are the same coin (empirical content is entirely on the refutation side). This STRENGTHENS the existing "empirically disciplined, if not straightforwardly falsifiable" framing and connects it to the line-117 "boundary of empirical accessibility" admission the article already makes; it does NOT downgrade any claim. LENGTH: near-neutral (one added clause, ~25 words) — article is 2398 body words, well under the 4000-word topics hard threshold, so no ceiling constraint, but keep the edit minimal. Optional secondary (LOW): at line 89 the load-bearing indexical-identity premise ("a fact about which result I experience") is a legitimate [[tenets#^no-many-worlds]] dependency but is invisible to a reader not following the tenet link — consider a 1-clause "this rests on the No-Many-Worlds tenet" pointer at the point of use. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché; preserve the dense hedging (the article's signature strength). ai_contribution=100.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Reconcile disconnect-vs-active-generation framing in conversion-disorder-as-consciousness-side-fault.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From pessimistic-review 2026-06-04 (see reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-04-conversion-disorder.md). The 2026-06-04 propagation pass correctly updated the imaging section (lines 68, 72) to carry the Edwards/Friston (2012) *active-generation / aberrant-precision-weighting* account and the *increased* amygdala→motor coupling — but the article's dominant framing elsewhere still describes FND purely as a subtractive *channel disconnect* ("functional decoupling", "the channel fails", "conscious command cannot recruit it", "psychogenic disconnection", para 1/2, the three-route table row, the alien-hand contrast at line 86). The current FND literature frames the symptom as *actively generated* (an abnormally precise prior the system fulfils), not as a channel that simply fails — and the disconnect metaphor is the one the Claude Opus 4.8 outer-review 2026-06-04 flagged as closer to the discarded Freud/Janet picture. This is an INTERNAL-COHERENCE gap, not a citation defect (Voon, Edwards/Friston, Vuilleumier all web-verified real and correctly attributed; the literature treats precision-weighting and decoupling as COMPLEMENTARY, so do NOT over-correct into "the disconnect framing is simply wrong"). FIX: add one reconciling clause/sentence where the "channel fails / cannot recruit" framing first appears (paras 1–2) noting that at the mechanistic level the failure is realized as *active over-generation of an inhibitory/symptom prior*, not a passive break, so "the descending channel is separable" is an architectural-level description whose physical realization is additive; ensure the three-route table's "Psychogenic — functional decoupling" cell and the alien-hand "inverse" contrast are consistent with active generation. Keep the existing two-tier interface-vs-intra-cerebral calibration (lines 72, 98, 106) — it is already correct. LENGTH: body is ~2972w / 4000 hard — headroom exists, but keep the addition minimal (≤~40w net); this is a reconciliation, not an expansion. No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché.

This addresses the cross-article half of the review; the prose-calibration tasks above cover the target article itself.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Soften over-strong amnesia and intact-substrate claims in clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-04 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). Two empirical-precision fixes, verified verbatim in the live article: (1) the amnesia section (lines 71–73) asserts "The information was stored, encoded, and retained" and "access returns immediately and fully" — too strong. Reversibility does not by itself establish intact storage, accuracy, or non-reconstructive retrieval. Reframe toward "Recovery is often reported, sometimes abruptly, but reversibility does not by itself establish intact storage, accuracy, or non-reconstructive retrieval," and acknowledge memory reconstruction / source monitoring / state-dependent retrieval / suggestibility as live alternatives. (2) "the neural substrate remains anatomically intact" (line 41) is too blunt — if the substrate includes dynamic connectivity and predictive control, the substrate is precisely what changed. Reword toward "no gross destructive lesion is required; functional connectivity, agency, salience, and predictive-control networks may be selectively altered." This prevents "intact substrate" from smuggling in the conclusion that physicalism lacks a substrate-level explanation. Both edits are calibration-tightening and should be roughly length-neutral. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; do not over-hedge into mush.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Add the recall-vs-recognition DID memory-transfer counterevidence to clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md (Marsh/Huntjens 2021)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From Gemini 2.5 Pro outer review 2026-06-04 (the single genuinely-DISTINCT Gemini finding; not captured by the ChatGPT or Claude 06-04 legs). The DID section (lines 53-57) presents intra-state cognition intact + cross-state integration lost as favouring the interface reading, citing only Reinders 2003. It omits the most direct empirical counterevidence: **Marsh, Dorahy, Butler, Middleton, de Jong, Kemp & Huntjens (2021), "Inter-identity amnesia for neutral episodic self-referential and autobiographical memory in DID: An assessment of recall and recognition," *PLoS ONE* 16(2):e0245849** (VERIFIED real, correct attribution, DOI confirmed). Finding: DID patients subjectively report inter-identity amnesia on free RECALL, but objective RECOGNITION tests show significant cross-identity memory transfer (more than simulators / partial-info controls). This dissociates a metacognitive/retrieval-ownership deficit from a structural cross-state severance — i.e. the physical substrate demonstrably retains and integrates the information, so the "lost cross-state integration" claim should be qualified: the loss is at the level of executive/metacognitive access and felt ownership, not demonstrably at the substrate-integration level. FIX: add the recall/recognition distinction as a live counter-reading in the DID section; note the substrate retains the information (recognition transfer), which is exactly what a connectivity-physicalist predicts and which weakens the interface reading at this site; keep the article's two-tier "earns the architecture, not the dualist significance" calibration ([[evidential-status-discipline]]). A 2024 systematic-review/meta-analysis (ScienceDirect S0272735824001351) corroborates the transfer pattern and is an optional second anchor — WEB-VERIFY at publisher before adding ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]). Length-watch: target is length-constrained — keep the addition minimal/surgical; if it busts the ceiling, flag for human length decision rather than auto-condensing calibration content. NO mode labels ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Strengthen physicalist steelman + import DID sociocognitive caveat in clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-04, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews. All three flag the missing DID sociocognitive/fantasy/transtheoretical alternative + an under-engaged physicalist steelman. Gemini W2 supplies a VERIFIED concrete anchor: Lynn, Polizzi, Merckelbach, Chiu, Maxwell, van Heugten & Lilienfeld (2022), "Dissociation and Dissociative Disorders Reconsidered: Beyond Sociocognitive and Trauma Models Toward a Transtheoretical Framework," *Annual Review of Clinical Psychology* 18:259-289 (all seven authors, venue, pages confirmed). Claude: "The sociocognitive/fantasy model of DID (Spanos 1994; Lilienfeld, Lynn, Kihlstrom et al. 1999; Merckelbach) ... entirely absent." ChatGPT: the "single non-physical mind ... interface" reading "should not be presented as a natural reading until these clinical alternatives are locally discussed." Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: the article under-steelmans its opponent. (1) The strongest physicalist reply is not "connectivity does it somehow" but a developed functionalist / global-workspace / predictive-processing / self-model cluster on which selective disruptions of access, ownership, memory, and motor agency are EXACTLY what the architecture predicts — they are diagnostic probes of functional organization, not evidence a non-physical mind fails to couple. State this directly and in natural prose before giving the interface reading. The article correctly notes functionalism is a central position (not an ad-hoc retreat) — do NOT regress that; the reviewer flags that the "physicalism progressively adds complexity" framing is unfair since functional organization is where functionalism began. (2) The DID section (lines 53–57) should import the live sociocognitive-alternative caveat with more force, as the sibling [[dissociative-states-as-intact-substrate-interface-evidence]] article already does — discuss trauma vs. sociocognitive vs. fantasy/suggestion vs. metacognitive and non-reification models before offering the "single mind, fragmented interface" reading. Watch length: target is length-constrained — if adding the steelman busts the ceiling, condense elsewhere rather than truncate; if it forces an over-ceiling state, flag for human length decision rather than auto-condensing the calibration content.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Calibrate clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md framing to its own two-tier concession
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-04, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews. All three flag that the framing outruns the article's own two-tier bracketing. ChatGPT: "the title, section heading 'Why This Favours Dualism,' trauma-protection language, and Minimal Quantum Interaction paragraph pull harder than the evidence warrants." Claude: "Section architecture overrides the hedges ... Framing the physicalist's own prediction as an epicycle is the central rhetorical tilt." Gemini (hostile-referee register, W5 Seth & Bayne 2022 VERIFIED): "a tenet-bracketed framing that seamlessly smuggles its dualist conclusions into its very evaluation of the evidence." ⚠ TONE: Gemini overshoots ("circularity," "smuggling," "reject"); the live article is already two-tier and concedes the connectivity-physicalist reading — this is calibration-tightening, NOT thesis-reversal (Claude's own verification softens its "no evidential work" headline to exactly this). The ChatGPT structural finding (verified against the article). The title, the "Why This Favours Dualism" heading (line 100), the trauma-protection language, and the Minimal Quantum Interaction paragraph (line 122) pull HARDER than the article's own well-calibrated concession that the data "earn the architecture" but leave dualist significance to be argued separately. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is tenet-protective bracketing not fully honored. Identify what the issue calls for — here, honestly marking that the evidence discriminates against crude localization / region-identity views but does NOT strongly discriminate interface dualism from sophisticated functionalist/predictive-processing physicalism — and apply the reply in natural journal-quality prose (see [[writing-style|writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents" section). Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body; classification is editor-internal (changelog only). Concrete, LENGTH-NEUTRAL edits the reviewer proposed and I verified are accurate to the live text: (a) reword the "Why This Favours Dualism" heading toward "What the Dualist Reading Adds — and What It Does Not Add"; (b) replace the Minimal Quantum Interaction line "would produce exactly the dissociative pattern observed" (line 122) with a non-exact "could be made consistent with the pattern, but the clinical evidence does not currently discriminate quantum-interface from ordinary network-level explanations" — note the article ALREADY classifies MQI as a consistency (not novel-prediction) claim, so this aligns it with [[tenets]]; (c) demote "purposive selectivity" (line 114) to "apparent clinical selectivity" with the competing affective-salience/predictive-processing explanation given equal billing (the article already gestures at this in the same paragraph). Prefer minimal surgical edits over a rewrite; the article is already one of the better-bracketed pieces. Check length status before/after — keep length-neutral.

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Engage the precision-weighting common-cause undercut of the convergence argument in clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-04, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. Upgraded P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews — this is the cycle's highest-value shared finding: all three reviewers independently fault the article for never engaging the 2012–2026 predictive-processing / active-inference / precision-weighting paradigm, and Claude sharpens it into a common-cause undercut of the article's own convergence argument. Claude: "precision-weighting pathology is a *single* common cause spanning FND, DPDR, and dissociation generally — which undercuts the article's 'compound signature / convergence without a shared mechanism' claim at its root." Gemini W3: "the manuscript's total failure to test its interface hypothesis against the dominant cognitive-computational paradigm of the current decade: Predictive Processing and Active Inference." ChatGPT: "A serious physicalist response is not merely 'connectivity does it somehow' [but] a developed cluster of functionalist, global-workspace, predictive-processing ... accounts." The article's "compound signature / convergence without a shared mechanism" claim is undercut by a SINGLE shared physicalist common cause: precision-weighting pathology (aberrant precision in active-inference / predictive-processing) spans FND (Edwards/Friston 2012), DPDR (Seth 2012; Ciaunica 2022), and dissociation generally as a "functional dysconnection syndrome" in Friston's terms. If one mechanism (precision-weighting) generates all the converging routes, the convergence does NOT triangulate independent confirmations — exactly the [[common-cause-null]] / [[epistemology-of-convergence-arguments]] failure the Map's own discipline guards against. FIX: engage the precision-weighting common-cause explicitly; either (a) show the routes are NOT all reducible to precision-weighting (genuine independence), or (b) honestly downgrade the convergence claim's evidential weight where a shared upstream cause is plausible ([[evidential-status-discipline]], common-cause-null). ⚠ This is the Map's OWN discipline applied to its own argument — apply [[common-cause-null]] rigorously. ⚠ WEB-VERIFY any new cite at publisher of record before adding ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]); the precision-weighting mechanism sources (Edwards/Friston 2012, Seth/Suzuki/Critchley 2012) are verified-and-queued via the staleness task; Gemini's framing cite Constant/Clark/Friston 2022 is corroboration only and must be web-verified. NO mode labels ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Fix conversion-disorder factual error in clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md (increased, not severed, amygdala-motor coupling)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From Claude Opus 4.8 outer review 2026-06-04 (VERIFIED FACTUAL ERROR, the review's headline finding, likely distinct from ChatGPT's same-day review). The article frames conversion disorder / FND as a "descending motor channel DISCONNECTING" (citing Vuilleumier 2005, Brown 2004). The modern FND literature reports the OPPOSITE: **increased** functional connectivity between amygdala and motor/supplementary-motor regions — heightened limbic *influence over* motor control, not a severed channel. Voon et al. (2010, *Brain*, "Emotional stimuli and motor conversion disorder": greater right-amygdala↔right-SMA connectivity, Granger-causal amygdala→SMA direction); Hassa et al. (2017, *NeuroImage: Clinical*, n=13 functional-hemiparesis: increased left-amygdala↔(pre-)SMA + subthalamic-nucleus connectivity). The contemporary mechanism is Edwards, Adams, Brown, Pareés & Friston (2012, *Brain* 135(11):3495-3512, "A Bayesian account of 'hysteria'"): an abnormally precise intermediate-level motor prior ACTIVELY GENERATES the symptom (aberrant precision-weighting), not a severed channel — the article's framing is closer to the discarded Freud/Janet "hysteria" picture than current FND science (Stone/Edwards/Aybek/Hallett/Perez). FIX: replace the "channel disconnect" framing with the increased-coupling / active-generation account; concede "intact motor system + functional symptom" is now explained by active generation, not disconnection. ⚠ WEB-VERIFY every cite at publisher of record before adding (Voon 2010 Brain, Hassa 2017 NeuroImage:Clinical, Edwards/Friston 2012 Brain) — full author incl given name ([[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]]; wrong-author/dual-date caught repeatedly). ⚠ PROPAGATE to siblings inheriting the same motor-channel framing (conversion-disorder-as-consciousness-side-fault, neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture, dissociative-states-as-intact-substrate-interface-evidence). Benchmark that would partly rehabilitate the disconnect framing: a post-2015 replication showing *reduced* prefrontal-motor connectivity as dominant FND signature.

- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review. The temporal becoming article argues genuine creativity requires accumulated temporal context (durée) for generating new representational primitives. Mathematical creativity — where insight can be checked against objective truth — provides the strongest test case. Connects the temporal creativity thesis to the Map's strongest empirical anchor for phenomenological claims. See optimistic-2026-03-15-morning.md

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Write article on the phenomenology of interface opacity
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review. Phenomenal transparency explains why we can't see the representational medium; embodied-consciousness shows the interface extends beyond the brain. This article would systematically develop interface opacity — the specific ways consciousness fails to detect its own point of contact with matter — as distinct from both phenomenal transparency and general mysterianism. See optimistic-2026-03-15-morning.md

### ✓ 2026-06-04: Fix wrong-author misattribution in voids/mood-void.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review (pessimistic-2026-06-04-mood-void.md) web-verified a citation defect. The paper "The Intentional Structure of Moods" (*Philosophers' Imprint* 19:49, 2019) — quoted verbatim at line 40 ("happy glows or somber shadows...") and listed as reference 4 — is by **Uriah Kriegel**, NOT Jonathan Mitchell. The PDF URL in ref 4 resolves to the Kriegel paper; the quote is correctly transcribed. Jonathan Mitchell's 2019 mood paper is a DIFFERENT work ("The Intentionality and Intelligibility of Moods," *Eur. J. Philosophy* 27(1):118-135). 3-STATE: real-paper / WRONG-author → FIX, not delete. FIX (length-neutral): line 40 "Jonathan Mitchell (2019)" → "Uriah Kriegel (2019)"; ref 4 "Mitchell, J. (2019)." → "Kriegel, U. (2019)." Leave title/venue/quote/URL unchanged (all correct for Kriegel). Article is at soft_warning (2437/3000) — do NOT expand; this is a pure swap.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Fix Han & Choi (2016) citation gloss in brain-internal-born-rule-testing.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review (pessimistic-2026-06-03-brain-internal-born-rule-testing) found a load-bearing CITATION GLOSS defect in §"What the Corridor Is" (L55). The text attributes to Han & Choi (2016) the claim that "the per-trial information-theoretic capacity of such channels vanishes asymptotically as the number of trials grows." WEB-VERIFIED: the cite is REAL (Sci. Rep. 6:22986; arXiv:1307.2026) but the paper does NOT prove this — it derives the Born rule from a strengthened relativistic-causality condition and bounds quantum nonlocality; there is no per-trial, trials-indexed capacity-vanishing theorem. This is a possibility→theorem upgrade (an informal corollary dressed as a proven information-theoretic result), exactly what [[evidential-status-discipline]] guards against. FIX (surgical — do NOT disturb surrounding calibrated prose): downgrade to the qualitative claim the source actually supports — Born-deviating probability assignments under their causality condition enable superluminal signalling, so a strictly Born-compliant selection channel transmits no ensemble-level information (this is also exactly what the article's Torres Alegre 2025 cite proves: Φ(p)=p unique under no-signalling). Do NOT assert-fabricated and do NOT delete the cite — the paper is genuine; only its gloss is wrong. Article conclusion survives on the qualitative argument. The other three hot-zone cites (Torres Alegre arXiv:2512.12636, Arana 2025 PhilArchive ARATCQ-2, Kerskens & López Pérez 2022) were verified genuine — leave them.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Address "explanatory gap as intuition vs datum" counterargument across zombies.md, ncc.md, and explanatory-gap.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review (2026-04-16c) identified a recurring issue across three articles: the Map treats "persistent failure to close the explanatory gap after decades of neuroscience" as support for the dualist reading, but physicalists deny the gap was ever there to close — "persistent failure" is question-begging from their perspective. The gap is reported by philosophers primed to experience it and is largely absent from neuroscientific practice. Add acknowledgment in each article that the gap's status as a datum vs artefact of philosophical framing is itself contested; the Map takes the former view. Also address the "our conviction of phenomenal consciousness is better explained by actual experience than systematic illusion" assertion in zombies.md's Illusionist Challenge section — either develop the argument or hedge as a stance rather than a proof. See pessimistic-2026-04-16c.md Counterarguments section and Unsupported Claims table.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write voids article on the Readiness Void
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Research completed in `research/voids-readiness-void-2026-03-04.md` (2026-03-04) and never synthesised into an article. The readiness void concerns the inaccessibility of the pre-deliberative state in which an action is *about to be undertaken* — the felt readiness Schurger's stochastic-threshold model targets but cannot itself decompose into report-bearing components. Adjacent to but distinct from `voids/transit-void.md` (inaccessibility of transitions) and the Libet-readiness-potential literature. Voids cap currently 97/100 — has capacity but tight; verify the void's distinctness from `voids/transit-void.md` and from `concepts/phenomenology-of-choice-and-volition.md` before writing. Target section: voids/. Scope 1800-2400 words. Tenet alignment: Dualism (readiness as a phenomenal mode not extensionally captured by motor-preparation neural correlates), Bidirectional Interaction (readiness functioning as the staging ground for the agent's contribution).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Refine apex/taxonomy-of-voids.md to anchor the phenomenology-vs-function axis to its new concept-page
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Chain from today's expand-topic completion (2026-04-28 19:18 UTC). The apex `apex/taxonomy-of-voids.md` introduced the *phenomenology-vs-function axis* at line 110 (the apex-evolve cycle this morning) and discusses it in §Phenomenological Cluster paragraph plus the changelog entries at lines 220, 226, 247 — four references in total — but none yet wikilink to the dedicated concept-page anchor `[[phenomenology-vs-function-axis]]` (created today 19:18 UTC). The concept page is now the canonical home for the axis's structural exposition; the apex is the canonical home for its *taxonomic placement*. ### ✓ 2026-06-03: Reframe illusionism rebuttal in concepts/consciousness-and-scientific-explanation.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-03 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-03-consciousness-and-scientific-explanation]]) found ONE medium-value finding; the article is otherwise mature and converged (deep-reviewed 2026-05-31, NO critical issues, NO boundary-substitution, NO label-leakage, citations all canonical+correct — no web-verify needed). The "Objections Considered" reply to "The hard problem is an illusion" (line ~113) currently rebuts illusionism by asserting the hard problem "is not a theoretical posit but a report of what is directly given in experience." That RESTATES the disputed premise — the felt directness of givenness is exactly what Dennett/Frankish deny — so the reply begs the question against the illusionist. FIX (natural prose, no mode labels, [[direct-refutation-discipline]]): lead the rebuttal with the explanatory-burden ASYMMETRY the article already half-makes — the meta-problem does NOT vanish under illusionism; the illusionist still owes an account of why the seeming is so robust and so resistant to deflation that even illusionists must theorise it. Demote the bare "directly given" assertion from load-bearing to supporting. Keep the existing [[meta-problem-of-consciousness]] cross-link. LENGTH: 2454 raw words / well under the concepts threshold — this is a length-neutral reframe of ~2 sentences, do NOT grow the article materially and do NOT touch the (correctly-hedged, do-not-flatten) MQI testability paragraph at line ~59. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Optional low-value polish if convenient: "divides cleanly" → "divides into distinct kinds of question" (line ~97).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write project doc on convergent-conclusion-opposite-reasoning as named pattern
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-02 (Medium Priority). The 2026-05-02 18:54 UTC Duch research dossier (`research/wlodzislaw-duch-consciousness-2026-05-02.md`) explicitly names the *convergent-conclusion-opposite-reasoning* structure between Duch's anti-substantive-quantum stance and Tenet 2's Minimal Quantum Interaction (Duch wants zero quantum role because consciousness is fully classical-computational; the Map wants minimal-but-real because parsimony favours the smallest non-physical influence). The same structural pattern operates elsewhere — the Map's anti-Penrose-Hameroff-scale stance converges with Tegmark's decoherence argument despite opposite metaphysical reasoning; the Map's parsimony-favouring-minimal-interaction stance converges with Occam's-razor-style critiques of substantive-quantum despite Tenet 5's "Occam's Razor Has Limits" commitment. The pattern is now visible at multiple cluster locations and has a name (in the Duch dossier) but no project-doc treatment. Project-doc would (a) name the pattern at the project-doc level; (b) supply three worked examples (Duch quantum critique; Tegmark decoherence argument; Occam-style critiques); (c) articulate the discipline: when a competent opposing position converges with a Map commitment, the article must distinguish the *convergence-point* from the *divergence-point* explicitly, because shared conclusion does not entail shared reasoning and importing the opposing reasoning would be a category error; (d) note the operating distinction from `bedrock-clash-vs-absorption` — that discipline handles disagreement-as-bedrock; this one handles agreement-as-coincidence; (e) honest limitation: the pattern is currently sparsely instantiated at three cases; if no fourth instance accrues across the catalogue's ongoing engagements, the pattern may not warrant its own discipline. Project-doc placement preferred (concepts/ at 229/250 = 92%; apex/ at ~24/20 over informal cap). Estimated scope: 600–1,000 words. Parallel framing to `project/bedrock-clash-vs-absorption.md`. Tenet alignment: methodological. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-02]] and [[research/wlodzislaw-duch-consciousness-2026-05-02]].

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write project doc on cross-review-vs-pessimistic-review severity-register distinction
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-02 (Medium Priority). The 2026-05-02 06:55 UTC `voids/recognition-void` cross-review found one medium issue (clean integration); the 09:00 UTC pessimistic-review on the *same article* found six issues including two high-severity (Capgras-prosopagnosia double-dissociation over-read; "phenomenologically silent" contradicted by TOT/aha-moments/déjà vu phenomena). The empirical pattern — that cross-review and pessimistic-review surface defects at *different severity registers* on the same content — has now been visible across multiple article pairs (recognition-void 2026-05-02; cognitive-phenomenology 2026-05-01). The pattern is not yet articulated at project-doc level. Project-doc would (a) name the severity-register distinction: cross-review surfaces *integration-fidelity* concerns (does the article reciprocate sibling links? does it match the new article's framing? does it preserve the cluster's reciprocal structure?); pessimistic-review surfaces *content-defensibility* concerns (does the article overclaim? does it import contested causal claims as load-bearing? does it treat contested positions as established? does it engage conflicting phenomenology?); (b) supply the recognition-void worked example (clean cross-review + six-issue pessimistic-review on same article in same day); (c) articulate the discipline (run both review types when an article is being substantively engaged; do not let a clean cross-review preempt a pessimistic-review); (d) note the operating distinction from the four existing methodology disciplines — this is a *review-discipline* note operating at a different level, not a *fifth* methodological discipline; (e) honest limitation: the severity-register distinction is most defensible when an article is being substantively engaged; for stability-confirmation reviews on mature articles, the distinction may collapse into either-review-equivalence. Project-doc placement (concepts/ at 229/250 = 92% argues against concept-page placement). Estimated scope: 800–1,200 words. Parallel framing to existing project docs but at review-discipline scope rather than editorial-methodology scope. Tenet alignment: methodological. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-02]].

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write topic article on Reconsolidation as Selection-Window
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-09 (Medium Priority). The reconsolidation framing in `topics/consciousness-and-memory.md` line 122 names a load-bearing Map argument — "this is where non-physical consciousness contributes: not replacing neural reconsolidation mechanisms but providing the directional input that makes reconstruction purposive rather than merely stochastic" — but is currently a paragraph within a broader article rather than a topic-tier subject explainer. Reconsolidation is the cleanest empirically-anchored *selection-window* in the consciousness-physics interface: the pre-decoherence Quantum Zeno window (12 orders of magnitude too fast) is mechanism-debated, the moment-of-experience selection in real-time perception is harder to operationalise empirically, but reconsolidation provides a hours-long-scale window where (a) the lability is empirically established (Nader, Schafe, Le Doux 2000), (b) the multiple-outcome openness is real (Kube et al. 2025 directional-input result), and (c) the conscious-retrieval involvement is the trigger. Article would deliver (i) the empirical signature (protein-synthesis dependence, lability window, retrieval-triggered destabilisation); (ii) the directional-input claim (Kube 2025, Nader 2000) at its actual evidential weight; (iii) the connection to forward-in-time-conscious-selection and the Map's better-developed post-decoherence selection mechanism; (iv) the personal-identity stakes (the perpetually-rewriting-self framing); (v) the empirical-falsification conditions. Topics/ at ~225/250; cap allows. Estimated scope: 2200–2700 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) gets its most empirically-anchored selection-window exhibit; Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) is engaged through the connection to post-decoherence selection mechanisms; Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds) is engaged through the singular-determination commitment. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-09]].

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write topic article on Bidirectional Interaction as Positive Selection Mechanism
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-10 (Medium Priority). The convergence argument's new line 150 assigns the bidirectional-interaction tenet a *load-bearing positive selection* role: it selects dualism from among the irreducibility-respecting alternatives (panpsychism, neutral monism, idealism, Madhyamaka). This selection role is structurally important across the Map but is not currently articulated as its own topic-tier subject. Article would name the bidirectional-interaction tenet's specifically-selective role ("requires two genuinely distinct domains in causal contact: incompatible with idealism, under-specified on neutral monism, more naturally expressed in dualist than panpsychist vocabulary") and the supporting arguments that establish bidirectional interaction independently of the irreducibility convergence (the empirical-mental-causation evidence — placebo effects, choking under pressure, motor-control evidence — and the philosophical arguments from agent-causation literature). The article would replace silent appeals to the tenet across the catalogue with a single referential anchor. Topics/ at ~225/250; cap allows. Estimated scope: 1500–2200 words. ### ✓ 2026-06-03: Engage the indexical-thesis-as-itself-a-tenet challenge in Tenet 4 (Madhyamaka critique)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From pessimistic-2026-05-11 "The Buddhist Philosopher (Nagarjuna)" critique. Tenet 4's primary load-bearing argument (the indexical objection — "why am I *this* branch rather than any of the others?") rests on a substantialist conception of self where "I" exists independently of branch-assignment. The Madhyamaka tradition, which the Map's `concepts/Indian-eastern-philosophical-engagement.md` material engages elsewhere, has spent two millennia deconstructing exactly this conception: the "I" in "why am I *this* one" is not a self-existing thing whose branch-assignment is mysterious, but an aggregate of conditions that includes the branch. The reviewer's diagnosis: Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits) just made the page symmetric on parsimony — it should bind here too: how do we know our intuition that "why am I this one" is meaningful is not the same kind of folk-metaphysical reflex that "why does the falling stone want the ground" was? Tenet 4 currently makes the indexical-thesis-is-meaningful the load-bearing premise without acknowledging it is itself a substantive metaphysical claim that the Map has not independently argued for. Refine `obsidian/tenets/tenets.md` Tenet 4 to add a ~80–120 word acknowledgement: the indexical thesis presupposes a non-deflationary conception of personal identity that the Map endorses on independent grounds (the no-deflationary-self route would require accepting that "why am I this one" is a confused question, which the Map's commitments do not permit); name the Madhyamaka challenge as the bedrock disagreement (Mode Three in [[direct-refutation-discipline]] terms — not in-framework defect but framework-boundary disagreement); cross-link to whatever material the catalogue already has on Buddhist no-self engagement. Apply [[direct-refutation-discipline]] — this is honest Mode Three engagement, naming the boundary rather than dismissing the critique. Convergent with the existing P3 task on Sebens-Carroll self-locating uncertainty (which addresses the MWI-friendly Bayesian resolution that the Map rejects on independent grounds); this task addresses the deflationary-self resolution that is symmetric. ~100 words touched. Tenet alignment: Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds), methodological / [[direct-refutation-discipline]]. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-11]] § The Buddhist Philosopher.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write project doc on the delocalisation discipline as a named methodology pattern
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: From optimistic-2026-05-11 "Ideas for Later". Today's optimistic review identified the *delocalisation discipline* as a structural pattern present across the catalogue: when a strong claim would require evidence the article does not supply, the load-bearing claim is shifted to a tractable adjacent claim. The review surfaced four exhibits in today's quartet alone: PVR delocalises "metaethics is solved" → "metaethics has a coherent home in consciousness"; quantum-biology delocalises "burden has shifted" → "categorical objection has weakened"; IIT critique delocalises "interactionism is proven" → "IIT inherits an epistemic problem"; cognitive-distinctiveness delocalises "great apes are/are not conscious" → "consciousness-dependent capacities track the gap". The pattern is named in the review but not yet anchored at a referential point. Create `obsidian/project/delocalisation-discipline.md` (project-doc placement, alongside `evidential-status-discipline`, `direct-refutation-discipline`, `coherence-inflation-countermeasures`, `common-cause-null`) defining: (a) the discipline — when an inference would over-reach the available evidence, locate the strongest evidentially-supported adjacent claim and make *that* the load-bearing inference; (b) when to deploy — boundary cases between in-framework engagement and bedrock disagreement; (c) the audit checklist for refine-draft and deep-review skills to apply when touching contested-evidence pages; (d) catalogue at least six exhibits across the corpus (today's four plus yesterday's baseline-cognition / the-binding-problem pair); (e) cross-links to `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` (the evidential-status sibling), `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]` (delocalisation is often the right response to Mode Three boundary-disagreement cases), `[[common-cause-null]]` (structural cousin — discount-of-evidence rather than relocation-of-claim), `[[concession-convergence]]` (concession-convergence tracks where opponents have already delocalised). Estimated 1500–2200 words; project-doc placement (no section-cap constraint). Tenet alignment: methodological. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-11]] "Ideas for Later".

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write topic page `q3-as-coherent-dualism` mapping the under-discussed quadrant
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review (evening — 2026-05-11c). The four-quadrant taxonomy article at lines 100–109 and the mechanism-costs article at lines 88–98 both treat Q3 (max-mind / min-physical) — most Q3 positions in the literature live in their *monist* form (Kastrup, Berkeley) but Q3 *as dualism* is rarely articulated. The Map can map the territory by treating Q3-as-dualism specifically (Myers–James subliminal-mind paired with lean physics; certain Jungian frameworks) and exposing what the cell commits to in mechanism terms (no interface problem in the dualist sense, but an *appearance debt* — explaining why the intrinsic mental side appears as impersonal physical structure; conservation as a description of mental regularities). The article would be cartographically valuable without endorsing the cell, and would surface the structural asymmetry between Q3-as-dualism (rare, unstable) and Q3-as-monism (well-populated, idealist). Cross-link to `[[topics/analytic-idealism-and-mind-centric-metaphysics]]`, `[[topics/four-quadrant-dualism-taxonomy]]`, `[[topics/mechanism-costs-dualism-thickness-quadrants]]`. Estimated scope: 2,400–3,000 words; topics/ placement. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism); cartographic.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write topic article "The Nirvanic Brain-Coupled-Qubit Architecture and Its Limits"
- **Type**: expand-topic

Target section: topics/ (240/250, room available — but tight). Estimated scope: 2,000–2,500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — first concrete experimental programme).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Write article on Ramanujan as a cross-tradition phenomenology-of-reception exhibit
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-25d.md (High Priority). Ramanujan is currently invoked in two cluster articles as a striking bridge case but never developed in his own right: `comparative-phenomenology-of-mathematical-insight.md` §"The Convergence Beneath Variation" names him as "a bridge case... trained within Indian mathematical traditions yet producing results recognised as profound by Western mathematicians, his phenomenology of reception—results arriving in dreams and visions—neither fits the Greek visual model nor the classical Indian constructive model cleanly," and `consciousness-and-mathematics.md` §"Ramanujan and the Phenomenology of Reception" treats him in one paragraph. He is the cluster's single best *concrete* data point against the post-hoc-confabulation objection that `comparative-phenomenology` names as the eliminativist's strongest counter — a documented case where reception-phenomenology (vision, certainty without derivation) and cross-tradition transmissibility (results checkable and confirmed by Hardy and the wider Western mathematical community) coincide. A standalone article would: (a) document the reported phenomenology (results "received" via the goddess Namagiri in dreams; certainty preceding any derivation; the implausibility of brute mechanical search for the infinite-series and continued-fraction results); (b) treat the Hardy collaboration as the external-validation hinge — the results were checkable, many were correct, some were wrong, and the *pattern* of reliability is the explanandum; (c) explicitly engage the deflationary/confabulation reading (selective memory, retrospective narrative shaping, survivorship bias in which "visions" are remembered) and state honestly what the case can and cannot establish; (d) keep all claims at the phenomenological-report tier — "tracked mathematical truth with remarkable reliability" must NOT be upgraded to a mechanism claim per [[evidential-status-discipline]]; (e) cross-link reciprocally to `comparative-phenomenology`, `consciousness-and-mathematics`, `aesthetics-and-consciousness` (aesthetic filtration), `incubation-effect-and-unconscious-processing`, and `creative-consciousness`. Estimated scope: medium (~2,000 words). Tenet alignment: Dualism (irreducible insight resisting computational search) and Bidirectional Interaction (insight producing checkable physical proofs). The article's value is as a worked exhibit holding the line on register-separation in the single domain most tempting to over-claim. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-25d.md` (High Priority).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Research Berridge wanting/liking dissociation as value-in-mechanism fork empirical anchor
- **Type**: research-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-17b.md (High Priority). The `apex/moral-architecture-of-consciousness.md` §"The Open Frontier: Value in the Mechanism" (lines 131–141) cites Berridge's wanting/liking dissociation (line 139) as one of four discriminating tests between the value-blind and value-sensitive selection options: "Berridge's dissociation of dopaminergic 'wanting' from hedonic 'liking' provides a discriminating test: if selection is denominated in felt quality, liking rather than wanting should determine which outcome consciousness selects when the two conflict." Berridge is the only one of the four discriminating tests with a substantial empirical-programme literature specifying experimental designs that could discriminate between the two fork-options. A research-topic pass would document Berridge's empirical programme in detail: (a) Robinson & Berridge 1993 incentive-salience theory; (b) Berridge & Robinson 1998 wanting-vs-liking dissociation; (c) Berridge 2009 (cited in apex); (d) Berridge & Kringelbach 2015 pleasure neuroscience; (e) recent 2020s neuroimaging work on the dissociation (Berridge & Kringelbach 2024 if available; ventral pallidum hedonic hotspot work); (f) the discriminating-experiment designs (preference-reversal protocols where wanting and liking diverge; addiction phenomenology as the canonical case of wanting-without-liking; depression phenomenology as the canonical case of liking-without-wanting; deep-brain-stimulation cases that dissociate the two); (g) identify the matching catalogue articles ([[concepts/valence]] if exists, [[concepts/attentional-economics]], [[topics/valence-and-conscious-selection]], [[concepts/consciousness-value-connection]], [[concepts/moral-responsibility]]); (h) specify what observations would *fail* the value-sensitive-selection prediction (preference-reversal results showing wanting tracks selection independent of liking would be a partial-meeting of the value-blind option). Estimated research scope: ~1.5–2 hours; output ~1,500–2,500 words in `research/berridge-wanting-liking-{date}.md`. Tenet alignment: Bidirectional Interaction (Tenet 3) at mechanism-investigation level; [[mqi-empirical-fragility]] / [[framework-stage-calibration]] at methodological level — the research note should explicitly specify falsification conditions for the value-sensitive-selection option. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-17b.md` (High Priority).

[[topics/recovery-order-asymmetry-as-interface-evidence]] names a four-member signature family — (1) hysteresis of state transition, (2) recovery-order reversal across mechanisms, (3) abrupt reconnection vs gradual onset, (4) direction-decoupled timing — that is currently defined inside the topic article and referenced as a named seam class from [[apex/self-concealing-interface]], but has no canonical concept node of its own. ### ✓ 2026-06-03: Fix DeBrota et al. (2021) author-list citation defect in topics/pragmatist-quantum-foundations-and-the-agent.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /pessimistic-review 2026-06-03 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-03-agent-shaped-hole-cluster]]). WEB-VERIFIED defect (publisher of record: APS Phys. Rev. A 104, 022207, 13 Aug 2021; arXiv:2012.14397). The Born-coherence/urgleichung paper is attributed to "DeBrota, Fuchs & Schack" but **Schack is NOT an author** — correct authors are **DeBrota, J. B., Fuchs, C. A., Pienaar, J. L., & Stacey, B. C.** Famous-name substitution ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]): Schack is the canonical "S" of the founding Caves-Fuchs-Schack QBism trio, so the wrong name is highly plausible. Title / venue / volume(104) / issue(2) / article-number(022207) are all CORRECT — fix ONLY the author list. Three occurrences: (1) Reference #2 (~line 164) — replace "& Schack" with ", Pienaar, J. L., & Stacey, B. C."; (2) in-text ~line 54 "DeBrota, Fuchs & Schack 2021" → "DeBrota et al. 2021"; (3) in-text ~line 96 "DeBrota, Fuchs, and Schack (2021)" → "DeBrota et al. (2021)". ⚠ DO NOT touch the Caves-Fuchs-Schack (2002) [ref #1] or Fuchs-Mermin-Schack (2014) [ref #5] references — Schack is correctly present on BOTH of those; this is a targeted single-paper fix, not a global Schack sweep. While in the file, OPTIONAL low-priority: the Healey section's de-psychologising disjunction leans on Tenet 1 for one of its two legs — per [[direct-refutation-discipline]] keep the in-framework boundary-drawing leg (functionalist-style "agent" indeterminacy) separate from the tenet-register "real fact about consciousness" leg, natural prose, no mode labels. ### ✓ 2026-06-03: Close the van Inwagen life→consciousness gap on the biological route in consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-composition.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-03 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), Sections 3.1 & weakness #5 — the ONE genuinely new finding from the Gemini leg (the rest CONVERGED with the ChatGPT+Claude tasks above; see this review's `## Verification Notes`). The biological route (van Inwagen) in the convergence argument treats van Inwagen's organicism as a "route to consciousness," but van Inwagen's actual composition criterion is **metabolic/homeostatic/teleological life-unity, NOT phenomenal consciousness**. The article relies on an undefended bridge premise: that *all and only living things are conscious* (biopsychism/panbiotism). Plants, fungi, and single-celled organisms are living composites on van Inwagen's view yet are not phenomenally conscious subjects in the sense the unity/interaction argument needs — so the biological route reaches *life-unity*, and only reaches *consciousness* via an unstated, contestable identification. This is the same "convergence only after redescription / equivocation on 'consciousness'" structure the ChatGPT+Claude legs flagged for the IIT and Merricks routes (the convergence-calibration P2 above), but applied specifically to the van Inwagen leg — so COORDINATE with that task (it is the natural home for this if the refine touches the convergence framing; keep as a distinct sub-point so it isn't lost). FIX (length-neutral): where the biological route is introduced, either (a) explicitly state and defend/qualify the life→consciousness bridge (note that van Inwagen himself defends a *materialist* ontology of persons — do not recruit him as a consciousness-criterion proponent without caveat, cf. the parallel McQueen & Tsuchiya living-author-misattribution P1 above), or (b) recast the route as reaching *organic/causal unity* that the Map then argues is consciousness-involving, marking that second step as a Map-internal move rather than van Inwagen's. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: this is most likely an honest framework-boundary / unsupported-foundational-move case (the article has not earned "life = consciousness" by van Inwagen's own standards) — reply in natural journal-quality prose per [[writing-style]], NO mode labels in the body. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] (van Inwagen = living author; verify any attributed position). Do NOT grow the article past its length threshold — coordinate with the other open composition tasks to stay length-neutral ([[outer-review-same-file-task-pileup]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Methodology proposals from outer review — source-role table, tenet-transfer ledger, post-coalescence link audit
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-03 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). The reviewer proposes three site-methodology additions (target: a project methodology doc such as `project/writing-style.md` or a new `project/` methodology note — assess-first, do NOT create a redundant page if the discipline already has a home). (1) **Source-role table**: for each external source in an argumentative article, identify the source's own thesis, what the Map uses it to support, whether the source would accept the Map's conclusion, and which premise does the extra work — this operationalises the recurring "supports vs is-compatible-with vs can-be-recruited-by" distinction (proposal 19) and overlaps the existing convergence-arguments / [[evidential-status-discipline]] machinery, so EXTEND those rather than duplicate. (2) **Tenet-transfer ledger**: when an article recruits a source for dualism/MQI/bidirectional-causation/no-many-worlds, require an explicit note of what the source does NOT support. (3) **Post-coalescence internal-link audit**: the stale "Phenomenal Non-Compositionality" reference (see the P1 citation task above) shows archive/coalesce operations need a final live-page check for old titles, old slugs, and duplicate references — this connects to known code-level defects [[coalesce-stale-hugo-duplicate-urls]] and [[archival_link_rot]], so frame as a methodology/checklist addition and flag the code-fix candidate for the operator rather than attempting a code change here. Keep additions concise; honour writing-style + length discipline. (Proposal 18 — a "2020s rival-literature pass" checklist — is partly discharged by the convergence/rivals refine task above; fold it into the methodology note if it adds value, otherwise note as covered.)

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Reconcile phenomenal-parts vocabulary across composition cluster (cross-review)
- **Type**: cross-review
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-03 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). The reviewer flags a genuine cross-article tension: `consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-composition` (and `composition-and-consciousness`) lean on phenomenal NON-compositionality ("experience cannot be composed"), while `[[unity-of-consciousness]]` treats experiences as PARTS of larger experiences (a mereological relation among experiences). Both can be true only if the cluster distinguishes **phenomenal part-whole structure** (experiences having internal structure) from **composition out of independently existing physical/experiential subjects** (subject-summing). Add a reciprocal clarifying note to `unity-of-consciousness` ("experiences are parts of larger experiences" does not by itself imply subject-summing or ordinary mereological composition) and ensure the composition articles state the distinction. Also: post-2026-06-01-coalescence, `composition-and-consciousness` (concept primer) now DUPLICATES much of the target topic article's van Inwagen/Merricks/McQueen dialectic — decide the division of labour (concept page = navigational primer, topic page = long-form argument) and de-duplicate accordingly; do NOT have both carry independent full arguments ([[two-binding-problem-articles-same-title]] is a related "same dialectic, two pages" pattern). Length-aware on all touched files; reciprocate load-bearing wikilinks ([[archival_link_rot]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Repair the Many-Worlds and Occam paragraphs in consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-composition.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-03 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro). (a) The "No Many Worlds" paragraph (line 119, verbatim) claims that under MWI "every possible composition obtains in some branch, trivialising the SCQ." The reviewer's objection is sound: an Everettian can still ask the composition question branch-relative (given a branch's physical ontology, when do its entities compose a further object?). MWI may complicate personal identity and actuality but does not obviously trivialise mereology. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit — either rebut the branch-relative reading on its own terms, or honestly mark this as a downstream Map-internal tenet concern (No-Many-Worlds, Tenet 4) rather than a *consequence* of the composition argument; reply in natural journal-quality prose per [[writing-style]], NO mode labels in body. (b) The Occam paragraph (line ~113/Relation-to-Site-Perspective) treats universalism/nihilism's greater simplicity as failing to "account for the distinctive status of conscious beings" — but that is not yet an argument against them (universalists/nihilists can call conscious beings distinctive fusions / personwise-arranged simples). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits). Also flag the MQI link (line ~113 cluster): the composition argument does not by itself support minimal quantum interaction — label it a downstream Map hypothesis, not a result. Length-neutral.

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Repair the Bayne & Chalmers unity taxonomy + engage Bayne (2018) rebuttal in consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-composition.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-03 (Claude Opus 4.8), Dimension 4 finding (4)/(5). Two linked defects in the Unity Argument section (line ~105). (a) **Taxonomy error / internal inconsistency:** the article says the unity argument "appears across all three dimensions of unity identified by Bayne and Chalmers — synchronic, diachronic, and subject." Bayne & Chalmers's (2003) signature contribution is *subsumptive* unity (alongside objectual, spatial, subject); "synchronic/diachronic/subject" is NOT their characteristic taxonomy, and the Map's OWN [[unity-of-consciousness]] article states it more accurately — an internal inconsistency to reconcile. WEB-VERIFY the correct B&C taxonomy against consc.net/papers/unity.pdf before editing (not independently re-verified at processing time — see this review's Verification Notes), then make the focus article match the unity-of-consciousness page. (b) **Missing direct rebuttal:** Bayne (2018) "Problems with Unity of Consciousness Arguments for Substance Dualism" (in *The Blackwell Companion to Substance Dualism*, pp. 208–225) is the most direct rebuttal to the article's unity argument and is omitted — add at least a cite-and-engage paragraph applying the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] (do not merely name-drop it). Also (Dimension 4 finding 6) the article presents Hasker's emergent dualism as straightforwardly supporting the Map, but Hasker's soul is *generated by and dependent on* the brain (closer to strong emergentism) and other substance dualists judge it to face its own combination-style problem — qualify the line ~107 framing. Length-neutral / length-aware. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and [[ai_citation_metadata_unreliable]] (web-verify Bayne 2018 pagination/venue before asserting).

### ✓ 2026-06-03: Calibrate the convergence thesis and add a "strongest rivals" pass to consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-composition.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-03, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini] — UPGRADED P2→P1 by /combine-outer-reviews 2026-06-03. All three legs independently reached the SAME verdict that the headline "three independent routes converge on consciousness" is a SELECTION ARTEFACT, real "only after abstraction"/"only after redescription" — the highest-confidence finding of this cycle. ChatGPT: "the convergence is real only after abstraction" to organised/causally-unified/integrated systems. Claude: "a selection artefact compounded by redescription" (Dimensions 1/3/5). Gemini: "this purported convergence is entirely an artifact of editorial manipulation and selection bias... equivocation on the term 'consciousness'" (Section 3). The headline claim (line 33, verbatim) — "three of the most prominent answers to the SCQ independently converge on consciousness" — overstates the field: the standard mereology map (universalism, nihilism, restricted composition, brutal composition, composition-as-identity, deflationism) does not treat van Inwagen/Merricks/McQueen-Tsuchiya as *the* three dominant answers, and the convergence is real "only after abstraction" to organised/causally-unified/integrated systems (consciousness one candidate marker, not the only one). The article already carries a strong selection-effect hedge (line 83) and a non-forcing disclaimer (line 33) — PRESERVE those; this is calibration of the *headline framing*, not removal of the argument. Reframe toward the reviewer's weaker formulation ("restricted-composition strategies place life, consciousness, causal irreducibility, or integrated information near the composite/aggregate boundary"). Then add coverage of the strongest live rivals the article omits: universalism (consciousness marks a special composite, not the boundary of composition), nihilism (simples-arranged-personwise paraphrase), brutal composition (denies any non-arbitrary boundary need exist), and the naturalistic restricted-composition rivals — Bird 2023 ("Restricted Composition is Information Compression", *Phil. Quarterly* 73(3):677–700, VERIFIED) and Beni 2025 ("Constraining the Compression: Thermodynamic Depth and Composition", *Phil. Quarterly* 75(2):396, advance pqae019, VERIFIED) — which explain non-arbitrary composition WITHOUT making consciousness fundamental. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: identify whether each rival can be defeated on its own terms, whether the Map must instead name an unsupported foundational move, or whether the disagreement is honestly at the framework boundary; reply in natural journal-quality prose per [[writing-style]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. Do NOT expose mode labels in the article body — keep the classification in the changelog entry. Consider the reviewer's "what would weaken this argument?" box (candidate defeaters listed in the review). **CONVERGENT + EXTENDED by the 2026-06-03 Claude Opus 4.8 leg** (`reviews/outer-review-2026-06-03-claude-opus-4-8.md`, Dimensions 1, 3 & 5), which independently reached the same "selection artefact compounded by redescription" verdict and adds TWO rivals the ChatGPT leg did not name explicitly, both of which the refine should cover: (i) **metaontological DEFLATIONISM (Thomasson's *Ontology Made Easy*; Hirsch's quantifier variance)** — Claude calls this "the load-bearing objection": if the SCQ is shallow/verbal there is no deep fact for ANY criterion to track and the convergence dissolves into a non-question; an article premised on the SCQ being "one of the deepest in metaphysics" (line 33) must engage this in its proponents' framing. (ii) **Korman's conservative realism** (*Objects: Nothing Out of the Ordinary*, OUP 2015) — defends ordinary-object realism with NO consciousness criterion while rejecting both eliminativism and permissivism, a direct counterexample to the implicit claim that a non-arbitrary restricted answer requires consciousness; pairs naturally with the Bird/Beni compression rivals already named above. Claude also flags (Dimension 3) that the criterion's most natural home is **constitutive panpsychism / cosmopsychism (Goff; Nagasawa & Wager; Albahari) — a MONISM, the chief rival to dualism — which the article neither acknowledges nor rebuts**; this overlaps the separate panpsychism-monism P2 cross-review task below, so coordinate (do not double-write the monism section). LENGTH-AWARE: article is ~3000 body words near the topics soft threshold — adding a rivals section risks the 4000 hard ceiling; condense elsewhere to stay length-neutral, or if genuinely forced over, flag as a human length-decision rather than auto-condensing the calibration content ([[refine-then-condense-same-session-churn]]). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and the corpus convergence-arguments discipline (independence / methodological-diversity / against-the-grain).
