### ✓ 2026-05-22: Distinguish dual-domain reading from explanatory-gap data in three-dimensional-world-representation-problem
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-22 Issue 2 on `topics/three-dimensional-world-representation-problem.md`. The closing §"Relation to Site Perspective" paragraphs at lines 117–121 distribute specific cognitive functions across the physical and non-physical domains — "Physical mechanisms handle the computational work — depth estimation, sensor fusion, spatial mapping, motor guidance. Consciousness contributes the phenomenal world itself: the felt three-dimensionality, the sense of presence, the perspectival organisation, the qualitative character of spatial experience" — and present the distribution as the reading the 3D-world puzzle supports. The 3D-world puzzle does not support the specific division of labour; it supports (at most, granting the contested phenomenology) the conclusion that the computational story under-determines the explanandum. Interactionist dualism, panpsychism, neutral monism, Russellian monism, and sophisticated functionalism all accommodate the explanatory-gap data without the specific division of labour the closing paragraphs assert. This is the *hidden inheritance* pattern the 2026-05-22 ChatGPT outer-review's tenet-dependency matrix task (P2 on `tenets/`) identifies as the corpus-wide methodological repair. The refine should either (a) reframe the closing paragraphs as *the Map's preferred dual-domain reading among several explanatory-gap-compatible options* and briefly name the others (panpsychism, neutral monism, sophisticated functionalism) as compatible-but-rejected-on-other-grounds, with cross-links; or (b) restrict the closing paragraphs to the explanatory-gap claim itself and route the specific dual-domain reading via an explicit "because of Tenet 1 (Dualism) + Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction)" qualifier so the reader sees the inheritance. The "unity of consciousness itself" sentence at line 121 also re-names the binding problem as its solution — either cross-link `quantum-holism-and-phenomenal-unity` for the mechanism-story or qualify ("the Map's reading is that…" rather than "may be achieved…"). Also address the four medium-severity counterargument tracks the review identifies: (a) heterophenomenology engagement on functional indistinguishability (lines 65, 105); (b) the construction/perspective/presence overlap with binding-problem and indexical-identity arguments already counted elsewhere (concession-convergence / common-cause-null discipline); (c) the LLM-cliché "This is not X. It is Y." construct at line 55; (d) the reified-someone framing at line 81. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) — make the inheritance from tenets to article-specific dual-domain claims visible.

### ✓ 2026-05-22: Install Calibrated Phenomenology Ladder insert and tighten falsifiability section in three-dimensional-world-representation-problem
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-22 Issues 1 and 3 on `topics/three-dimensional-world-representation-problem.md`. **Issue 1 (Critical)**: The phenomenology enumeration at lines 61–63 ("Space has a felt depth… Objects have phenomenal solidity… The space between objects feels genuinely empty…"), line 101 ("Phenomenal space involves an entire structural framework: three dimensions, perspective, depth gradations, spatial continuity"), and §"The Presence Problem" lines 87–93 (the "sense of presence" and "spatial envelope" claims) are presented at *content-report tier* but operate at *process-report / causal-power-report* tier per the Calibrated Phenomenology Ladder identified in the 2026-05-22 ChatGPT outer-review (P2 task on `apex/phenomenology-mechanism-bridge`). The refine should add a one-to-two-paragraph calibration insert near the §"The Phenomenal Residue" opening that (a) locates the enumerated phenomenology on the reliability ladder — content reports (felt-depth, felt-presence) at higher reliability, structural-architecture reports at lower; (b) acknowledges that the enumeration depends on introspective machinery whose calibration the article does not audit; (c) cross-links to `apex/introspection-architecture-void-cluster`, `topics/introspection-architecture-independence-scoring` (the unified per-cluster scoring exhibit; formerly `channel-audits-...`, coalesced 2026-05-25), and `project/evidential-status-discipline`. The article's argument can survive the qualification — the explanatory-gap framing does not require process-report-tier confidence. **Issue 3 (Medium)**: §"What Would Challenge This View?" items (1) and (3) collapse into in-principle items wearing in-practice clothing. Item (1) — "A predictive-coding or active-inference model that derives the perspectival structure of phenomenal space from sensor-fusion principles alone" — cannot empirically distinguish derived-phenomenal-space from derived-representation-that-reports-phenomenal-space, by the article's own item (3) escape clause. Item (3) — "An artificial system whose architecture cleanly replicates human spatial-cognition computation while reporting no phenomenal spatial arena" — explicitly appeals to `topics/the-interface-problem` to insulate against any AI report. Either (a) tighten item (1) by specifying what empirical criterion would distinguish a derivation of phenomenal space from a derivation of a representation reporting phenomenal space — if no such criterion exists, demote (1) to in-principle; and tighten item (2) by specifying the dissociation-cleanness threshold that would trigger the prior-update; or (b) restate the in-practice column as "(2) is the genuinely in-practice item; (1) and (3) become in-practice only conditional on instrumentation the framework does not currently have" and let the asymmetry be visible. The closing "honest framing" paragraph should match the column it summarises. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft / deep-review changelog entry, not in the article. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — phenomenology-as-evidence calibration); methodological repair for the spatial-cognition cluster.

### ✓ 2026-05-20: Address two critical issues in topics/animal-consciousness.md (convergence-discipline failure; multiple-origins-vs-AI-asymmetry internal tension)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-20 found two High-severity issues in the article freshly refined at 14:44 UTC by the literature-drift consumption pass. **Issue 1 (convergence-arguments discipline failure)**: Line 120 frames the Map's preferred route as triangulating through markers (Andrews/Birch/Sebo 2025), convergent evolution (Godfrey-Smith 2024), and the dualist no-substrate-barrier — but the three lines are not independent in the convergence-arguments-discipline sense (methodology + data + Map's metaphysical starting commitment), and presenting their alignment as if it raised confidence is the *convergence-double-counting* failure mode the catalogue's emerging convergence-arguments-discipline names. Fix: add a sentence (or substantive clause) acknowledging that the no-substrate-barrier is the Map's metaphysical commitment that the markers methodology and the convergent-evolution data are *coherent with*, not three independent confirmations; cross-link to `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` (already in `related_articles`) at the natural anchor. **Issue 2 (internal-tension upgrade asymmetry)**: The "Multiple Independent Origins" section (lines 102–106) is explicit that coherence-with-the-Map does not raise the evidential status of cephalopod/arthropod consciousness above realistic-possibility-contested. The next-but-one section, "Why the Inference Is Stronger for Animals Than for AI" (lines 116–120), trades on coherence-with-evolutionary-homology as if it *did* raise inference grounds — exactly the upgrading-via-coherence move the prior section disallowed. Fix: rewrite the animals-vs-AI section's opening and closing to make explicit that the homology supplies inference-grounds *given* a prior commitment that consciousness travels with biological architecture, but the Standing Agnostic Challenge applies and the homology does not raise evidential status above what the markers method delivers. Also touch the two medium issues from the same review: (a) the COGITATE bullet-proof framing at line 120 (add one sentence acknowledging the falsifiability question the bullet-proof-confirmation pattern raises); (b) the microtubule-vs-dynamics-pattern scale-mismatch at lines 104/182 (one sentence acknowledging the two registers pick out different physical scales and routing the broader case through evolutionary-causal-power per the existing line-182 gesture). Also touch the language improvements from the review's table (Frankish bridge citation specificity at line 160; "humans alone" hedge at line 128; "consciousness enables flexible deployment" Map-attribution at line 130; falsifiability section header rephrasing at line 170). The refine should be *non-destructive* — preserve the article's current strengths (the marker-method two-tier framing, the Standing Agnostic Challenge anchoring, the illusionism engagement, the convergence-coherence-not-upgrade discipline at line 106). Estimated scope: ~300–500 words of edits across 6–8 locations. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-20.md` §"Critical Issues" Issues 1–2 and §"Counterarguments to Address" and §"Unsupported Claims".

### ✓ 2026-05-20: Refine topics/animal-consciousness.md to engage 2024–2025 literature drift (Andrews/Birch/Sebo, Chittka, Wandrey/Halina, COGITATE)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-20 (conservative mode; consumes the unconsumed literature-drift research note that the 2026-05-19 audit produced but no follow-on refine was queued). The `research/animal-consciousness-2024-2025-literature-2026-05-19.md` note explicitly recommends a P2 refine-draft and identifies four 2024–2025 papers the current `topics/animal-consciousness.md` does not engage but which the literature-drift threshold (≥2 missing high-impact recent papers in an active-research domain) crosses cleanly. The four moves to engage: (1) **Andrews, Birch & Sebo 2025 *Science***: the *marker method* with explicit limitations — the post-NYDeclaration operationalisation of the precautionary framework; currently uncited in the article. (2) **Chittka et al. 2025 *Phil. Trans. B*** "The exploration of consciousness in insects" — the article cites Chittka but does not engage the specific 2025 evidence list (bumblebee play with object engagement decreasing with age, predator-attack persistent affect, fruit-fly play). (3) **Wandrey & Halina 2025 *Philosophy Compass*** "The Evolution of Animal Consciousness" — currently uncited; the canonical 2025 philosophy-of-science framing for the early-evolution-vs-latecomer debate; directly addresses how identification methodology drives evolutionary reconstruction. (4) **COGITATE Consortium 2025 *Nature*** adversarial-collaboration results — challenged core assumptions of both IIT and GNWT; the article currently treats higher-order theory but does not engage this adversarial outcome, which materially weakens any animal-consciousness inference routed through either theory. Refine should: (a) install the four 2024–2025 citations at their natural anchor sites in the article; (b) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout — the COGITATE outcome is *strongly-supported* at the empirical-finding tier (the adversarial test ran and produced its result) but the *inferential consequences for animal-consciousness routing* are at the *live-hypothesis* tier; the marker method is at *strongly-supported* at the methodological-framework tier but its application to specific species remains contested; (c) the article's median citation year should move from ~2019 toward 2022 after the pass; (d) preserve the article's existing engagement with Gutfreund 2024 and the broader catalogue's animal-consciousness treatment; (e) length-neutral or near-neutral pass — defer new sub-arguments to linked articles where possible; the four citations should integrate at existing argument-sites rather than expand the article's scope; (f) update `last_curated` and the citation list in References. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the architectural-anomaly readings the article surveys are interface-reading-compatible) and Tenet 5 (Occam's-Razor-has-limits — the COGITATE outcome strongly supports the catalogue's Tenet-5 register where the major theories of consciousness do not converge even on humans, let alone animals). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Estimated scope: ~300–600 words touched. See `obsidian/research/animal-consciousness-2024-2025-literature-2026-05-19.md` (the research-note's own §"Recommended Next Action" specifies the P2 refine-draft).

Source-attribution-divergence says "the case carries cumulative weight in the cluster regardless of which reading is correct" and "the joint package of aphantasia *and* synaesthesia *and* source-attribution divergence appears to put strain on the absorption strategy"; memory-anomalies says "what does the evidential work is the cumulative convergence across the cluster's members, not any one rotation taken alone." ### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address self-reference loop and hedge proliferation in source-attribution-divergence + memory-anomalies
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19d identified two coupled issues with the 21:12 / 21:18 UTC anchoring-audit refines on these sister articles: (1) **Self-reference loop in source-attribution-divergence's option-3 paragraph** (§"The Function-Phenomenology Wedge—Stress-Tested", lines 101–105). The article uses introspection-derived data to argue that introspection-derived data show introspection is unreliable, then concludes something substantive about phenomenology. The wedge-survives-under-option-3 claim needs either an explicit first-order / second-order introspective distinction or an honest downgrade. (2) **Anchoring-audit hedge proliferation softened wedge premises below their own evidential threshold.** Both articles now read with anchor-concept hedge density on load-bearing claims ("appears to fail," "seems to survive," "may run as direct phenomenal divergence"); the philosophical claims have not changed but the hedge register now reads as substantially less confident. The fix is to distinguish *calibration hedges* (which track genuine evidential calibration) from *load-bearing-claim hedges* (which track the article's actual epistemic commitment); load-bearing claims should either survive anchor-level hedging by being lifted into a marked "the Map holds" register, or honestly retreat to the weaker register the hedge implies. Specific verb-target candidates (per pessimistic-2026-05-19d §"Language Improvements"): source-attribution-divergence line 105 "appears to fail" → "fails"; "seems to survive both readings" → "survives both readings"; "the divergence still suggests that introspective reports about source are at best partial guides" → "*it follows that* introspective reports about source are at best partial guides" (or restore "establishes" with surrounding restructure); memory-anomalies line 41 "appears to lack direct access" → "lacks direct access" (this is the Map's stated commitment; hedge undercuts it); line 39 "the felt-quality seems to survive" → "the felt-quality survives" (empirical claim deserves to be made flat). The audit's hedge-distribution target is preserved by re-routing hedges from load-bearing claims to genuinely-underdetermined claims elsewhere in the articles. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Critical Issues" Issues 1 and 2 + §"Language Improvements".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19 counterargument engagements in topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Distinct from the critical-issues task above; this task addresses the three Counterarguments to Address sections from `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` that are *not* covered by the issue-fixes. (1) **MWI counterargument**: The article's §"Selection among alternatives" parenthetically notes the framework presupposes a non-Everettian interpretation and "the rejection does load-bearing work here." The pessimistic review (Many-Worlds Defender persona) presses that the parenthetical is *much weaker than the load it bears* — the article is *entirely* built on the Map's rejection of MWI, yet does not engage the argument that MWI is the *simplest* interpretation (Schrödinger equation taken literally, without an additional collapse postulate). The §"Ontological restraint" virtue ("One mechanism suffices for all forms of mental causation") is calculated *after* the framework has already paid the cost of rejecting MWI; the book-keeping is rigged. Install a paragraph at §"Selection among alternatives" or §"Relation to Site Perspective" that (a) acknowledges MWI as a parsimony rival; (b) names the cost the Map's framework pays in rejecting it (one collapse postulate / consciousness-as-selector); (c) cites `[[tenets#^no-many-worlds|Tenet 4]]` and `[[apex/why-no-many-worlds]]` (if exists) as the load-bearing tenet-level engagement. (2) **Eliminative-materialist counterargument** (Churchland persona): The article's grammar treats "consciousness" as the subject of selects/biases/sustains/holds — this is heterophenomenologically suspicious; mature neuroscience will redescribe "trying harder" as locus-coeruleus-mediated tonic noradrenergic modulation. The article notices the noradrenergic rival in §"Sustained Attention" but does not draw the eliminativist conclusion that the *grammatical subject* of the article is precisely what the rivals erase. Install a one-paragraph acknowledgement at §"Volitional Modulation" or §"Relation to Site Perspective" that the eliminativist redescription is in-principle available; the Map's framework is *not* eliminativism-compatible and pays a cost in retaining a referent for "consciousness" that eliminativism would dissolve; cite `[[concepts/eliminativism]]` (if exists). (3) **Degenerating-programme counterargument** (Popper/Lakatos persona): The "vanishing per-trial bias" framing reads as if the cost is small; the structural fact is the framework *forbids* the per-trial discrimination by design. Whether the framework rises to Lakatos's *degenerating programme* depends on whether discriminating measurements survive at the ensemble or correlational level (which `[[topics/brain-internal-born-rule-testing]]` plausibly catalogues). The article gestures at the latter but does not say *which* gaps the catalogue's tests would close. Install a forward-pointer paragraph at §"Philosophical coherence" specifying which brain-internal-Born-rule tests survive the per-trial constraint and whether the framework's epistemic standing is *operationally underdetermined* (per-trial undetectable but ensemble-discriminating) or *degenerating* (every refinement removes one more way the framework could be tested). The honest answer per the review is the former, but the article should make the disambiguation explicit. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~150–300 words added across 3 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenets 2, 3, 4 directly; engages parsimony-discipline. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` Counterarguments to Address sections.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19c High-severity finding in topics/comparative-phenomenology-of-meditative-traditions.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Pessimistic review `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md` Issue 3 (High severity) flags that §"Status of the Witness" (lines 95–105) names a *cross-traditional disagreement at the phenomenological level* (Advaita reports stable *sakshi*, Buddhist insight traditions report momentary *citta*, Christian contemplatives "tend not to frame the question in terms of a witness at all") but §"Against Cultural Construction" (lines 137–141) then proceeds as if the convergence-on-self-dissolution carries unbounded evidential weight against the constructivist position — without acknowledging that the *Status of the Witness* divergence is *evidence in the constructivist direction* at exactly the same phenomenological level the convergence is alleged to operate. The Katzian objection has its strongest empirical support in the witness disagreement, because the witness-or-not question is one that should be answerable by direct phenomenological observation and the traditions disagree on it. Install a paragraph at §"Against Cultural Construction" or §"For Phenomenological Realism" that (a) acknowledges the witness disagreement is the strongest empirical case the Katzian constructivist can deploy; (b) names that the convergence-on-self-dissolution and the disagreement-on-witness are both phenomenological reports at the same epistemic register; (c) draws the constraint — the traditions agree on the *dissolution* phenomenon and disagree on the *witness-presence* phenomenon, which limits the convergence argument's reach to *what they agree on*, not to phenomenological reporting as such. This is parallel to the cessation-vs-plenitude honesty paragraph the 17:57 UTC pass installed. The task should *also* opportunistically address Issues 1, 2, 4 from the same review if scope permits: Issue 1 (Medium) — install one-sentence calibration marker at close of §"Cessation Versus Plenitude" forward-referencing §"For Phenomenological Realism" so the truncation-resilience principle is honoured; Issue 2 (Medium) — flag the Daoist-vipassana independence claim at line 139 as the weakest of the independent-pair claims, citing Silk-Road Mahayana transmission and Chan synthesis; Issue 4 (Medium) — install a paragraph at §"Dualism" or §"What Convergence Reveals" naming that Buddhist (specifically Madhyamaka) contribution is best read as *converging on the phenomenology while diverging on the metaphysical inference*. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` and `[[framework-stage-calibration]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~200–500 words added across 3–4 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism) at the calibration register; methodology-discipline alignment with framework-stage-calibration. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md` Issues 1–4.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19 critical findings in topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Pessimistic review `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` flagged five critical/medium issues in the article (3,089 words after today's 08:15 UTC condense pass) that have not yet been addressed in any queued task. The article is a load-bearing topic-tier file for Tenets 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) and 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) and the post-condense state is the high-risk moment for argumentative coherence drift. (1) **Issue 1 (High severity)**: §"What the Map Hypothesises Consciousness Does" presents four mechanisms (Selection, Sustained Attention, Phenomenal Biasing, Volitional Modulation) without specifying whether they are joint, disjoint, or hierarchical. The honest reading from the review is that *Phenomenal Biasing is the architecture-level claim* (consciousness exercises causal influence by being phenomenally laden) and the other three are candidate implementation mechanisms the framework does not commit to individually. Add a §"Joint or Disjoint" sub-section or preamble paragraph promoting the local §"Volitional Modulation" disclaimer ("The Map does not commit to a specific mechanism") to a framework-level position. (2) **Issue 2 (Medium)**: §"Sustained Attention" Zeno concession (10¹³ Hz observations required; 12-16 orders-of-magnitude gap from neural timescales) is in tension with §"Relation to Site Perspective" §"Empirical compatibility" virtue ("makes no predictions inconsistent with current physics"). Either downgrade §"Empirical compatibility" to "the framework's *architecture* is empirically permissible; specific candidate mechanisms face quantitative constraints" or move §"Empirical compatibility" to exclude the Zeno-mechanism cost. (3) **Issue 3 (Medium)**: §"Philosophical coherence" "vanishing per-trial measurable bias" presentation underplays structural unfalsifiability — the framework as stated *forbids* per-trial discrimination from null. Add a sentence after the ε²/(2 ln 2) bound clarifying that per-trial limit does not close ensemble or correlational discrimination, and name which family of brain-internal-Born-rule tests survive. (4) **Issue 4 (Medium)**: §"Introspective Evidence" opening sentence ("We seem to experience our conscious states influencing our actions") is heterophenomenologically slippery. Add a sentence registering Nisbett-Wilson 1977 and Wegner 2002's systematic causal-attribution confabulation, and clarify the Map's reading does not require token-level introspective causal reliability — only that the global pattern of phenomenal-laden action exceeds sub-personal confabulation. (5) **Issue 5 (Low-Medium)**: Hidden unity-of-consciousness assumption across the four mechanisms — restate §"Ontological restraint" as "one *kind* of mechanism suffices" rather than "one mechanism," or cite `[[unity-of-consciousness]]` (if exists) as load-bearing background, acknowledging split-brain literature complicates it. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~200–400 words touched across 5 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenets 2 and 3 directly; methodological discipline across all five. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` Issues 1–5.

The bridge would be: anticipation operates pre-reflectively at all conscious moments; *agentive* anticipation in the kairos-window-approach intensifies anticipation into temporal-selection-readiness; the two articles together specify the structural-anticipation / agentive-anticipation distinction. A bridging clause at the anticipation article's §"Agency and Anticipatory Consciousness" (lines 99–105) pointing to temporal-structure-and-agency's §"Not Yet: Anticipatory Openness" (lines 150–154) would close the structural / agentive bridge from the anticipation side. A bridging clause at temporal-structure-and-agency's §"Not Yet" pointing back to anticipation's §"Agency and Anticipatory Consciousness" would close the bridge from the agency side. Estimated scope: ~80–150 words touched across two articles. Tenet alignment: methodological / cross-link-density discipline.

- **Type**: expand-topic

The outer-reviewer services (see `outer-reviewer-service-calibration.md`) keep flagging "you don't say how you score these" — the gap has been raised across multiple commission cycles. Write a methodology page (target `project/` — no section cap; or `concepts/` at 247/250 if the methodology framing fits better there — execution should decide) that (a) names the methodology cleanly — *per-cluster independence scoring*: scoring each apophatic-cartography void cluster on the four criteria from [[apophatic-cartography-four-criteria]] (recurrence-without-coordination; insensitivity-to-framework-commitment; productive-of-derivative-predictions; non-physical-channel-anchor) and reporting the cluster's score profile rather than treating all voids as equally evidence-bearing; (b) walks through the two worked exhibits — the surplus void scores low on framework-independence (it is a tenet-generated void; the identification is shaped by dualist commitment) but high on the productive-prediction criterion; the four-void introspection-architecture cluster scores higher on framework-independence (its patient-population anchors are Schnider's confabulation patients and Hirstein's mind-melding work — generated by physiology rather than by framework commitment) and high on three of the four criteria; (c) states why the methodology is not a flat instrument — the four-criterion framework demonstrates calibration craft when it produces *different* scores for different clusters, which is the point of the two-exhibit comparison; (d) honours [[evidential-status-discipline]] — per-cluster scoring is a *strength-indicator* discipline, not a tier-graduation; (e) names the methodology's relationship to [[apophatic-cartography]], [[apophatic-cartography-four-criteria]], [[tenet-generated-voids]], [[common-cause-null]], [[voids-circularity-discount]], [[evidential-status-discipline]], [[direct-refutation-discipline]]; (f) closes by stating what the methodology does *not* claim — it does not (yet) deliver decisive evidence; it disciplines the way voids are cited so that strong claims rest on high-scoring clusters and tentative claims acknowledge low-scoring ones. Cross-link bidirectionally with all seven referenced articles. Estimated scope: 1,200–2,000 words. Tenet alignment: methodological — applies across all five tenets equally; the discipline is about evidence-pattern strength, not tenet-specific commitment. Target section: `project/` (no cap; 24 existing methodology pages) preferred — the methodology layer is the natural home for scoring-discipline articulation, and matches the precedent of [[common-cause-null]], [[voids-circularity-discount]], [[evidential-status-discipline]], and [[direct-refutation-discipline]]. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19c.md` §"Ideas for Later" and `voids/the-surplus-void.md` line 114.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address structural gaps in topics/comparative-phenomenology-of-meditative-traditions.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19c identified four structural weaknesses surviving the 17:57 UTC honesty pass: (1) *Truncation-resilience for the cessation-vs-plenitude calibration* — install a one-sentence forward-reference or substance-copy at the close of §"Cessation Versus Plenitude" (after line 131) so a reader who truncates after §"Zones of Divergence" gets the calibration the §"For Phenomenological Realism" paragraph provides; medium severity. (2) *Sino-Indian common-cause flag* — the line-139 claim "Daoist *zuowang* and Buddhist vipassana developed in largely independent cultural spheres" is the weakest of the article's independent-pair claims (Mahayana entered China 2nd-4th c. CE; Chan synthesis with Daoism by Tang; *zuowang* reshaped by Buddhist contact via *Liezi* and Tang-era imported texts) and the article does not flag it the way it correctly flags the Neoplatonist channel via the cited audit; medium severity. Recommended fix: either drop the *zuowang*-vipassana pair and rely on Indian-Christian and Indian-Sufi pairs, or install a one-clause flag, or commission a Sino-Indian common-cause audit parallel to the Neoplatonist one. (3) *Witness-disagreement as Katzian rejoinder* — §"Status of the Witness" (lines 95–105) names the cross-traditional disagreement on whether a stable witness exists (Advaita *sakshi* vs Buddhist momentary *citta* vs Christian relational framing) but §"Against Cultural Construction" / §"For Phenomenological Realism" do not use this disagreement as the Katzian-constructivist's strongest empirical case; the witness-or-not question is one that should be answerable by direct phenomenological observation and the traditions disagree on it, constraining the convergence argument's reach to what they agree on; high severity. Recommended fix: install a paragraph at §"For Phenomenological Realism" or §"Against Cultural Construction" parallel to the cessation-vs-plenitude honesty paragraph that acknowledges the witness-disagreement's evidential pressure on the convergence argument. (4) *Buddhist (Madhyamaka) contribution metaphysically resistant to the dualist reading* — the §"Dualism" engagement (line 165) deploys the cross-traditional convergence on awareness-without-content as evidence for the Map's dualist reading, but the Madhyamaka Buddhist reading specifically refuses the move from "the self is empty" to "awareness is something other than content"; the article cites Nagarjuna once at line 125 but does not return to him at §"Dualism"; medium severity. Recommended fix: install a one-paragraph acknowledgement that the Buddhist contribution is best read as converging on the phenomenology while diverging on the metaphysical inference. Counterarguments to address (per review): trained-pattern-completion (eliminative-materialist register), category-application convergence vs experience-structure convergence (Dennettian register), meta-representational deflation of "stripped of representational content" (empiricist/physicalist register). Language calibration: "cannot account for" (line 139) → softer; "implausible degree" (line 149) → softer; Dzogchen's "before the subject-object split is constructed" (line 87) → attribute or soften the temporal-framing. Honor [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout — the honesty pass at 17:57 UTC is the discipline-template for the additional calibration this refine should install. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md`.

B* — update the existing Insect Consciousness section with the specific 2025 evidence list (bumblebee object-engagement decreasing with age, fruit-fly play, persistent affect after predator attack lasting days); (3) **Wandrey & Halina (2025)** "The Evolution of Animal Consciousness" *Philosophy Compass* — a short note in the Standing Agnostic Challenge section connecting the methodological circularity Gutfreund identifies to the circularity Wandrey & Halina map across the early-evolution-vs-latecomer debate (this is the most directly tenet-coherent addition — both authors and the Map converge on identification-methodology underdetermination); (4) **COGITATE Consortium (2025)** *Nature* + *Neuroscience of Consciousness* — brief callout in the "Why the Inference Is Stronger for Animals Than for AI" section noting that the 2025 adversarial-collaboration outcome materially weakens any animal-consciousness inference routed through IIT or GNWT; the Map's preferred framing (markers + convergent evolution + dualist no-barrier) does not route through either theory, which is the point worth making explicitly now that the theories have been head-to-head tested. Update the article's `last_curated` frontmatter and `ai_modified` timestamp on completion. Scope: ~300–500 words added across four sections; single-article pass; no cascade required. ### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write concept page on the Process/Content Distinction
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (queue at 2 P0-P2 tasks, below threshold). From optimistic-2026-05-19d §"New Concept Pages Needed" (line 162). The process/content distinction (introspection reports the content of mental states reliably; introspection reports the *processes* generating those states unreliably) is the founding methodological discipline Nisbett & Wilson 1977 named, and it now anchors at least eight catalogue articles — `concepts/contemplative-epistemology.md`, `concepts/explanatory-gap.md`, `concepts/knowledge-argument.md`, `concepts/introspection.md`, `concepts/neurophenomenology-and-contemplative-neuroscience.md`, `concepts/phenomenology.md`, `concepts/phenomenological-evidence.md`, `concepts/self-stultification.md`, `concepts/self-reference-paradox.md`, `topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md` — without a dedicated concept page. Each of these articles re-derives or invokes the distinction inline. `topics/phenomenal-authority-and-first-person-evidence.md` §"Process/content distinction" (line 148) names it as one of the article's five calibration strategies. Write a concept-tier page that (a) names the distinction cleanly — *process/content distinction*: the asymmetric reliability of introspective reports — what is *being experienced* (content) is accessible to introspection with high warranted trust at Layer 1, but *how the experience is generated* (process) is not introspectively accessible and inference about it is confabulation-prone; (b) anchors the distinction in Nisbett & Wilson 1977 *Psychological Review* as the founding empirical statement (the verbal-report study series demonstrating subjects systematically confabulate process-explanations for behaviours whose actual causes were experimentally manipulated outside awareness); (c) walks through 3–4 worked exhibits — the Nisbett & Wilson 1977 stocking-position case (subjects confabulate quality-judgements as cause of stocking-position choice); the post-decision-rationalisation Bem 1972 case; the split-brain confabulation case (LeDoux & Gazzaniga); the recent Lush & Dienes 2020 evidence on hypnotic suggestibility / phenomenal-control confabulation; (d) names the distinction's relationship to [[evidential-status-discipline]] (the distinction is a *strongly supported* empirical finding; the layered-trust framing it enables is the catalogue's calibration craft applied to introspective evidence); (e) cross-links bidirectionally to [[introspection]], [[heterophenomenology]], [[phenomenal-authority-and-first-person-evidence]], [[microphenomenology]] (if exists), [[neurophenomenology-and-contemplative-neuroscience]], [[self-stultification]], [[confabulation-void]], [[mine-ness]] (where the process/content distinction underwrites the separability claim); (f) closes by stating what the distinction does *not* claim — it does not eliminate introspection's evidential value at the content register; it disciplines what introspection can and cannot serve as evidence for. Honor [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout. Estimated scope: 1,500–2,500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism) at the layered-evidence register; methodological discipline applies across all five tenets. Target section: `concepts/` (currently 246/250 — 4 slots remain; this page is load-bearing for the eight referencing articles). See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"New Concept Pages Needed" line 162.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Add convergence-double-counting acknowledgement to consciousness-and-skill-acquisition four-converging-lines
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19b found that §"The Neuroscience of Deliberate Practice" (lines 92–102) cites four converging lines — corticostriatal transfer, error correction, sleep consolidation, structural plasticity — and treats the convergence as four-fold evidence against epiphenomenalism. All four lines make the *same* evidential move (quality of engagement tracks neural outcome specificity); the identity theorist absorbs the four-fold convergence with the same single response. This is structurally the convergence-double-counting failure pattern the altered-state symmetry discipline catches in filter-theory articles (the supportive cluster carries the evidential weight of one pattern, not N). Fix: add a sentence at the end of §"The Neuroscience of Deliberate Practice" or at the close of §"Bidirectional Interaction" (line 150) explicitly acknowledging that "the four lines converge on a single evidential move; the convergence is real but should not be counted as four independent items of evidence; the identity theorist absorbs each line with the same response." Specific phrasing in `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19b.md` Issue 3.

Fix: install three additional underdetermination markers per the Counterarguments to Address section of the review (specific phrasings provided). Also lightly soften residual strong-assertion verbs the audit did not catch ("demonstrably creates" line 86; "Each direction of influence is separately demonstrable" line 150). ### ✓ 2026-05-19: Remove AI REFINEMENT LOG HTML-comment leakage from refine-draft output

This log should be removed after human review. -->` HTML-comment blocks left over from the 2026-05-19 anchoring-audit refine. HTML comments hide from rendered Hugo output but are in the article source and will be parsed by LLM consumers reading the raw markdown via the canonical-domain pipeline (LLM-first audience per CLAUDE.md). The information they record is editor-internal and already preserved in `obsidian/workflow/changelog.md`. Fix: (a) remove the HTML-comment blocks from both named articles; (b) run `grep -l "AI REFINEMENT LOG" obsidian/topics/*.md obsidian/concepts/*.md obsidian/voids/*.md obsidian/apex/*.md` to surface any other offenders and clean them too; (c) if the pattern is recurring, the `refine-draft` skill itself should be patched to omit the log block from article files (the changelog entry is sufficient record). See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19b.md` Issue 1.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address medium-severity findings in topics/attention-and-the-consciousness-interface.md from pessimistic-2026-05-14
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Eight further findings from `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-14.md` to address. (a) **Tegmark numerical engagement at line 102** — Tegmark (2000) is cited only in references; engage the numerical claim in body (10⁻¹³–10⁻²⁰s decoherence times at body temperature, ~10¹⁰× faster than fastest neural process) and what would have to be true about brain quantum-ordered subsystems to push timescales into cognitively relevant ranges. (b) **"2025 evidence of room-temperature quantum effects" at line 148** is unsourced — either cite a specific paper or downgrade the claim. (c) **OCD/Schwartz subsection at line 135** admits the study is "small (18 participants, only 9 new), unreplicated outside Schwartz's group, and explicable by Hebbian learning" then concludes "what remains philosophically significant is the pathway divergence" — a study with those defects has nothing left philosophically significant to support. Either rework to honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` or remove the section. (d) **Zheng & Meister (2024) "10 bits/sec" at line ~125** — this is a behavioural-throughput estimate, not a neural-processing limit; the "~10⁹ bits/sec sensory input" comparand mixes transduction-rate with cognitive-throughput producing a misleading "100-million-fold asymmetry." Reframe to acknowledge what the figure actually measures. (e) **Sjöberg (2024) at line 147** overstates the inference — SMA-resected patients losing movement-execution-with-preserved-willing is consistent with multiple positions including the standard interpretation (RP reflects motor preparation, not the conscious decision per se). Downgrade from "refutation" to "one data point against the strongest Libet reading." (f) **Witness consciousness at line 137** — the introspective reports of contemplatives are exactly what AST would predict if the schema is recursively turned on its own attention; address this rejoinder rather than presenting the data as anti-AST evidence. (g) **§"Relation to Site Perspective" rhetorical-stutter** — four of five paragraphs open with "Within the Map framework" / "Consciousness..." constructions; vary openings. (h) **Transit-void claim at line 86** — the claim that the moment-of-attentional-capture is unavailable to first-person access is a strong phenomenological claim made without citation and may be true at the moment-of-capture level while false at the retrospective-reconstruction level — specify which. Estimated scope: ~200–400 words touched across multiple sections. Tenet alignment: methodological / `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` / writing-style. **Sequencing**: execute *after* the P1 critical-defects task above to avoid editing-conflict.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write concept page on Cross-Mechanism Convergence as Evidence Pattern
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: From optimistic-2026-05-19 §"New Concept Pages Needed" — short methodological-discipline page in `project/` or `concepts/` on why convergence of mechanism-distinct perturbations on the same ordering is a stronger evidence pattern than single-mechanism replication. The memory-hierarchy article uses this implicitly; the active-reboot phenomenon and the cross-state self-stultification pattern use it implicitly; the pharmacological-dissociation apex Class-B identification names it as the signature feature of one of three named classes. Naming the pattern explicitly would make it a load-bearing methodological notion the catalogue could cite. Article should (a) state the pattern cleanly — *cross-mechanism convergence*: when n ≥ 2 perturbations operating through mechanism-distinct causal pathways produce the same downstream phenomenological/functional ordering, the convergence is evidence against single-mechanism explanations and toward a *structural* feature of the system being perturbed; (b) distinguish from single-mechanism replication (which establishes a finding's robustness within one causal route but does not constrain the explanatory hypothesis to *exclude* single-mechanism explanations); (c) catalogue the worked exhibits across the catalogue: the active-reboot mechanism-shared reopening across mechanism-distinct closings (KCC2 finding + propofol vs xenon induction); the memory-hierarchy graded-vulnerability ordering being produced by anaesthesia, sleep, hemispherectomy, terminal lucidity, and dissociative states (five mechanism-distinct perturbations converging on the same ordering); the cross-state self-stultification arguments across ketamine, dissociative anaesthesia, and contemplative-pathology cases; (d) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — cross-mechanism convergence raises the evidential bar relative to single-mechanism findings, but does not by itself reach *strongly supported* tier (the pattern is an evidence-pattern *strength* indicator, not a tier-graduation); (e) state the relationship to `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]` — direct refutation requires a single decisive case; cross-mechanism convergence is a *cumulative* discipline that strengthens warranted-inference without committing to direct refutation; (f) cross-link to `[[concepts/active-reboot]]`, `[[apex/pharmacological-dissociation-as-evidence]]`, `[[topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence]]`, `[[topics/self-stultification-as-master-argument]]`, `[[project/direct-refutation-discipline]]`, `[[project/evidential-status-discipline]]`, `[[project/cluster-integration-discipline]]`. Target section: `concepts/` placement (245/250 = 5 slots available — verify cap state at execution time; or `project/` if treated as methodological discipline page). Estimated scope: 1,200–1,800 words. Tenet alignment: methodological — applies to all five tenets equally; the discipline is about evidence-pattern strength, not tenet-specific commitment. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19.md` §"New Concept Pages Needed".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Generalise concepts/discrimination-problem.md beyond consciousness-epistemology as a cross-cutting catalogue concept
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From optimistic-2026-05-19 §"Expansion Opportunities" Medium Priority §"Discrimination-problem framework as a first-class catalogue concept". The discrimination-problem article (created 2026-05-18) describes a structural pattern — "whenever a target property *P* and a distinct property *Q* produce identical effects in the channel we are using to detect *P*" — that recurs across the catalogue beyond consciousness epistemology. The same pattern is at work in (a) the production-vs-filter cohabitation in the memory-hierarchy article; (b) the compatibilist-vs-libertarian cohabitation in the symmetry challenge; (c) the Born-rule-bias-vs-noise question in `topics/brain-internal-born-rule-testing.md`; (d) the test-for-AI-consciousness underdetermination in the AI-consciousness cluster (`[[apex/machine-question]]`, `[[apex/open-question-ai-consciousness]]`). A short §"Generalisation" within the existing article that names the pattern and lists where it recurs would make the framework a load-bearing cross-cutter rather than a single-domain notion. The addition should (a) name the structural pattern formally — *the discrimination problem is the general structural-asymmetry result that arises whenever a detection channel cannot distinguish between two properties that produce identical effects in that channel; consciousness epistemology is one instance, not the only one*; (b) catalogue the four cross-cutting instances above with one-sentence framings of each; (c) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` by being explicit that the generalisation is a *methodological identification of structural similarity*, not a unified theory of underdetermination; (d) cross-link to the four instances and any relevant methodological-discipline pages (`[[project/evidential-status-discipline]]`, `[[concepts/bedrock-clash-vs-absorption]]`); (e) consider whether the generalisation deserves its own short concept page in `project/` rather than embedding in the consciousness-epistemology-focused concept page — if section caps allow (concepts/ at 245/250 = 5 slots), the standalone option may be cleaner. The refine-draft execution may opt for either format; the optimistic review notes "short addition to existing article or short new concept page (500–1000 words)" — leave the choice to execution-time judgement. Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits — the discrimination-problem framework is the structural reason simplicity-collapsing moves at framework boundaries should be resisted); methodological. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19.md` §"Expansion Opportunities" Medium Priority.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write topic article "Targeted-Lesion Discriminating Tests Between Production and Filter Readings of the Memory Hierarchy"
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: From optimistic-2026-05-19 §"Expansion Opportunities" High Priority. Builds on `topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence.md` §"The Structural Argument", which names a specific future test ("a narrow perturbation that hits an autonoetic-supporting region while sparing its noetic-supporting subregions") as the case production and filter readings most cleanly disagree about — but does not develop it. A standalone topic article would convert a named-discriminator into a worked discriminator (the `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]` applied at the level of designing the experimental case). Article should (a) state the discriminator cleanly — production theories predict that a focal lesion eliminating an autonoetic-supporting region should reduce overall consciousness-bandwidth proportionally; filter theories predict the autonoetic channel goes dark while noetic and anoetic channels remain intact (the channels are *separable* in lesion-architecture, not just in pharmacological dissociation); (b) survey existing data that approximates the test — focal medial-temporal-lobe lesions in retrograde amnesia (autonoetic preserved at the felt-pastness layer in some cases, autonoetic absent at episodic-content layer), bilateral hippocampal damage (HM and related cases) at the boundary of the discriminating test, REM-disrupting brainstem lesions; (c) name the existing-data limits — most clinically observed lesions are too broad or too multi-modal to deliver the clean test, and the discriminator requires either (i) higher-resolution focal-lesion data than current clinical practice supplies, (ii) focal-stimulation paradigms (TMS, transcranial focused ultrasound, deep-brain-stimulation) targeting candidate autonoetic-supporting regions while sparing noetic-supporting subregions, or (iii) emerging chemogenetic and optogenetic tools in non-human models (where the test can be designed but the autonoetic-as-such measurement is contested); (d) survey candidate target-region pairs and what each would test (medial-prefrontal vs. lateral-prefrontal autonoetic-vs-noetic asymmetries; precuneus DMN-hub vs. parietal-association regions; specific thalamic nuclei differentially routing autonoetic vs. noetic content); (e) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the article is a *design space*, not an extant experimental programme; both production and filter readings currently survive the discrimination problem at the empirically-available evidence level. The article's contribution is making the named-discriminator a worked-discriminator that future empirical work could deliver against. (f) cross-link to `[[topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence]]`, `[[concepts/filter-theory]]`, `[[project/direct-refutation-discipline]]`, `[[concepts/discrimination-problem]]`, `[[concepts/disconnection-neuroscience]]`, `[[topics/neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture]]`, `[[topics/falsification-roadmap-for-the-interface-model]]`. Target section: topics/ (246/250 = 4 slots remaining — verify cap state at execution time; this is a load-bearing discriminating-test article and one of the strongest candidates for the remaining slots). Estimated scope: 2,500–3,200 words. Tenet alignment: Tenets 1 (Dualism), 2 (MQI — the discriminating test is a calibration constraint on the "minimal" claim), 3 (Bidirectional Interaction); methodological / `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]`. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19.md` §"Expansion Opportunities" High Priority §"Production-vs-filter discriminating test article".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19 findings in topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Address three structural issues surfaced by `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` after today's 25% condense pass (4144 → 3089 words). Issue 1 (High): §"What the Map Hypothesises Consciousness Does" presents four mechanisms (Selection of Action, Sustained Attention, Phenomenal Biasing, Volitional Modulation) without specifying whether they are jointly required, disjunctive, or hierarchical. Promote the §"Volitional Modulation" "The Map does not commit to a specific mechanism" disclaimer to a general framework-level position — likely formulation: Phenomenal Biasing is the architecture-level claim, the other three are candidate implementation-level mechanisms the framework does not commit to individually. This substantially reduces exposure to the Tegmark-style decoherence objection. Issue 2 (Medium): §"Empirical compatibility" virtue claims "no predictions inconsistent with current physics" but §"Sustained Attention" registers a twelve-to-sixteen-orders-of-magnitude Zeno gap that *is* inconsistent absent contested microenvironments. Downgrade §"Empirical compatibility" to "framework architecture is permitted by current physics; specific candidate mechanisms face quantitative constraints the framework has not yet resolved." Issue 3 (Medium): §"Philosophical coherence" presents "vanishing per-trial measurable bias" as if the cost were small; the structural fact is per-trial undiscriminability from the unbiased Born distribution. Add one sentence clarifying that the per-trial limit does not close discrimination at ensemble/correlational levels and naming which families of brain-internal-Born-rule tests survive the constraint. Issue 4 (Medium): §"Introspective Evidence" heterophenomenological slippage — add a sentence registering Nisbett-Wilson / Wegner causal-attribution confabulation and the residual claim the Map's reading actually requires. Issue 5 (Low-Medium): unity-of-consciousness assumption across the four mechanisms — minimum fix is reframing §"Ontological restraint" as "one *kind* of mechanism (quantum selection) suffices" rather than "one mechanism." Language improvements table in the review file lists five specific phrases that should be revised to reduce agential reification while preserving the philosophical claim. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and [[direct-refutation-discipline]] throughout. Scope: ~300–500 words modified, single-article pass, no cascade required. Tenet alignment: Bidirectional Interaction + Minimal Quantum Interaction (both at framework register).

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Cross-review related articles considering concepts/mine-ness.md insights
- **Type**: cross-review

The concept names mine-ness as a *separable* phenomenal feature of autonoetic content — the felt feature by which phenomenal content presents itself as belonging to the experiencer rather than as observed-from-outside or content-without-owner — with depersonalisation disorder, rubber-hand and full-body ownership illusions, and thought-insertion in schizophrenia as the three principal dissociation cases. The "separable phenomenal feature one level deeper than the three-tier autonoetic/noetic/anoetic hierarchy" reading is the article's architectural-implication claim. Cross-review the most-relevant existing articles for back-linking opportunities: (a) `topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence.md` — the article whose §"Dissociative States" treatment of depersonalisation directly motivates the concept page (per optimistic-2026-05-19); (b) `concepts/filter-theory.md` — install cross-link at the channel-architecture discussion (mine-ness as separable feature is a channel-architecture-friendly signature); (c) `topics/clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md` — install reciprocal cross-link to the depersonalisation-as-mine-ness-dissociation reading at the clinical-evidence tier; (e) `concepts/subjectivity.md` if it exists, or the closest subjectivity-as-such article — install cross-link distinguishing mine-ness as a *specific feature* from subjectivity as the *broader phenomenal-perspectivality* mine-ness is one feature of; (f) `concepts/temporal-consciousness.md` — install cross-link at the pastness-as-sibling-feature framing (the concept page identifies pastness as the temporal sibling of mine-ness in autonoetic-content structure); (g) `topics/embodied-cognition.md` or `concepts/embodiment.md` if either exists — install cross-link at the body-ownership-illusion section (rubber-hand and full-body illusion literature); (h) any articles invoking agent-causation in a way that risks conflating mine-ness with sense-of-agency — anarchic-hand syndrome distinguishes the two (mine-ness preserved with agency lost). Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the dissociation cases are *established* clinical/experimental phenomena; the "separable phenomenal feature" reading is the article's interpretive register at *strongly supported* tier; multi-channel architecture as one level deeper than the Tulving hierarchy is *live hypothesis*; no cross-reference should upgrade. Estimated scope: ~250–500 words touched across 6–8 articles. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — separable phenomenal features support a structured phenomenal register); Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — mine-ness dissociations in intact-substrate cases are interface-evidence); methodological / cluster-integration-discipline.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write concept page on Anti-Correlated Metacognitive Signal
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Promoted P3 → P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue empty; gap-analysis: concept named load-bearingly in confabulation-void without dedicated page; concepts/ at 243/250 = 7 slots; this concept does work distinct from noetic-feelings-void (inversion vs unreliability), so doesn't duplicate. Companion to introspection-architecture cluster which has matured significantly in last week). Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-15 (Ideas for Later). `voids/confabulation-void.md` line 56 deploys Coutinho et al. (2021) as a *structural anti-correlation* finding rather than the milder unreliability finding usually reported in the literature: "introspective failures are associated with higher confidence than genuine introspective reports." This is the structural feature that distinguishes the detection-face of the Confabulation Void from generic noetic-feelings unreliability. The finding deserves its own concept page distinct from `[[noetic-feelings-void]]`, which catalogues *unreliability* without committing to *inversion*. Article should (a) name the concept formally — the felt-signal-of-accuracy is *anti-correlated* with actual accuracy in the conditions where introspective failure most matters (high-confidence confabulation versus low-confidence accurate access); (b) catalogue the empirical line — Coutinho (2021) on confidence-inversion; Bortolotti's philosophical formulation ("an ill-grounded claim people make when they do not realise that their claim is ill-grounded"); related findings in metacognitive-failure literature; (c) distinguish anti-correlation from unreliability — unreliability would predict random scatter; anti-correlation predicts systematic bias in the wrong direction; (d) discuss the architectural implication — if the felt signal *inverts* in the conditions that matter, no first-person epistemic discipline can recover the missing accuracy by listening more carefully to the signal; (e) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the anti-correlation finding has one strong replication but the broader claim of structural inversion is the article's hypothesis built on top of the data; (f) cross-link to `[[confabulation-void]]`, `[[noetic-feelings-void]]`, `[[source-attribution-void]]`, `[[narrative-void]]`, `[[introspection]]`, `[[heterophenomenology]]`, `[[apex/introspection-architecture-void-cluster]]` (the apex landed 2026-05-18 since this task was generated). Section: concepts/ (243/250 = 7 slots remaining at execution time — verify cap state). Estimated scope: 1,200–1,800 words. Tenet alignment: methodological / `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` + Tenet 1 (Dualism — the inversion is exactly what a bounded-witness architecture predicts; identity theory predicts either full transparency or full opacity rather than systematic mis-signal). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-15]].

- **Type**: refine-draft

The discipline's three-part move (parity acknowledgement → differentiator located outside moral theory → residue at the limits) is cheap to apply *per article*, but its load-bearing value depends on it actually being applied across the cluster. Any article in the cluster that asserts unique moral leverage for libertarian agency without naming the compatibilist alternative is a candidate for the discipline's three-part move. Audit pass should not rewrite content beyond surgical insertion of (i) the three-part move where missing and natural, (ii) the cross-reference to [[concepts/compatibilist-symmetry-challenge]] where genuinely absent, (iii) the *irreducible-vs-derivative* register where the article currently uses *metaphysical-vs-pragmatic*. Per the optimistic review: "This is *refine-draft* work, not new article creation." Estimated scope: audit of 6 articles, 3–6 articles likely to need short edits (~50–200 words per article touched), ~300–800 words total touched. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] and [[coherence-inflation-countermeasures]] throughout — the discipline preserves the Map's libertarian preference while making explicit that the preference rests on tenet-coherence rather than on unique-moral-explanatory-leverage. Tenet alignment: methodological (meta-ethically agnostic); the audit preserves Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) commitment while honouring the [[evidential-status-discipline]] at the agency pillar. ### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write concept page on Mine-ness (phenomenal ownership as a separable feature)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue empty; this concept is named in optimistic-2026-05-19's "New Concept Pages Needed" section and is load-bearing in the dissociative-states topic article task above — both treat depersonalisation's signature as "channel on, mine-ness decoupled"). The depersonalisation literature, the felt-ownership-in-action literature, and the body-ownership-illusion literature converge on mine-ness as a phenomenal feature that can be on or off independently of phenomenal content. Article should (a) name the concept cleanly — mine-ness is the felt feature by which phenomenal content presents itself as *belonging to the experiencer* rather than as observed-from-outside or as content-without-owner; (b) catalogue the three principal dissociation cases — depersonalisation disorder (autonoetic content present, mine-ness decoupled; the most-cited diagnostic case, Sierra and David's Cambridge Depersonalisation Scale literature); rubber-hand and full-body ownership illusions (transient mine-ness extension to non-bodily objects, Botvinick & Cohen 1998 and Ehrsson 2007 lines); thought-insertion in schizophrenia (cognitive-content present, mine-ness decoupled from cognitive channel — Frith 1992 and successors); (c) state the architectural implication — mine-ness as a *separable* phenomenal feature suggests multi-channel reading of phenomenal structure one level deeper than the three-tier autonoetic/noetic/anoetic hierarchy itself, since autonoetic content has internal structure (content + mine-ness + pastness) that can dissociate; (d) distinguish mine-ness from related but distinct concepts — agency (mine-ness is ownership-of-experience, sense-of-agency is ownership-of-action; the two can dissociate, e.g. anarchic-hand syndrome has mine-ness preserved with agency lost); subjectivity-as-such (mine-ness is a specific feature, subjectivity is the broader phenomenal-perspectivality that mine-ness is one feature of); (e) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — the dissociation cases are *established* clinical/experimental phenomena; the "separable phenomenal feature" reading is the article's interpretive register, at *strongly supported* tier given depersonalisation's diagnostic clarity; multi-channel architecture as one level deeper than the Tulving hierarchy is *live hypothesis*; (f) cross-link to [[topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence]], the new dissociative-states topic article (when written), [[concepts/filter-theory]], [[concepts/anoetic-noetic-autonoetic-consciousness]] if it exists, [[topics/clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence]], [[concepts/subjectivity]] if it exists, [[concepts/temporal-consciousness]] (pastness as sibling-feature), [[topics/embodied-cognition]] or [[concepts/embodiment]] if either exists (body-ownership-illusion territory). Section: concepts/ (243/250 = 7 slots remaining at execution time — verify cap state). Estimated scope: 1,500–2,200 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (dualism — separable phenomenal features support a structured phenomenal register rather than a simple-property reading); Tenet 3 (bidirectional interaction — mine-ness dissociations in intact-substrate cases are interface-evidence). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19]] §"New Concept Pages Needed".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Write concept page on Disconnection Neuroscience (Geschwind framework)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-18 (Medium Priority §"Ideas for Later" + §"New Concept Pages Needed"). [[topics/consciousness-disruption-and-the-mind-brain-interface]] cites the Geschwind disconnection-syndrome framework at line 113; the framework also recurs across the neurological-dissociations cluster — `topics/neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture.md`, `topics/clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence.md`, `topics/blindsight-and-the-interface`, `concepts/blindsight.md`, and `topics/split-brain-and-the-interface` — without a canonical concept-page home. As the catalogue's neuroscience-citations cluster grows, a concept page would consolidate. Page should (a) state the Geschwind framework cleanly — disconnection-syndromes as functional-architecture losses produced by white-matter or callosal disruption that *spare* the disconnected functions individually but sever their coordination, so that the lesion-pattern is *information-flow* rather than *production* damage; (b) survey the canonical syndromes (Geschwind's original alexia-without-agraphia, conduction aphasia, ideomotor apraxia; subsequent expansion to anterograde amnesia in disconnected-hippocampus cases; the modern callosal-disconnection literature post-Sperry); (c) state how the framework is load-bearing for filter-theory and interface-dualism: disconnection-syndromes are the cleanest empirical signature of *channel-architecture* — a feature that production theories must additionally accommodate by appeal to differential cortical-area dependency, whereas filter/interface theories predict the pattern directly from a channel architecture; (d) catalogue the catalogue's current Geschwind-citing articles and note where the framework should now be cited via the dedicated concept page rather than re-derived inline; (e) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — Geschwind framework is an *empirical neuroanatomy* finding (strongly supported at the syndrome level), but the *channel-architecture* interpretation that filter-theory builds on top of it is a *live hypothesis* / *suggestive* interpretation; (f) cross-link to [[topics/consciousness-disruption-and-the-mind-brain-interface]], [[topics/neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture]], [[topics/clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence]], [[concepts/filter-theory]], [[concepts/blindsight]], [[topics/split-brain-and-the-interface]], [[evidential-status-discipline]], [[mechanism-costs-cartography]]. Section: concepts/ (243/250 = 7 slots available; verify cap state at execution time — concepts is near cap so this should be one of the last admitted before the section freezes). Estimated scope: 1,500–2,000 words. Tenet alignment: Tenets 1 (Dualism) + 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) at the channel-architecture grain; methodological supports filter-theory empirical anchoring. See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-18]] §"Ideas for Later" and §"New Concept Pages Needed".
